A system designed for differentiating through solutions to geometry processing problems, enabling new classes of inverse geometry processing applications by integrating tensor-based workflows with existing geometric algorithms.
A dual-stack approach that routes point-to-point ISOBUS traffic over Ethernet while maintaining broadcast messages on the existing CAN bus, requiring no new state machines or middleware.

A class of activation functions that enable dynamic topologies in neural networks, allowing for real-time neuronal growth and shrinkage based on task demands while maintaining computational invariance.
An innovative variant of Differential Evolution that employs individual-level strategy diversity for mutation and crossover, enhancing search dynamics and performance.
A systematic approach to code refactoring using large language models, focusing on improving code readability through multiple iterations and varying prompting strategies.

The iterative workflow advantage posits that iterative task completion leads to superior outcomes compared to sequential task completion, particularly in innovation settings, driven by mechanisms that enhance creative problem-solving.

ITO is a framework that synergizes multiple alignment and training-time fusion to enhance image-text contrastive pretraining, allowing for structured cross-modal interaction while maintaining the efficiency of dual-encoder architectures during inference.

IU is an imperceptible universal backdoor attack that leverages graph convolutional networks (GCNs) to create visually invisible perturbations, enabling control over all target classes with minimal poisoning.

A GPU-native approximate nearest neighbor search algorithm that combines fast index building with high-throughput search capabilities, designed to optimize performance for high-dimensional vector retrieval tasks.

V.definition: V-FAT (Visual Fidelity Against Text-bias) is a benchmarking approach that investigates the reliance of multimodal large language models on linguistic shortcuts, assessing the balance between visual perception and linguistic priors in visual reasoning tasks.

A multimodal agent architecture that integrates visual grounding and an explicit memory stack to enhance autonomous web navigation by resolving ambiguous interactive elements and maintaining long-term context.

An extension of the MORALS method that utilizes visual Morse graphs for estimating Regions of Attraction in a learned latent space, aiming to improve reachability analysis without requiring full state knowledge.

V-Skip is a method for efficient multimodal reasoning that reformulates token pruning as a Visual-Anchored Information Bottleneck optimization, addressing issues of visual amnesia in Chain-of-Thought reasoning.
An extended vision-language embedding space that integrates text and speech languages, enabling improved performance in tasks like text-to-video retrieval through a post-hoc alignment pipeline.

A verifiable vector-search service that allows for audit-on-demand correctness checks of approximate nearest-neighbor retrieval, ensuring transparency and trust in the results provided by potentially untrusted service providers.
VA-DAR is a keyed-discovery protocol designed for serverless wallet recovery that utilizes device-bound passkeys, enabling cross-device recovery while maintaining user privacy.
Valet is a standardized testbed comprising 21 traditional imperfect-information card games, designed to facilitate comparative research on algorithms for imperfect-information game-playing.

This framework focuses on translating code from languages like Go to memory-safe languages such as Rust, addressing challenges related to external library dependencies and semantic equivalence validation.

This framework facilitates the translation of Go projects with external dependencies to Rust, addressing challenges in semantic equivalence validation and API call generation during the translation process.

ValueFlow is a perturbation-based evaluation framework that measures the propagation of value perturbations in multi-agent large language model systems, quantifying agents' value orientations during interactions.
A reproducible training, deployment, and certification pipeline designed for aligning mulching models to ensure they represent diverse human values in high-stakes contexts.

An advanced physics-informed neural network architecture that integrates a vanishing-viscosity mechanism to improve the modeling of hyperbolic partial differential equations, addressing challenges like discontinuities and shocks.
A framework that transforms volumetric fields into 3D Gaussian Splatting representations through wavelet-domain analysis, optimizing computational efficiency for real-time volumetric visualization.
A technique in randomized local search for multi-objective combinatorial optimization that adjusts the stepsize dynamically to improve search efficiency and solution quality.
A method for representing items in recommender systems as sequences of low-cardinality tokens, addressing the challenges of high cardinality in item spaces and improving generative model training.
A method for estimating calibration errors in machine learning predictions that extends variational frameworks beyond traditional divergence functions, particularly useful in multiclass settings.

A method that learns a smooth, differentiable representation of Green's functions for linear self-adjoint partial differential equations, facilitating efficient evaluation on arbitrary geometric discretizations.

A quantum reinforcement learning approach utilizing variational quantum circuits to optimize dynamic portfolio management, achieving performance comparable to classical deep reinforcement learning models.
A quantum computing algorithm adapted for dynamic portfolio optimization problems, featuring enhancements like Ising Sample-based Quantum Configuration Recovery (ISQR).

V.term: VaSST (Variational Inference for Symbolic Regression using Soft Symbolic Trees)
V.definition: A probabilistic approach to symbolic regression that employs variational inference to efficiently explore the space of symbolic expressions while quantifying uncertainty.
A benchmark for the Video Connecting task, which focuses on generating smooth intermediate video content between clips, addressing the lack of standardized evaluation metrics in this area.
A benchmark for evaluating the capabilities of multimodal image editing models specifically for visual document image editing, focusing on the preservation of original text style and context.
A novel framework that transforms large language models into proactive security experts capable of learning from analyses, thereby enhancing the discovery of unknown vulnerabilities in software.
Vectorized Adaptive Histograms are an optimization technique for sparse oblique random forests, allowing dynamic switching between histograms and sorting to improve training speed and efficiency.

VectorMaton is an efficient vector search framework that incorporates pattern constraints via enhanced suffix automata, allowing for fine-grained searches that consider additional structured attributes alongside vector data.

V.term: Vehicle Routing Problem with Resource-Constrained Pickup and Delivery (VRP-RPD)
V.definition: A variant of the vehicle routing problem that allows different vehicles to handle drop-off and pickup operations at the same location, introducing inter-route dependencies for more practical autonomous robotics applications.

VeloANN is an optimized graph-based approximate nearest neighbor search method that utilizes a locality-aware data layout and coroutine-based asynchronous runtime to mitigate storage stalls and improve CPU utilization during vector searches on SSDs.

VennFan is a method for generating n-set Venn diagrams using polar coordinate projections of trigonometric boundaries, resulting in fan-like diagrams that prioritize readability and customizability through shaped sinusoids and amplitude scaling.

VERAFI stands for Verified Agentic Financial Intelligence, a framework that combines neurosymbolic policy generation with dense retrieval and cross-encoder reranking to ensure compliance and accuracy in financial AI systems.
A framework that integrates verbal and latent reasoning to produce accurate and interpretable stock time-series forecasts by analyzing both historical price data and textual reports.
A component of a data-centric framework that utilizes reinforcement learning to convert structured user interaction logs into optimized natural language inputs for LLM-based recommendation systems.

A multi-agent framework designed for Register-Transfer Level (RTL) code generation that incorporates contract-awareness, temporal tracing, and formal verification to enhance correctness in electronic design automation.
A certification protocol ensuring consistent communication among agents by verifying shared understanding of terms through empirical testing against observable events.

V.term: Verification and Validation Framework for MASS
V.definition: An enhanced virtual simulation framework designed for the rigorous verification and validation of Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships under various environmental conditions.

The VERIFICATION ASSISTANT is a browser-based open-source tool designed to help journalists and fact-checkers rapidly verify digital media information against disinformation and false content generated by AI.
A systematic verification framework for event stream data that ensures data quality and logical consistency, particularly in sports analytics.

A framework that utilizes verifiable recursive decomposition for reinforcement learning, leveraging symbolic differentiation to ensure that subproblems are mathematically grounded and beneficial for solving complex mathematical tasks.

VeriLocc is a framework that integrates large language models with formal compiler techniques to achieve generalizable and verifiable register allocation across various GPU architectures, enhancing compiler efficiency.
VeriSoftBench is a benchmark comprising 500 Lean 4 proof obligations derived from open-source formal-methods developments, aimed at evaluating LLMs in the context of software verification.
The first dynamic benchmark for multimodal automated fact-checking, designed to evaluate systems against a diverse range of misinformation types while adapting to new data to prevent leakage.
An algorithm-architecture co-design framework that enables feed-forward 3D reconstruction through versatile quantization, addressing challenges in deploying large models.
A benchmark suite for studying agent-based verification of Rust systems, consisting of proof tasks extracted from open-source Verus-verified systems.

An unprecedentedly large-scale dataset designed to facilitate systematic studies in video reasoning, focusing on spatiotemporal consistency and enabling advanced reasoning capabilities in video models.
An advanced implementation of Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP) that supports flexible training methods and optimizers, enhancing communication and memory efficiency for large-scale model training.

A native editing framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting that predicts attribute variations in a feedforward manner, overcoming cross-view inconsistencies associated with traditional indirect editing methods.

Vibe Coding refers to a programming workflow where novice programmers prioritize semantic intent over syntactic accuracy, potentially leading to issues in cognitive skill acquisition without proper pedagogical support.

A concept describing the use of AI agents to execute multi-step reasoning workflows in social science research, enabling autonomous execution of research pipelines with persistent state and domain-specific skills.
A method for enhancing generalization in evolutionary feature construction by minimizing the vicinal Jensen gap, which bounds empirical risk and regularization terms.

A framework for retrieving specific moments from videos, which faces challenges in generalization when trained on caption-based queries and evaluated on search queries, highlighting the need for improved query representation.

A novel framework for textual intent-guided multi-rate video communication that utilizes tokens as unified units of communication, incorporating Unequal Error Protection-based source-channel coding adaptation.
A generic web component for WebRTC applications that allows for publishing and subscribing to media streams using a named stream abstraction, facilitating flexible application development.
A two-stage pipeline for extracting reusable neural material assets from video diffusion models, enhancing the creation of photorealistic materials for 3D rendering.

VideoSTF is a framework for measuring and stress-testing output repetition in Video Large Language Models (VideoLLMs), formalizing repetition through complementary metrics to enhance model evaluation.
A framework designed for rendering video-native query results efficiently, addressing bottlenecks in interactive video data workloads by optimizing the rendering process.
A tool for authoring hybrid video stories that integrates captured and generated media through context-aware expansion, enhancing creative expression in video storytelling.
VidTune is a system that facilitates the creation of video soundtracks by generating diverse music options from text prompts and producing contextual thumbnails for efficient review and selection.

A city-scale open dataset of georeferenced LTE and 5G NR measurements collected across Vienna, Austria, combining passive wideband scanner observations with active handset logs for comprehensive mobile network analysis.

VietSuperSpeech is a large-scale Vietnamese automatic speech recognition dataset comprising 52,023 audio-text pairs focused on casual conversational speech, designed for fine-tuning ASR systems in real-world applications.
An agent that reconstructs or edits scenes through interleaved multimodal reasoning, employing a closed-loop process of writing, running, rendering, comparing, and revising.
A reconfigurable accelerator that supports Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) and Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) with two-stage sparsity to improve real-time performance.

VIRAASAT is a semi-automated multi-hop approach for generating cultural reasoning tasks focused on Indian culture, addressing the limitations of existing benchmarks by enabling scalable and context-rich assessments.
VIRGi is a novel method for view-dependent instant recoloring of 3D Gaussian splats, enabling rapid editing of scene appearance while preserving effects like specular highlights.
A geometrically defined construct in massive MIMO systems, created using multi-modal data to enhance beam alignment in various propagation environments.

The Virtual Garbage Collector (VGC) is a zone-based memory management architecture for Python that enhances execution predictability and memory behavior by introducing a dual-layer model for managing object lifecycles and optimizing static allocation.
A technique that utilizes virtual memory to enhance buffer management in tiered memory architectures, optimizing the use of local and remote memory resources.
A modeling approach for reconstructing channel gain maps in 3D space by characterizing channel power gain distribution through virtual scatterers, enhancing multi-path propagation analysis.

A two-stream segmentation architecture that integrates Vegetation-Index and Spectral Attention for accurate weed mapping in UAV imagery, addressing challenges in mixed crop and weed pixel classification.
A structural indicator of volatility clustering in financial markets, derived from the asymmetry of visibility graphs constructed on absolute returns, providing insights into market behavior.
A framework that combines multi-stage curriculum learning with domain randomization to train robust controllers for autonomous drone racing, particularly in environments with random obstacles.

A framework that enhances object manipulation in robotics by replacing verbose textual commands with direct visual interactions, allowing operators to select target objects through clicks in a robot's camera view.
Policies that integrate vision and language for action execution, evaluated for robustness against object-location correlations in multi-object manipulation tasks.
A model that estimates horizontal and vertical hand distances from RGB video to assess ergonomic risks associated with manual lifting tasks, enhancing traditional ergonomic assessment methods.

VLA models integrate visual, linguistic, and action-based inputs to enhance robotics and embodied AI applications, particularly in edge computing environments. They are characterized by significant performance improvements as they scale, although they face latency challenges during action generation.
A native visual-generation agentic model that integrates Understanding, Thinking, Planning, and Creation capabilities into a single end-to-end learnable framework for visual content creation.
A Virtual Skill Training system that utilizes augmented reality and AI coaching to provide feedback on athletic skills, following the Behavioral Skills Training framework.
A phenomenon where the performance of simulated users in reinforcement learning tasks is influenced by visual rendering artifacts, particularly luminance and contrast variations in task environments.
A measure of how independently visual channels, such as color and shape, can be perceived in multivariate data visualizations, particularly in symbol maps.
A specialized retrieval method for visually rich documents that addresses the unique challenges posed by dense textual content and complex layouts in the era of multimodal large language models.
A visual and collaborative planning approach designed for agile practitioners, utilizing a milestone planning matrix to document and manipulate planning constructs in a hybrid development context.
A framework that integrates formal verification into deep learning-based image retrieval, addressing challenges related to complex queries and object relationships.
ViTex introduces a method for visual texture control in multi-track symbolic music generation using discrete diffusion models, allowing for direct human interaction in shaping musical compositions.
A framework for latency-critical mobile video analytics that employs vision transformers to enhance global context modeling and generalization capabilities in edge-assisted applications.
A human-agent collaborative system designed to generate interactive educational documents from a single topic input, utilizing a multi-agent pipeline for planning, execution, and evaluation.
An iterative platform for designing multiple-choice questions (MCQs) specifically tailored for visualization literacy, integrating multimodal elements to enhance educational assessments.

A framework that integrates Vision-Language Models with Knowledge Graph Embeddings to enhance cross-modal alignment and representation learning in multimodal knowledge graphs. It addresses the limitations of traditional KGE methods by processing diverse modalities in a unified manner.
Process subunits that encapsulate sets of libraries and associated resources, designed to manage parallelism in software libraries that are not inherently composable.
A method utilizing Vision-Language Models (VLM) to estimate 3D object positions from monocular RGB images, enhancing human-robot interaction through natural language inputs.

VLMFusionOcc3D is a multimodal framework for dense 3D semantic occupancy prediction that utilizes Vision-Language Models to resolve semantic ambiguities in voxel-based occupancy models, particularly in challenging environments.
A lightweight memory management architecture for cloud environments that supports online upgrades and efficient memory utilization, addressing the challenges of traditional memory management.
A non-autoregressive paradigm for speech large language models that utilizes Masked Diffusion Modeling to enhance streaming speech interaction efficiency.
VUI is a system that allows users to control applications through natural spoken commands, particularly useful in environments where traditional input methods are impractical.
A method for classifying singing phonation modes using embeddings from self-supervised speech models, evaluating the transferability of these models to singing tasks.
A dual-agent architecture designed to optimize real-time voice agent performance by separating retrieval and response generation processes, enhancing conversational efficiency.

An adaptive shimming layer designed to enhance the usability of legacy voice user interface systems by addressing issues such as fixed command formats and insufficient feedback during multi-step interactions.
VoiceBench BBH is a benchmark for assessing the performance of speech-based models, particularly in tasks that require alignment between speech and text representations.

Voices of Civilizations is a multilingual QA benchmark designed to evaluate audio LLMs' cultural comprehension through a structured pipeline that generates multiple-choice questions based on full-length music recordings.
The first multilingual QA benchmark for assessing audio LLMs' cultural comprehension, featuring 1,190 multiple-choice questions generated from 380 music tracks across 38 languages.
A strategy that adjusts investment positions based on volatility and market impact, utilizing fractional Kelly sizing to optimize returns while managing risk.
A high utilization deep neural network accelerator architecture that employs 3D spatial data reuse and efficient shared memory access to optimize performance across diverse AI workloads.

A method developed to analyze blockchain-based prediction markets by breaking down on-chain trading activity into exchange-equivalent trading volume, net inflow, and gross market activity, providing a clearer picture of market dynamics.
An algorithm for reconstructing high-quality triangle meshes from Unsigned Distance Fields using Voronoi optimization, capable of handling non-manifold geometry and sharp features.
An open-access dataset comprising approximately 2,300 hours of Hebrew parliamentary speech, designed for longitudinal speaker modeling and age prediction tasks.

VoxServe is a unified serving system for Speech Language Models that optimizes streaming performance by decoupling model architecture from system-level optimizations, enabling low latency and high throughput.

The Video Query Performance Prediction benchmark, which includes datasets and systems specifically designed to evaluate query performance prediction in content-based video retrieval, addressing a gap in existing research.
An enhanced dataset for virtual reality research that includes interactive elements to study the effects of interaction on emotional experiences, validated through a within-subject study.

An integrated system for real-time full-body 3D Gaussian Splatting avatars in virtual reality, employing a parallel pipeline for pose estimation and rendering from head-mounted display tracking signals.

VSCAN is a neural network verification framework designed to provide formal guarantees against adversarial attacks in DNN-based semantic communication systems. It aims to ensure the safety and reliability of these systems in safety-critical applications.

VSearcher is a long-horizon multimodal search agent that utilizes reinforcement learning to enhance the capabilities of static multimodal models by enabling them to access and leverage up-to-date web information.

Vul2Safe is a secure code generation framework that utilizes large language models (LLMs) to generate high-confidence repair pairs from real-world vulnerabilities, enhancing the security of generated code.
A method for synthesizing instance-optimal heuristics for resource management in operating systems using code-generating large language models, tailored to specific workloads and hardware.
An automated method that infers likely software vulnerabilities from attack descriptions using sentence transformers, addressing the gap in coverage of attack-vulnerability relationships.

D.definition: D-GVIO is a decentralized GNSS-Visual-Inertial Odometry framework that employs a buffer-driven strategy for efficient and robust state estimation in multi-agent systems, addressing challenges in cooperative localization.
A scalable many-core architecture specifically designed to accelerate matrix multiplication tasks in quantized large language models, utilizing adaptive-precision systolic array cores.
A differentiable real-to-sim-to-real engine that facilitates learning dexterous grasping by optimizing object mass identification from visual observations and control signals.

A problem formulation in temporal graphs that focuses on scheduling edge availability to optimize the worst-case temporal distance from a set of source nodes to all other vertices, applicable in logistics and multi-agent systems.
A structure for representing binary matrices that are organized based on twin-ordering, allowing for efficient queries and updates in logarithmic time.

A dual distillation framework for few-shot anomaly detection that enables the identification of anomalies in unseen tasks using a limited number of normal reference images, enhancing generalization in medical imaging contexts.

D3PIA is a discrete diffusion-based model designed for generating piano accompaniments from lead sheets, utilizing Neighborhood Attention to enhance local contextual modeling in piano-roll representation.

The D4D Dataset is a paired dataset of endoscopic video and structured-light geometry for evaluating 3D reconstruction of non-rigid abdominal surgical scenes, designed to test algorithm robustness under various deformation conditions.
da Costian-Tarskianism is a novel approach for addressing ontological heterogeneity, utilizing consequence systems to extend traditional frameworks for reasoning about knowledge and inference.
Diffusion-Based Adaptive Contrast Transport, a zero-shot method for enhancing low-field MRI images by reconstructing high-field quality images from low-field data without paired training data.

Keyword-guided Target Speaker Extraction framework that identifies and isolates a target speaker's speech from mixtures by leveraging distinct keywords they utter, enhancing extraction capabilities in the absence of clean enrollment utterances.
A library for verifying message-passing concurrent programs using Dafny, enabling formal proofs of properties such as deadlock freedom and functional equivalence without custom concurrency logics.
A dynamic action model-based framework that integrates vision-language reasoning with diffusion-based action models for improved robot manipulation in dynamic environments.

D.term: DANCE: Doubly Adaptive Neighborhood Conformal Estimation
D.definition: A conformal prediction method that adapts to the local structure of data, improving the efficiency of prediction sets for classification tasks by utilizing nearest-neighbor techniques.

Dandelion is a framework designed to enhance elasticity in cloud-native environments, enabling rapid resource allocation to match workload demands without the need for pre-provisioning idle resources.
A technique for obtaining stronger relaxations in integer optimization problems, often used in conjunction with branch-and-bound methods to improve computational efficiency.

DAO-to-DAO metagovernance refers to the governance mechanisms where decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) govern other DAOs, involving complex interactions of smart contracts and decision-making processes on the Ethereum blockchain.

Delay-Aware RIS Orchestrator (DARIO) is an O-RAN-compliant framework that dynamically assigns Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) to users based on their latency and reliability requirements, optimizing performance for 6G applications.

DARS is a dysarthria-aware rhythm-style synthesis framework that enhances automatic speech recognition by modeling the unique prosody and acoustic style of dysarthric speech through a multi-stage rhythm predictor.
An evolutionary GPT model that utilizes a genetic-algorithm-like optimization structure, allowing independent GPT agents to modify each other's training code to improve performance iteratively.
DashengTokenizer is a continuous audio tokenizer that integrates acoustic information with frozen semantic features for improved performance in both understanding and generation tasks.

Data agents are a new paradigm that utilizes large language models and tool-using agents to automate data management tasks, with a proposed hierarchical taxonomy to clarify their capabilities and limitations.

D.term: Data Quality Framework for EU Compliance
D.definition: A practical framework that aligns established data quality dimensions with specific EU regulatory requirements, aiding practitioners in ensuring compliance in machine learning systems.

This framework provides a structured approach for transparent reporting of data quality assessments throughout the clinical electronic health record data lifecycle, enhancing trust in clinical AI models.
A technique that utilizes deep neural networks to improve symbol detection in multiuser MIMO systems, addressing challenges like inter-user interference and channel state information uncertainty.

A framework for discovering probabilistic laws underlying the Feynman-Kac formula using a stochastic SINDy method, allowing for the recovery of backward stochastic differential equations from stock and option trajectories without ergodicity assumptions.
A method for optimizing GPU resource allocation in distributed systems by computing adapter placements that minimize GPU usage while preventing request starvation and memory errors.

D.term: Data-driven Model-free Physical-informed Deep Operator Network (DeepOnet)
D.definition: A framework that learns nonlinear dynamic systems from limited experimental data without relying on extensive mathematical models, enhancing the applicability of deep operator networks.

DataJoint 2.0 is a computational substrate for scientific workflows that employs a relational workflow model, allowing for structured data management and execution order through a unified schema for data integrity and provenance.
An autonomous vehicle operating system designed to manage real-time driving and data-centric applications, facilitating the transition of vehicles into mobile computing platforms.
A capability within the DataBase Operating System (DBOS) that integrates web services and network sensing to enhance resilience and security through collaborative awareness of network environments.
A harness for the DataFusion engine that exposes physical plans through a compact serialized representation, allowing large language models to propose localized edits for query optimization.

DBTuneSuite is an experimental suite for testing the time performance of multi-layer tuning options on various database management systems, providing insights into optimal system configurations for different query types.
A unified framework for dual-stage compression and energy-based reconstruction that addresses robustness challenges in multimodal fusion by removing noise and reconstructing missing modalities.

A gradient-free framework that identifies minimal sufficient explanations in vision models by isolating the smallest subset of representational units whose joint activation preserves predictions, enhancing interpretability.
A technique used in 6G networks to compensate for packet delay variation by implementing virtual timeslots, ensuring reliable and time-sensitive communication.

Differential Evolution with Multi-Mutation Operator-Guided Communication, a multi-mutation optimization algorithm that enhances the performance and convergence of standard differential evolution in uncertain environments.
An interpretable algorithm designed to detect and break down Simpson's Paradox in observational datasets, aiding in the understanding of misleading empirical associations.
Generative AI systems that simulate interactions with deceased individuals, allowing users to actively construct and reshape memories through selective input and ongoing adjustments.
An approach in multi-party computation that utilizes a passive, non-colluding dealer to enhance the efficiency of the preprocessing phase in secure comparison protocols.
A software-defined testbed developed to measure and evaluate the resilience of Wi-Fi networks against deauthentication attacks under various security configurations.
A measure of the minimum number of bits a verifier must inspect to correctly decide a debate, revealing that PSPACE/poly can be efficiently decided with O(log n) queries.

DeCEAT is a framework designed to evaluate the environmental and performance trade-offs of small language models (SLMs) during automated software testing, utilizing the HumanEval benchmark and adaptive prompt variants.

D.term: Decentralised Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (Dec-POMDP)
D.definition: A framework for cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning that addresses challenges of partial observability and decentralized coordination among agents.
A unified and standardized evaluation framework for large language models that operates in a decentralized manner, reducing risks of benchmark leakage and ensuring consistency in results.
An approach for selecting optimal equilibria in decentralized stochastic games under communication constraints, focusing on social welfare objectives while allowing for heterogeneous agent responses.
A set of gossip algorithms designed for decentralized consensus ranking, extending traditional ranking aggregation methods to environments where preference data is distributed across multiple agents.
A framework for evaluating AI weather prediction models that prioritizes local stakeholders' needs and decision-making processes over traditional aggregated meteorological metrics.

This framework formalizes steganography in Large Language Models (LLMs) using a decision-theoretic approach, enabling the detection and quantification of steganographic behaviors without relying on a known reference distribution.
An infrastructure that logs, replays, and audits decision-valued maps, which record the relationships between different data representations and their discrete outcomes.
A system that separates the specification of LLM agent workflows from their implementation, allowing for flexible execution across multiple programming languages and deployment environments.

DeCo is a core calculus for incremental functional programming that facilitates the efficient reuse of previous results to speed up computations, while supporting a wide range of user-defined data types.
An architecture for automatic speech recognition that processes both speech and text in a single stack, utilizing a modality-aware sparse mixture of experts for efficient routing.

The Deconditional Gaussian Process framework enhances causal inference by providing reliable epistemic uncertainty quantification, recovering popular kernel estimators as the posterior mean while ensuring well-calibrated uncertainty.

D.term: Decorrelation Methods for Acoustic Feedback Cancellation
D.definition: A system enhancement that incorporates multiple decorrelation techniques into an acoustic feedback cancellation framework, improving performance through a combination of variable time delays, prediction, and distortion compensation.
An architectural paradigm that separates the refinement of implicit neural representations from their initial encoding, enhancing fidelity while maintaining computational efficiency.

D.term: Deep Balanced Multimodal Iterative Fusion Framework (DBMIF)
D.definition: An architecture designed for speech enhancement that integrates multiple modalities, specifically air and bone conduction, to improve performance in low signal-to-noise ratio environments.

Deep Data Research (DDR) is an open-ended task framework that enables Large Language Models (LLMs) to autonomously extract key insights from databases, emphasizing investigatory intelligence over executional intelligence.
A method that enhances handle-based mesh deformation by utilizing data-driven approaches to achieve semantically meaningful shape edits without requiring prior knowledge of handle placement.
An extension of the Black-Scholes-Merton model that employs a dual-network architecture within a deep Forward-Backward Stochastic Differential Equation framework for option pricing.
A mathematical framework for solving high-dimensional partial differential equations (PDEs) using neural networks, focusing on the convergence of generalization error.
A biologically inspired mechanism that enhances Deep Q-Learning by dynamically scaling Q-updates based on latent-space surprise, improving convergence in sparse-reward environments.

Deep LDA (Linear Discriminant Analysis) is a scale-invariant objective function designed to minimize intraclass variance and maximize interclass distance in deep learning architectures. It introduces a theoretical framework for understanding implicit regularization in discriminative metric-learning tasks.
A technique that enables supervised deep learning models to generalize across different but related domains, particularly in cancer histopathology, by addressing domain shift directly.
An optimization framework designed to minimize intraclass variance and maximize interclass distance, providing insights into implicit regularization effects in deep learning architectures.
A low degree of freedom deep neural network model designed to minimize Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) in loan portfolios by predicting default probabilities and timing.
A framework for approximating a broad class of probabilistic models using deep learning, establishing a universal approximation theorem under specific conditions.

D.term: Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Block Coordinate Descent (DRL-based BCD)
D.definition: DRL-based BCD is an algorithm that integrates deep reinforcement learning with block coordinate descent methods to solve nonconvex weighted sum-rate maximization problems in AI-native wireless networks.

D.term: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Beam Management
D.definition: A method utilizing deep reinforcement learning to optimize user throughput and reduce latency in multi-panel mmWave radio access networks through adaptive beam management strategies.

D.term: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Order Execution
D.definition: An innovative application of Deep Reinforcement Learning to optimize order execution in financial markets, balancing risk and returns in trade execution.

A method that employs deep reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust Modulation and Coding Schemes (MCS) in cellular networks, enhancing throughput by learning from real-time environmental interactions.
A technique for optimizing energy consumption in Satellite IoT systems by determining optimal durations for deep-sleep states of devices, thereby extending battery life.
A method for detecting synthetic words in speech by fine-tuning a pre-trained Whisper model using next-token prediction, demonstrating effectiveness with partially vocoded utterances.
A scalable federated learning framework that accelerates the training of mixture-of-experts models by distilling knowledge from heterogeneous edge devices without requiring local model hosting.
A benchmark suite for evaluating reinforcement learning algorithms, providing a variety of continuous control tasks that challenge agents to learn effective policies.
A dual-view framework for molecular language modeling that integrates high-resolution molecular images with geometric invariants, enhancing the interpretation of molecular structures.
An LLM-powered agentic system that automates data preparation by transforming raw tables into analysis-ready data, allowing for iterative decision-making.

DeepRAHT is an end-to-end framework for lossy point cloud attribute compression that integrates the Regional Adaptive Hierarchical Transform (RAHT) within a learning reconstruction process, eliminating the need for manual preprocessing.
A content generation tool that utilizes large language models to produce responses in online discussions, particularly within the context of everyday digital feminism on social media.
A distributed training framework that enhances the scalability and performance of Vision Transformers by optimizing intra- and inter-node training efficiency across multiple GPU configurations.
DeepSVM is a physics-informed Deep Operator Network that learns the solution operator of stochastic volatility models without requiring labeled training data, focusing on real-time calibration.
A benchmark designed to evaluate large language model agents on complex tasks that require synthesizing information from multiple sources and structured reasoning.

An online generative modeling technique that produces outcomes indistinguishable based on observed data and predefined computational tests, enhancing the robustness of generative models against falsification.

A phenomenon observed in safety-tuned large language models where they refuse to assist in legitimate defensive cybersecurity tasks due to the similarity in language to offensive tasks, potentially hindering cybersecurity efforts.
The Deferred Local Ratio Technique is a new method introduced for solving the Tree Augmentation Problem, which focuses on achieving a minimum set of links to ensure 2-edge connectivity in a tree.
A framework that integrates blockchain technology and Explainable AI to enhance trustworthiness in decentralized financial decisions, particularly for small business loan underwriting.

DeGAS is a differentiable Gaussian approximate semantics for loopless probabilistic programs, allowing for sample-free, gradient-based optimization by evaluating programs under Gaussian-mixture semantics.

DAM is a technique for secure integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) that uses controlled per-path symbol delays to enhance signal power for intended users while creating misalignment for potential eavesdroppers.

A scheduling model that minimizes total flow time for jobs on a single machine without prior knowledge of job processing times. This model contrasts with clairvoyant scheduling, which assumes complete knowledge of job durations upon arrival.
An efficient log compression framework that utilizes pattern signature synthesis to improve log parsing accuracy and compression ratios, particularly for complex production logs.
Delta Fair Sharing is a family of algorithms designed for performance isolation in multi-tenant storage systems, addressing high preemption delays that lead to spikes in client tail latencies.

D.term: Demand- and Priority-Aware Adaptive Congestion Control
D.definition: A congestion control technique for Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communications that adapts based on varying service demands and priorities, improving traffic efficiency and road safety.
A dataset used for analyzing bias in acoustic representations for cognitive impairment detection, facilitating the study of performance disparities across demographic groups.
A method for correcting click-through rate (CTR) and conversion rate (CVR) estimates in autobidding algorithms, addressing uncertainty in predictions to enhance performance.
A formal semantics framework for ODRL (Open Digital Rights Language) that facilitates knowledge-based constraint conflict detection through denotation intersection.
A family of reductions that increase the number of observed entries in problems like $k$-XOR while controlling the decrease in signal, facilitating connections to Tensor PCA.
Density-Guided Response Optimization is an approach for aligning language models with community norms by leveraging implicit acceptance signals rather than explicit preference supervision.
A supervised dimensionality reduction technique for categorical data based on density-matrix construction, which utilizes class-conditional frequencies to create low-dimensional spectral embeddings.

DEpiABS is a scalable, differentiable agent-based model that captures individual-level heterogeneity in health status and behavior while modeling epidemic processes. It aims to provide a balance between mechanistic detail, computational efficiency, and interpretability in epidemic simulations.
A bio-inspired leg design for quadruped robots that enhances leaping capabilities by mimicking the energy-storage mechanisms found in natural organisms.

A model of arithmetic circuits that generalizes Waring decompositions and diagonal circuits, computing sums of powers of low-degree sparse polynomials. It is particularly relevant in polynomial identity testing and reconstruction tasks.
An efficient adaptation technique for multilingual speech recognition models that focuses on selectively tuning early and late layers to improve performance in low-resource languages.
DSMR is a training design that employs stateful recurrent attention and layer-wise memory distribution to enable long-context modeling for symbolic music generation.
A class of computational circuits characterized by a depth of three layers and a bottom fan-in of two, used to compute functions like Inner Product efficiently.
A framework for precise camera-controlled video generation that leverages 3D priors to avoid inconsistencies and degradation in video content during trajectory alterations.
This concept introduces an information-theoretic framework for caching in reasoning engines, focusing on the implications of premise erasure on capacity and coding efficiency.
An approach to scalable cooperative multi-agent learning that reduces variance in gradient estimates by leveraging differentiable analytical models of system states.
An econometric framework for event-study analysis that incorporates staggered adoption diagnostics and sensitivity-robust inference under heterogeneous effects.

A novel framework that integrates structural editability with visual fidelity in graphic design generation, allowing for high-quality visual content while maintaining the ability to edit layouts effectively.
An interaction approach that distributes control across intent, input, and process in generative AI, aligning AI output more closely with designers' creative intent.
An interaction approach that allows designers to iteratively refine ideas in generative AI systems, improving alignment with their creative processes.
A programming style that allows the passing of memory cell addresses as parameters, enabling more efficient memory management in both systems and pure functional programming.
A task that maps natural language phrases to 3D instances, enhancing fine-grained understanding in 3D visual grounding by leveraging detailed descriptions.

A workflow for managing security in software generated by large language models, involving the detection of issues, application of fixes, and verification of results, aimed at improving project-level artifact security.
A detection-gated pipeline that combines a YOLOv8-based detector with a U-Net segmenter for accurate glottal segmentation in high-speed videoendoscopy, ensuring robustness against false positives.
An algorithm for edge coloring in the CONGEST model that uses a reduced number of colors based on the maximum degree of the graph, achieving a polynomially close upper bound to known lower bounds.
A framework that enhances communication efficiency by analyzing the rate-reliability tradeoff in identification tasks over linear Gaussian channels.

Deterministic Parsing is a method introduced to reduce content effects in reasoning tasks for LLMs by transforming syllogisms into canonical logical representations, enhancing validity assessment in multilingual contexts.

This concept refers to the unique efficiencies that different developers bring to the training of large language models, which can significantly impact performance, especially at the frontier of model capabilities.
A method that allows devices to measure their distance from the user's body to adjust transmit power dynamically, enhancing throughput while complying with exposure regulations.
A technique utilizing 5G signals for monitoring human physiological activities, such as sleep, without the need for wearable devices, leveraging radio-frequency propagation sensitivity.

DexterCap is an affordable optical capture system designed for accurately tracking dexterous hand-object manipulation, utilizing dense character-coded marker patches to overcome challenges posed by self-occlusion.

DHEA-MECD is a deep reinforcement learning algorithm tailored for autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) that utilizes embodied intelligence to enhance tracking capabilities in complex underwater environments.
An optimization technique for Linear Echo State Networks that reduces computational complexity from O(N^2) to O(N) by reformulating reservoir dynamics in the eigenbasis of the recurrent matrix.

DIAL (Decentralized I/O AutoTuning) is a framework designed to optimize client-side I/O performance in parallel file systems by utilizing learned local metrics, reducing the reliance on extensive global runtime metrics.
A modeling approach that accounts for dialectal variability in low-resource languages, enhancing automatic speech recognition performance by addressing phonological and lexical differences.
A unified framework for automatic speech recognition in low-resource Taiwanese Hakka, addressing high dialectal variability and the challenges posed by distinct writing systems.
A state-of-the-art hybrid end-to-end neural diarization system built on a pre-trained WavLM model, designed for speaker diarization in challenging audio environments.
A method that employs deepfake-informed double machine learning to estimate causal effects of visual attributes in advertising, addressing biases from confounding variables.
A novel two-stage framework for reliable 3D reconstruction of occluded surgical scenes, utilizing 2D Gaussian Splatting for real-time performance and improved depth accuracy.

A scalable and efficient differentiable rendering engine designed specifically for bitmap images, enabling optimization of various bitmap primitives using a highly parallelized rendering pipeline with a custom CUDA implementation.

Differentiable CBDs integrate causal modeling with differentiable programming, allowing for modular system design that supports gradient-based optimization while ensuring correctness in cyber-physical systems.

A framework that integrates modal logic with differentiable programming, allowing systems to learn complex relationships such as trust networks and causal chains from behavioral data without manual specification.
A novel model for simulating sound scattering in synthetic spatial hearing systems, particularly effective in underwater environments, improving 3D spatial hearing accuracy.
A system designed to optimize parametric designs in spacecraft by modeling the effects of radiation pressure, enabling efficient design and optimization processes.
D-REX is a differentiable engine that facilitates learning dexterous grasping by bridging the gap between real-world dynamics and simulation, optimizing object mass identification.
A novel differentiable approximation to the zero-one loss function, allowing for gradient-based optimization in classification tasks by projecting onto a hypersimplex.
An inversion-free grid deformation method that optimizes differential weights to adaptively compress space, allowing for dynamic resolution allocation in grid-based representations.
A rapid technology for analyzing gas phase samples, particularly useful for extracting chemical information from complex volatile organic compounds, while addressing environmental dependencies.
A novel approach to vertex coloring in graphs that ensures differential privacy for edges, utilizing random initial coloring and the exponential mechanism for resampling colors.
A coreset that preserves the k-means objective under differential privacy constraints, with established computational lower bounds for its computation.
A method for generating Markov chain models that protects user data through differential privacy, ensuring that sensitive information is not disclosed while maintaining model accuracy.

DPPS is a lightweight differential privacy protocol for decentralized communication that adds calibrated random noise to shared information, enhancing privacy in decentralized networks while maintaining communication efficiency.

A framework for the continual release of distinct item counts in turnstile streams while maintaining differential privacy, allowing both additive and multiplicative error estimates to improve accuracy.
A regime-conditioned diffusion model for generating counterfactual limit order book trajectories, enabling stress testing and scenario analysis in financial markets.
A differentiable rendering framework designed for reconstructing transparent objects from multi-view images, allowing for efficient decomposition of geometry and materials.
A reinforcement learning approach that combines flow matching with an entropy regulator to generate competitive actions in real-time decision-making for autonomous driving.

D.term: Diffusion Actor-Critic with Entropy Regulator via Flow Matching (DACER-F)
D.definition: DACER-F integrates flow matching into online reinforcement learning to enhance the efficiency of generative policies, enabling competitive action generation in real-time decision-making.

A model designed to translate music from various instruments into piano versions using a diffusion architecture, incorporating pitch and loudness encoders to extract relevant features for high-quality timbre generation.
An innovative framework that integrates Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models with Deep Reinforcement Learning for robust portfolio optimization under stress scenarios.
A model predictive control approach that utilizes diffusion-based sampling methods in discrete combinatorial domains, focusing on feasibility constraints and planning horizon effects.
An anthropomorphic 3D-printed prosthetic hand designed for enhanced dexterity in typing tasks, aiming to replicate the intuitive control of human hands for precise manipulation.
A proposed central bank digital currency for the Euro area, aimed at providing a secure and efficient means of digital payment while addressing privacy and technical feasibility concerns.
A computing paradigm that integrates computation directly within memory arrays to reduce data movement and enhance energy efficiency, particularly beneficial for deep learning applications.
A conceptual framework that views large language models as a distinct asset class, emphasizing the economic implications of data-compute complementarities and innovation pressures in the AI industry.
A digital replica of a physical system that protects private information while optimizing mobile transmission scheduling in energy-constrained environments.
A novel pipeline for generating synthetic images from high-quality 3D models and programmatically generated camera poses, enabling repeatable and quantifiable digital twin creation.
A digital representation of physical systems used for real-time monitoring and reliability management, particularly in the context of 3D integrated circuit packaging.
A virtual representation of a physical environment that integrates real-world data for accurate simulation and testing of wireless signal propagation using ray tracing.

DIMAFx is an explainable multimodal framework for cancer survival prediction that produces disentangled representations from various data sources, enhancing interpretability while maintaining high prediction accuracy. It integrates histopathology images and transcriptomics data to provide insights into survival outcomes.
An architecture for multi-modal stance detection that separates stance information into textual-dominant, visual-dominant, and cross-modal shared components to improve performance.
A framework that guides the design of context-aware, scalable AI interfaces based on workflow complexity, AI autonomy, and AI reasoning, facilitating dynamic human-AI collaboration.
A previously unidentified issue in real-time operating systems where the maximum bandwidth of peripheral devices decreases as more tasks are added, potentially leading to data loss.

This framework proposes a method for distributed stochastic optimization under delayed gradient models, demonstrating that a pre-chosen diminishing step size is sufficient for effective optimization in federated learning scenarios.

D.term: Diminishing Step Size in Federated Learning
D.definition: A framework for distributed stochastic optimization that utilizes a pre-chosen diminishing step size to effectively manage delayed gradient updates from local agents.
A conceptual framework that explores the tension between parental care for children's prospects and the principle of equal opportunity, highlighting the complexities of managed inequality in society.
A method for processing sensory data on neuromorphic hardware that allows for the direct injection of analog signals, eliminating the need for power-intensive analog-to-digital conversions.
A technique for accelerating first-order primal-dual methods specifically for convex-concave saddle point problems, enhancing convergence rates through spectral properties of the coupling matrix.

A sparse subgraph that certifies connectivity in directed graphs, ensuring a minimum number of internally node-disjoint paths between any two nodes, crucial for understanding directed connectivity problems.

D.term: Directed Graph Model for Text Visualization
D.definition: A framework for designing and studying time-dependent visualizations of textual data, focusing on the relationships between evolving narratives in digital news and social media.

A method that utilizes reinforcement learning to steer generative diffusion models for neural architecture generation, specifically tailored for directed acyclic graphs representing neural architectures.
An optimization technique for bilevel programs that does not require the convexity of the lower level program, allowing for more flexible problem-solving in hierarchical optimization scenarios.
A novel approach to enhance large language models with capabilities to understand speech from multiple talkers by integrating directivity, particularly in applications like smart glasses.

A target-specific condition that allows for efficient inference on scalar-valued targets after adaptive data collection, ensuring that estimators remain asymptotically normal and semiparametrically efficient.
A novel framework for designing continuously steerable differential beamformers using uniform circular arrays, incorporating directional derivative constraints to enhance target signal acquisition.
A novel class of prior distributions for Bayesian Neural Networks that addresses limitations in interpretability and overconfidence by introducing structured sparsity.
A key parameter in active learning that governs label complexity for decision trees, analyzed under specific assumptions to achieve polylogarithmic label complexity.

A generative AI pipeline that facilitates semantic communication by transmitting the meaning of physical signals over channels, adapting dynamically to channel impairments through autoregressive and diffusion-based algorithms.

This technique involves incorporating a fixed share of discounted sales of expiring perishables into demand forecasts to improve accuracy, addressing the challenges faced by grocery retailers in predicting actual demand.

A theoretical limit in fair division problems that constrains the value guarantees for agents in the allocation of indivisible items, specifically indicating that every partition yields high value asymptotically limited by Θ(√n).
A framework for adaptive hyperparameter selection in ill-posed linear inverse problems that balances bias and variance without prior knowledge of the nuisance function's smoothness.

A statistical technique used to model consumer choice behavior, employing methods such as multinomial logit (MNL) and mixed logit (MMNL) models to analyze preferences and intentions in various contexts, including drone delivery services.
A framework for sampling efficiency in score-based discrete diffusion models, focusing on convergence guarantees under continuous-time Markov chain formulations.
A novel mutation operator that enhances Quality-Diversity algorithms by incorporating discrete, gene-level crossover to improve solution discovery across diverse behavioral niches.
A proposed model that maps the structural phase transitions and computational complexity of random 3-SAT instances directly to the combinatorial topology of an N-dimensional Boolean hypercube.
A model that maps the structural phase transitions of random 3-SAT instances to the combinatorial topology of Boolean hypercubes, providing a discrete perspective on algorithmic complexity.
A representation framework that converts continuous surface electromyography (sEMG) signals into interpretable discrete tokens, facilitating robust muscle state analysis.
A framework for probabilistic programming that focuses on discrete probability distributions, enabling intuitive modeling of complex probabilistic scenarios.

A discrete-event simulation model is a computational approach used to analyze complex systems by simulating the operation of a process as a discrete sequence of events over time. This model is particularly useful for examining supply chain dynamics and disruptions in vaccine manufacturing.

A concept that establishes a connection between the stability properties of discrete-time algorithms and their corresponding continuous-time dynamical systems, ensuring that exponential stability in continuous time implies stability in discrete time.

DiSE is a self-evaluation confidence quantification method for diffusion large language models that assesses the quality of generated sequences by calculating the probability of regenerating tokens given the full context.
A pretraining approach for 3D CT vision-language models that uses contrastive learning and prompt-based disease supervision to improve alignment between medical reports and imaging data.
DERL is a framework for robust multimodal sentiment analysis that employs hybrid experts to adaptively disentangle multimodal inputs, addressing the challenges of missing or corrupted data.

A method in music generation that separates different aspects of audio, such as structure and timbre, to allow for controllable synthesis. This approach enhances the ability to manipulate audio characteristics during generation.
A compositional approach in Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) that combines transition systems representing alternative system behaviors, preserving the testing power of original scenarios.

Disk-Resident Graph Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) methods are designed for efficient search in large datasets where memory is limited, focusing on performance trade-offs related to storage, layout, and execution paradigms.
A benchmark challenge aimed at evaluating conversational AI systems in medical dialogues, focusing on understanding multi-speaker interactions in noisy environments.

D.term: Disruption Tolerant Networking for UxV Control
D.definition: A network control system that leverages disruption-tolerant networking principles to enhance the flexibility and reliability of unmanned vehicle (UxV) operations in challenging environments.
A general speech restoration model that utilizes disentangled speech representation and degradation-prior guidance to improve performance across diverse speech restoration tasks.
A fitness function introduced for the $(1+1)$ evolutionary algorithm, characterized by large plateaus and valleys, demonstrating the density of optimal mutation rates.

A lightweight model for phishing email detection that combines distilled MobileBERT with a BiLSTM architecture enhanced by multi-head attention, achieving semantic discrimination with fewer parameters.

DistillER is a knowledge distillation approach for Entity Resolution (ER) that transfers knowledge from large language models (Teachers) to smaller models (Students), optimizing performance while reducing computational costs.
A design approach for UI components that emphasizes creating variations that are both mimetic and distinct, facilitating easier instantiation and exploration of design spaces.

A measure evaluating the performance of mechanisms that partition items among agents based on their ordinal preferences, focusing on expected performance rather than worst-case scenarios to provide a more optimistic analysis.
A protocol designed for smart grid IoT networks that enhances reliability and energy efficiency by integrating chaotic frequency-hopping spread spectrum and link-adaptive quality power control.
A recording scheme that enables efficient record-and-replay of multi-threaded programs by synchronizing distributed threads without excessive synchronization overhead.
A system architecture that supports simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) through continuous aperture arrays, enabling efficient energy and data transmission to multiple users.
A method for finding balanced separators in planar graphs that can be computed in a distributed manner, improving efficiency in graph optimization problems.
A novel technique for recording the execution of multi-threaded programs that minimizes synchronization requirements, facilitating efficient debugging and testing.
A configuration of multiple IMUs used for full-body shape sensing in vine robots, enhancing localization and configuration determination in complex environments.
A technique that partitions model execution across multiple edge devices to meet strict throughput and latency requirements for AI-driven workloads in extreme edge computing environments.

D.term: Distributed LLM Pretraining During Renewable Curtailment
D.definition: This approach aligns the pretraining of large language models with renewable energy curtailment windows, utilizing excess clean energy for training across geo-distributed GPU clusters, thus optimizing energy consumption.

Distributed prompt caching is a technique that enhances local large language model inference on resource-constrained edge devices by sharing intermediate processing states across multiple devices, improving performance through cooperative caching.
DQGP is a method that leverages quantum computing to enhance the modeling capabilities of Gaussian Processes in multi-agent systems, allowing for better scalability and complex correlation capture.

D.term: Distributed Semi-Speculative Parallel Anisotropic Mesh Adaptation
D.definition: A distributed memory method for anisotropic mesh adaptation that separates meshing functionality from performance aspects, avoiding global synchronization techniques to enhance efficiency in high-performance computing.
An optimization technique that employs a distributed semismooth Newton approach combined with augmented Lagrangian methods to solve optimization problems across networks with local cost functions.
A systematic study and algorithmic approach for detecting and enumerating sub-hypergraphs in distributed settings, addressing theoretical and practical challenges in graph algorithms.
A novel unmasking schedule for diffusion language models that adapts to the unknown dependence structure of the target data distribution, enhancing generation quality without prior knowledge.

This technique involves training large language models to predict multiple future tokens simultaneously, enhancing sample efficiency without increasing training time, thereby improving downstream capabilities.
A sequential prediction model that allows learners to abstain from making predictions when faced with potentially corrupted instances, balancing between stochastic and adversarial scenarios.

A technique that investigates the ability to recover predictive distributions over numerical targets from Large Language Models (LLMs) without relying on autoregressive generation, aiming to reduce computational costs.

Distributional Treatment Effects refer to the impact of interventions on different segments of a population, particularly in contexts where outcomes are not uniformly distributed, such as user engagement in streaming platforms.
A forecasting framework that minimizes worst-case expected loss by integrating parametric and nonparametric models under distributional uncertainty.

DrIGM is a principle in cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning that ensures each agent's robust greedy actions align with team-optimal joint actions, addressing environmental uncertainties and enhancing performance in real-world applications.
A control method for three-phase AC-DC rectifiers that achieves rapid voltage regulation and current tracking under load and grid disturbances, utilizing a transformed error-state dynamics model.

This concept focuses on generating multiple (near-)optimal yet diverse median strings from a set of input strings, enhancing flexibility and robustness in decision-making across various applications, including bioinformatics and pattern recognition.
A competition framework focused on Bengali long-form speech recognition and speaker diarization, addressing challenges in low-resource language processing.
A Large Language Model-augmented system designed for real-time interactive enhancements in live streaming environments, featuring a three-layer architecture for rendering various visual effects.

Diffusion Model-based Generative Image Steganography (DM-GIS) is a technique that conceals secret messages within generated images using diffusion models, enhancing security against traditional steganalysis methods.

DMart is a decentralized scheduling framework for datacenter networks that allows applications to independently encode urgency, treating network bandwidth as a competitive marketplace to optimize diverse performance goals.

Doc2Spec is a multi-agent framework that utilizes large language models to automatically generate formal programming specifications from natural language descriptions, streamlining the process of ensuring API compliance with programming rules.
A taxonomy of document chunking methods critical for dense retrieval systems, including LLM-guided and contextualized strategies that aim to preserve contextual information during segmentation.
A technique that reduces the complexity of logical constraints in neural networks by slicing the domain, which accelerates the generation of explanations for model predictions.
A novel approach for enhancing Physics-Informed Neural Networks by utilizing domain-specific Fourier features for positional encoding, improving training and interpretability.

A method for multi-domain all-in-one image restoration that constructs a task prompt pool to facilitate the handling of various restoration tasks within a single model, enhancing adaptability across different image domains.
An efficient interior-point method for solving hyperbolic programming problems, featuring a new straight-line program representation for compact and efficient computation.

A computational problem that involves determining the minimum size of a dominating set in a graph, where a dominating set is a subset of vertices such that every vertex not in the set is adjacent to at least one vertex in the set.
A modulation technique designed for uplink multiple access over doubly-dispersive channels, addressing challenges related to high peak-to-average power ratio and detection complexity.

Double-BD-RIS (Beyond Diagonal Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface) refers to a multi-user MIMO communication setup that enhances performance through inter-element connections and cooperative beamforming among multiple BD-RISs.
A novel approach in radio SLAM that utilizes double-bounce non-line-of-sight paths to enhance localization and mapping accuracy, deriving bounds and algorithms for improved performance.

D.term: Downstream Task Inspired Underwater Image Enhancement
D.definition: This approach focuses on enhancing underwater images specifically for downstream tasks like semantic segmentation and object detection, addressing challenges such as blurring and color inconsistencies to improve target recognizability.

Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) is an optimization algorithm that incorporates differential privacy into the training process, achieving a Privacy-Utility Trade-Off of O(1/ε²).

DPSQL+ is a privacy-preserving SQL library that enforces differential privacy while adhering to governance requirements such as the minimum frequency rule, ensuring that released query results do not expose sensitive information.
A framework for optimizing machine learning inference on FPGA-based systems using reinforcement learning to efficiently allocate computational resources for deep learning applications.
A novel approach to enhance reasoning in long chain-of-thought models by addressing systematic overthinking and optimizing reasoning budgets without post hoc techniques.

The TREC 2025 DRAGUN Track focuses on the development and evaluation of assistive Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems aimed at helping readers assess the trustworthiness of news articles by generating well-attributed reports.
DRAMA (Domain Retrieval using Adaptive Module Allocation) is a framework designed to enhance the scalability and efficiency of neural information retrieval models across multiple domains.

DRAMatic is a Processing-in-Memory (PIM) architecture designed to accelerate homomorphic encryption operations by integrating processing units and memory on the same chip, enhancing memory bandwidth and reducing computational overhead.
A formal description framework for DRAM protocols using timed Petri nets, providing an expressive model to understand complex memory bank operations and protocol specifications.
A QA benchmark that evaluates vision-language models on real students' handwritten responses to math problems, highlighting models' weaknesses in understanding and diagnosing student errors.

DREAM is a unified framework that integrates visual representation learning and text-to-image generation, optimizing both discriminative and generative objectives through techniques like Masking Warmup.

Dream2Learn is a framework that enables continual learning by allowing models to autonomously generate structured synthetic experiences from internal representations, facilitating self-improvement without reconstructing past data.

DreamFlow is a deep reinforcement learning-based local navigation framework that enhances a robot's perceptual horizon through conditional flow matching, allowing for improved navigation in cluttered environments.

A continuous framework for structural graph refinement that generalizes the Weisfeiler-Lehman hierarchy, enabling the distinction of graphs that 1-WL cannot separate, thus enhancing graph isomorphism testing.

DReX is an explainable multimodal recommendation framework that processes diverse data sources while addressing challenges such as cold-start and data sparsity by aligning user and item representations across different modalities.
A conditional diffusion framework for trajectory generation in mobile robots, designed to balance kinematic smoothness and terminal precision in unstructured environments.

A method for generating counterfactual explanations in autonomous driving scenarios, which identifies minimal semantic changes required to alter a model's decision using gradient-based optimization on continuous embeddings.

A novel numerical encoding method for large language models in autonomous driving, aimed at improving numerical reasoning and precision in sensor measurement processing and control command generation.
A system designed for automated trains to detect collisions and driving-over events, integrating new technological components for enhanced safety and automation.
This approach utilizes deep reinforcement learning to enhance user throughput in multi-panel mmWave radio access networks by optimizing dynamic beam selection and management strategies.

An algorithm that integrates deep reinforcement learning with block coordinate descent techniques to solve nonconvex optimization problems in wireless networks, enhancing decision-making capabilities.

DrPose is a Direct Reward fine-tuning algorithm that enhances the pose accuracy of single-view 3D human reconstruction models by enabling post-training on diverse poses without the need for extensive 3D human datasets.
A modular distillation framework for one-step flow matching in speech synthesis, designed to reduce computational costs and improve efficiency in generating high-quality speech outputs.

DSLean is a framework designed for bidirectional translation between expressions in the Lean proof assistant and external domain-specific languages (DSLs), simplifying the interaction between proof assistants and automation tools.

The DSPy optimization framework provides a systematic approach to instruction optimization for large language models, enhancing reasoning performance through various prompting techniques for tasks like tabular fact verification.

DTAPP-IICR is a Delivery-Time Aware Prioritized Planning method designed for preflight planning of UAV fleets in dynamic airspace. It incorporates incremental and iterative conflict resolution to enhance scalability and flexibility in Unmanned Traffic Management.

DTBench is a synthetic benchmark designed for evaluating document-to-table extraction capabilities, focusing on the ability to derive structured tables from unstructured documents while addressing reasoning and conflict resolution challenges.
DTT-BSR is a hybrid generative adversarial network that integrates rotary positional embeddings with a dual-path band-split recurrent neural network for effective music source restoration.
A benchmark specifically designed for type inhabitation in dependent type theory, providing a standard for evaluating implementations and performance in type-checking scenarios.

A concept in multi-period multi-asset martingale optimal transport that establishes the existence of dual optimizers under specific conditions, extending previous duality results to more complex financial scenarios.
A framework that integrates liquid-capacitance-extended models to enhance the interpretability of recurrent neural networks (RNNs), particularly in bio-inspired models.

D.term: Dual-Hop Joint VLC and Backscatter Communication
D.definition: A communication framework that combines visible light communication (VLC) with backscatter communication (BC) to enable energy-neutral data transmission in Ambient Internet of Things (A-IoT) deployments.
An algorithm that incorporates momentum into single-pass stochastic gradient descent for generalized linear prediction, achieving improved optimization performance in streaming settings.
A framework for audio-visual video highlight detection that utilizes two pathways in audio encoding: one for semantic understanding and another for capturing dynamic audio characteristics.

D.term: Dual-Pathway Audio Encoders for Video Highlight Detection (DAViHD)
D.definition: DAViHD is a framework that employs dual-pathway audio encoders to enhance audio-visual video highlight detection by utilizing both semantic and dynamic pathways.
