A phenomenon observed in large language models where early memories are prioritized over recent ones during conflicting information processing, contrasting with human memory patterns.
A novel memory wear-leveling method inspired by the golden ratio, designed to extend the lifespan of computer memory by ensuring even wear across memory cells.

A Bayesian inference technique for constrained inverse problems that utilizes dual-space sampling to characterize the posterior distribution, particularly effective in scenarios with noisy and incomplete data.

A novel negative sampling technique for implicit collaborative filtering that utilizes large language models without relying on textual information or fine-tuning, enhancing the sampling process for better recommendation performance.

Dual-View Predictive Diffusion (DVPD) is a lightweight model for speech enhancement that exploits the dual nature of spectrograms, treating them as both visual textures and frequency-domain representations to improve efficiency.

An inference system designed to alleviate storage bandwidth bottlenecks in agentic LLM inference by introducing dual-path KV-Cache loading, optimizing the flow of data between storage and processing units.

A lightweight framework designed to detect targeted attacks on black-box Large Language Models via a dual entropy lull pattern, enabling effective defense without high access rights or significant performance costs.

Duel-Evolve is an evolutionary optimization algorithm designed for large language models that utilizes self-preferences through pairwise comparisons to optimize outputs at test time without relying on external scalar evaluators.
DuoMo is a generative method that reconstructs human motion in world-space coordinates from noisy video inputs by factorizing motion learning into camera-space and world-space diffusion models.

A design and fabrication method that integrates Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) printing with pneumatic actuation to create shape-changing interfaces, allowing for complex functionalities through a single fabrication process.

A dynamic weighted graph structure embedding technique that integrates attention mechanisms and frequency-domain MLPs to enhance traffic forecasting by capturing evolving spatial-temporal dependencies.

D.term: Dynamic Access Point Selection and Pilot Power Allocation (DAPPA)
D.definition: A framework for dynamic access point selection and pilot power allocation in cell-free massive MIMO systems, aimed at mitigating inter-user interference and enhancing spectral efficiency through hierarchical correlation-based clustering.
A technique for analyzing Android apps that addresses the challenges posed by packers, enabling runtime behavior capture while mitigating anti-analysis techniques.

D.term: Dynamic Attention with Global Registers for Video Quality Assessment (DAGR-VQA)
D.definition: DAGR-VQA is a novel framework that integrates register-tokens directly into a convolutional backbone for spatio-temporal dynamic saliency in no-reference video quality assessment, enhancing the contextual understanding of video signals.

DBNs are a class of probabilistic graphical models that represent a set of variables and their conditional dependencies via a directed acyclic graph. They are extended in this context to estimate market risk measures like expected shortfall.

A technique used in speech enhancement networks to reduce computational load by adaptively disabling specific channels based on current signal properties, enhancing efficiency without compromising performance.

DCDP is a framework that integrates chunk-based action generation with real-time corrections for robotic manipulation tasks, enhancing adaptability in dynamic environments through a self-supervised dynamic feature encoder.

A concept introduced to enhance situation awareness and decision-making in Uninhabited Ground Vehicles (UGVs) by creating an ontology-driven dynamic knowledge base that adapts to complex environments.
A novel approach to dense retrieval that adapts to new domains without the need for extensive retraining, addressing limitations of traditional fine-tuning methods.
DDFKs are used in fluid simulations to maintain divergence-free velocity fields while leveraging memory-efficient spatial representations, enhancing the simulation of incompressible fluid dynamics.
A technique for wearable navigation that utilizes a fingertip stimulation prototype with a grid of electrodes to render spatiotemporal patterns, enhancing interaction during walking.

D.term: Dynamic Enumeration of Regular Language Infixes
D.definition: A technique for dynamically enumerating infixes of a given word that belong to a fixed regular language, allowing for efficient updates through letter substitutions.
A novel framework for reconstructing speech audio envelopes from EEG data that utilizes dynamic temporal priors to improve fidelity and reduce noise in the reconstruction process.
A component of a semiparametric joint VaRES framework that captures time-varying tail severity in risk assessments, enhancing the understanding of extreme risk factors in financial contexts.

A novel architecture that enhances the performance of domain-specific accelerators by dynamically orchestrating data paths at execution time, addressing performance fragility with irregular input patterns.

D.term: Dynamic Flexible Assembly Flow Shop Scheduling
D.definition: A formal definition of a scheduling problem that addresses the coordination of part completion and product-level kitting in dynamic multi-product delivery environments.
A nonparametric revealed preference framework that allows for the valuation of goods considering habit formation, separating the effects of hedonic pricing from intertemporal choice.
A neuromorphic approach to solving the Edge User Allocation problem using an attractor network with a Winner-Takes-All mechanism and dynamic heuristic biasing.

This decomposition technique addresses all-to-all GPU communication bottlenecks in large-scale training clusters by optimizing scheduling and traffic management, improving overall communication efficiency in GPU-based systems.

A parallelism strategy that adaptively reconfigures communication groups and degrees of parallelism during the training of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), addressing issues of load imbalance and hardware utilization.
Dynamic Importance Estimation is a technique used to enhance persona following in role-playing language agents by adapting character behaviors based on situational context.
A technique in Text-to-SQL systems that dynamically associates user queries with database values, improving the accuracy of schema and value identification in ambiguous queries.

The Dynamic Kernel is a component of a proposed quantum-native organizational principle that allows nodes in a Quantum Internet to construct and execute a local Plan of Action (PoA) for service intents through dynamic composition of micro-protocols.
A new mathematical concept distinct from traditional dynamical systems, which may provide insights into Turing incomputable computations and has implications in various fields of mathematics.
A parameterized dynamic logic designed for program verification that simplifies reasoning by directly applying operational semantics without the need for extensive rule redesign.

A novel speech enhancement strategy that employs a robotic arm to dynamically adjust the geometry of a microphone array in response to varying acoustic conditions, improving speech recognition in noisy environments.

D.term: Dynamic Model of Purpose at Work
D.definition: A theoretical framework that describes how firms can invest in creating purpose and meaning in the workplace to enhance employee motivation and productivity. This model integrates worker preferences for meaning with firm profit objectives.
A method used for modeling forward rates in bond pricing that dynamically adjusts parameters based on market conditions, integrated within a no-arbitrage framework.

A model-agnostic framework that utilizes state machines to dynamically adjust the personality expression of large language models in response to conversational context, enhancing interactive performance.
A methodology for making longitudinal and time-to-event predictions in clinical settings, utilizing joint modeling and landmarking techniques to inform treatment decisions.
A method that assembles modular components of small defender teams into larger forces efficiently in polynomial time, enabling scalable drone defense strategies against adversarial swarms.

An optimization algorithm designed for Schatten-$p$ quasi-norm regularized problems that dynamically selects step sizes and avoids costly singular value decompositions, improving computational efficiency.
A method for optimizing spatio-temporal forecasting by selectively training on a dynamic subset of samples, reducing computational inefficiencies associated with redundant data in large datasets.
A variable scoping mechanism used in SAS that resolves variable references at runtime by searching through active macro layers.
A method for optimizing sensor network performance by dynamically partitioning nodes into informative subsets to minimize battery consumption and adapt to changing conditions.
An innovative technique for optimizing FP8 computation in digital compute-in-memory architectures by dynamically predicting and adjusting bitwidths for aligned-mantissa representations.
A statistical modeling technique used to capture network spillovers in the context of Generative AI adoption and its impact on productivity and systemic risk in the banking sector.

An advanced neural network architecture that integrates Phase Lag Index-based Graph Attention Network for spatial modeling and Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit for capturing temporal dynamics, aimed at early detection of pornography addiction in adolescents.
A collaborative optimization technique for executing backdoor attacks in self-supervised learning environments, designed to enhance stealthiness and reduce detectability of triggers.
A machine learning-based approach that recommends stocks from the S&P 500 by dynamically selecting the top 20% based on selected stock indicators with good explanatory power.

A technique for addressing non-ideal reference issues in analog in-memory computing, focusing on mitigating systematic drift in weight updates during training by dynamically tracking the symmetric point.
A method for analyzing articulated objects by separating dynamic and static components without prior knowledge of part mobility, enhancing segmentation and reconstruction quality.

DynamicAdaptiveClimb is an advanced cache replacement policy that dynamically adjusts the promotion distance of cached objects based on recent access patterns, outperforming traditional caching strategies like LRU.
An approach that enhances Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by leveraging graph topology representation preferences to improve their performance on graph-related question answering tasks.
A quantization framework designed to optimize multi-hop all-reduce operations in large model training by bridging the gap between quantization best practices and multi-hop aggregation.
A framework for generating first-order ambisonics from dynamic and acoustically complex 360-degree videos, addressing challenges related to dynamic sound sources and environmental effects.

A rethinking of Transformer architectures specifically designed for solving partial differential equations (PDEs), addressing the computational inefficiencies of traditional numerical solvers in high-dimensional settings.

U.definition: A federated learning approach that offloads part of the computation from client devices to servers while preserving data privacy by keeping data and labels on the clients' side.
A protocol for securely computing U-statistics of higher degree in a federated learning context, addressing challenges of local differential privacy and scalability.
A framework for Uncertainty-Aware 3D Emotional Talking Face Synthesis that addresses audio-vision emotion alignment and multi-view fusion strategies by incorporating emotion prior distillation.

UAMTERS is an uncertainty-aware mutation analysis framework designed for deep learning-enabled robotic software, which injects faults to assess the dependability of test suites in dynamic environments.

A novel architecture for vehicular networks that utilizes uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) to enhance coverage and connectivity in road-constrained environments by forming localized cell-free clusters.

A novel architecture utilizing uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) equipped with rotatable intelligent reflecting surfaces to enhance low-altitude network coverage and connectivity in 6G systems by dynamically adjusting the reflection pattern.

UC-Secure Distributed Key Generation (DKG) is a protocol that enables multiple parties to collaboratively generate a common public key while keeping the signing key secret-shared, ensuring security through verifiable-sharing enforcement without relying on traditional Verifiable Secret Sharing mechanisms.

UCB-LCV is an extension of the classical stochastic multi-armed bandit problem that incorporates limited control variates to provide tighter confidence bounds and reduce regret in scenarios with partial auxiliary information.

A unified dataset that consolidates diverse English cross-document coreference resolution corpora into a consistent format, correcting inconsistencies and enriching datasets for reproducible research.

ucTrace is a multi-layer profiling tool designed for UCX-driven communication in high-performance computing (HPC) systems, providing fine-grained communication traces and visualizations that capture transport-layer behavior.
ULTRA is a unified framework for autonomous humanoid loco-manipulation that integrates physics-driven neural retargeting and high-level task specifications to generate versatile behaviors.
A high-performance protocol defined in the Ultra Ethernet 1.0 specification, designed for reliable communication in AI and HPC systems, potentially fully hardware-accelerated.

URGT is a unified multi-patch transformer architecture that enhances monocular high-resolution depth and normal estimation by partitioning images into patches and incorporating coarse depth and normal priors.
A novel sequential recommendation model that leverages recent advances in large language models to improve user interaction history learning in large-scale recommender systems.
An ultra-lightweight framework for multimodal sleep stage scoring that efficiently integrates multiple physiological signals using a novel Dual-Stream Separable Convolution Block.
A modeling concept that captures hierarchical structural architectures of ROS 2 systems from code and launch configurations, facilitating better documentation and evolution of systems.
Innovative hardware designs for general matrix multiplication that utilize unary arithmetic, aimed at improving efficiency in low-precision deep learning applications.

UnSyncOT is a novel dynamical framework that synchronizes transport-reaction flows between multiple spaces while accommodating mass variation, reducing complex multi-space problems to single-space formulations.

A modeling framework for unbounded client-server systems where clients can dynamically enter and exit the system, enabling the expression of properties that are difficult to capture using traditional Petri nets.

U.term: Uncertain Agile Earth Observation Satellite Scheduling Problem (UAEOSSP)
U.definition: A novel problem that incorporates uncertain factors such as task profit and resource consumption into satellite scheduling, reflecting real-world complexities in agile environments.
Analytical results for the propagation of uncertainty through trained multi-layer perceptrons, providing exact expressions for mean and variance of outputs with multivariate Gaussian inputs.
A framework that solves the Black-Scholes partial differential equation as a mesh-free global surrogate, incorporating uncertainty quantification through an anchored-ensemble fine-tuning stage.
A novel approach for reconstructing photorealistic ground views beneath dense forest canopies using conventional RGB images and neural radiance fields, eliminating the need for specialized sensors.
A sampling method for Gibbs distributions that operates effectively in high dimensions, providing dimension-independent convergence guarantees in KL divergence.

A Unified Skill-centric framework that supports skill-aware planning and facilitates automatic evolution of skill libraries in robotic manipulation tasks, enhancing adaptability without manual intervention.
A unified framework for scientific document retrieval that integrates facet-aware retrieval methods to address mismatches between document-centric and question-driven retrieval approaches.

U.term: Unified AI-driven Data QC and DataOps Management
U.definition: A comprehensive framework that integrates various data quality control methods into a continuous governance layer, enhancing data integrity and management in regulated environments.
A framework for analyzing explanations of machine learning model predictions, encompassing sufficient and contrastive reasons across different probabilistic and non-probabilistic contexts.
A comprehensive framework that integrates learning-based argument mining with quantitative reasoning and ontology-based querying to enhance the analysis of debates and complex texts.
A comprehensive approach to analyze and derive sufficient and contrastive explanations for machine learning model predictions, integrating various computational complexity aspects.

A framework that maps various dexterous hand morphologies into a shared codebook, facilitating improved generalization and scalability in dexterous hand manipulation guided by free-form language commands.
A method for merging documents from multiple specialized retrieval channels in large-scale e-commerce search, optimizing for user conversion while adhering to latency constraints.
The UMQ framework jointly addresses both missing and noisy modalities in multimodal data, enhancing model robustness by treating these issues as a unified low-quality modality problem.
A motion planning strategy for Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships that ensures compliance with maritime regulations while navigating dynamic environments.
An architecture designed to enhance personal information retrieval by integrating temporal, spatial, and activity metadata, aligning with human memory recall mechanisms.
A computationally efficient framework for certifying optimality in sparse generalized linear models, reformulating relaxations as composite optimization problems that converge linearly.
A resource management strategy in aerial heterogeneous networks that aims to balance communication reliability and performance under diverse delay constraints and user mobility.
This paradigm focuses on region-of-interest (ROI) based image compression using a generalized Gaussian model to improve coding performance by adapting to distribution characteristics.

An adaptation of Pitts' proof-theoretic technique for uniform interpolation to the intuitionistic multi-succedent setting, enabling the construction of uniform interpolants for intuitionistic modal logic KM.
UniG2U-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark that categorizes generation-to-understanding evaluation into multiple regimes and subtasks, assessing the effectiveness of unified multimodal models.

A unified framework that integrates mesh and 3D Gaussian Splatting rendering with single-pass rasterization and proxy-based deformation, addressing occlusion and transparency challenges in graphics applications.

The problem of approximating the volume of the union of multiple geometric objects, which can be solved in optimal time using specific query types. This estimation is crucial in various applications including computer graphics and spatial data analysis.

Uniqueness type systems allow for efficient mutation in programs while maintaining a semantics that supports value independence, facilitating easier reasoning about software systems and formal verification.
A unified framework for scale-aware 3D reconstruction from multi-view images, integrating geometric priors to enhance robotic perception and navigation tasks.

UniSkill is a dataset that pairs university curricula with professional competencies, providing both manually annotated and synthetic data based on the ESCO taxonomy to address skill extraction challenges.
A modular framework that integrates text-to-speech and audio-to-face modeling, facilitating internal feature transfer to improve consistency between audio and facial expressions.

A unified, end-to-end diffusion framework for generating high-fidelity speech and lip-synchronized video, leveraging Multi-Modal Transformer Blocks to model the temporal correspondence between audio and video.
The UCP formalizes clustering as the maximization of a computable partition utility over metric spaces, proving NP-hardness and providing a unifying framework for various clustering paradigms.

UCL is a mathematical framework for systematic prompt optimization in AI models, transforming prompt engineering into a structured process that significantly reduces token usage and improves performance.
A novel architecture designed to enhance wireless network performance by leveraging artificial intelligence to optimize spectral efficiency and reduce signaling overhead.
A framework aimed at restoring speech quality across various degradation conditions, focusing on training target selection and data quality to enhance signal fidelity.

U.term: Universality of Shallow and Deep Neural Networks on Non-Euclidean Spaces
U.definition: A framework that establishes the universal approximation property for neural networks operating over general topological spaces, extending results beyond traditional Euclidean settings.

Algorithms that are optimal for every input graph in the worst case, extending the concept of universal optimality to data structures, particularly in dynamic graph problems involving vertex-weighted trees.

An efficient continual multi-task training framework for universal audio representation that integrates various audio tasks into a unified instruction format, improving performance across diverse audio domains.
A decentralized Federated Learning framework that employs a peer-based mixnet for unlinkability, allowing for secure model sharing without revealing participant identities.

A method for synthesizing training data from code graphs to improve the performance of large language models in newly released software frameworks, addressing issues of hallucination and understanding codebase relationships.
A two-stage flow-feature-based pipeline for unsupervised IoT device traffic profiling that utilizes density-based clustering to adaptively update models as traffic evolves.

U.term: Unsupervised Continual Learning for Amortized Bayesian Inference
U.definition: A framework that enhances Amortized Bayesian Inference by decoupling simulation-based pre-training from unsupervised sequential self-consistency fine-tuning, enabling the model to adapt to sequentially arriving data and distribution shifts.
A two-stage pipeline for profiling IoT device traffic using unsupervised learning techniques, specifically density-based clustering, to adaptively update models as traffic patterns evolve.
An unsupervised training strategy for hyperspectral image super-resolution that utilizes synthetic abundance data, decomposing images into abundances and endmembers through unmixing.

Unveiler is a framework designed for robotic manipulation in cluttered environments, which separates high-level spatial reasoning from low-level action execution, utilizing a Spatial Relationship Encoder to identify critical obstacles.

Unsupervised Progressive Domain Adaptation (UPDA) is a framework for no-reference point cloud quality assessment that employs a two-stage coarse-to-fine alignment paradigm to mitigate performance degradation across domain shifts.
A data structure designed for efficient similarity search in large-scale datasets that supports real-time updates, addressing the challenges posed by frequent data shifts.

A memory-efficient context parallelism technique that utilizes headwise chunking to enhance the processing of long sequences in Transformer models, optimizing memory usage while maintaining performance.
A structural result showing that online maximization of non-monotone Diminishing-Return submodular functions can be linearized under specific conditions, leading to improved regret bounds.
Urban Antifragility is a concept that describes how urban environments can benefit and grow stronger from shocks and stresses, as opposed to merely surviving them.
UrgenGo is a non-intrusive, urgency-aware GPU scheduling system designed for autonomous driving applications, which prioritizes GPU executions without needing access to application source code.
A unified generative framework designed to improve performance in automatic speech recognition and speech enhancement across varying noise and channel conditions.
A prototype write-ahead log implemented in Rust that aims to provide reliability and high performance for fault-tolerance in user space applications.

U.term: User-Centric Evaluation of Conversational Recommender Systems
U.definition: A paradigm for assessing Conversational Recommender Systems (CRS) that focuses on capturing subjective qualities such as user satisfaction, trust, and rapport through user-centric methods.
A technique for optimizing the prefill stage of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by selectively recomputing tokens based on user queries, improving efficiency in KV cache reuse.

U.term: User-Space Framework for Coordinating Multi-runtime Workloads
U.definition: A framework designed to manage and schedule multi-runtime and multi-process workloads in high-performance computing environments, addressing the challenges of oversubscription and interference.
A forecasting approach that optimizes decision-making under constraints such as transaction costs and risk limits by incorporating a utility objective and a friction operator.
Utonia is a self-supervised point transformer encoder designed to unify point clouds from diverse domains, enabling consistent representation learning across various sensing geometries and densities.
A hybrid user search simulation framework that integrates traditional user simulators with adaptive LLM agents to create a more dynamic and personalized user experience in interactive search systems.

A hybrid user search simulation framework that combines traditional user simulators with adaptive large language model agents, leveraging grounded data to create more dynamic and personalized user experiences in interactive search systems.

B.definition: B-DENSE is a novel framework for dense ensemble network learning that utilizes multi-branch trajectory alignment to enhance structural information retention during inference, addressing the limitations of existing distillation techniques.

An unsupervised speech emotion recognition method utilizing a modified Group Relative Policy Optimization to enhance sample selection during the learning process, addressing data sparsity and annotation bias.
A bounded variation of the posit number format designed for high-performance computing, offering improved efficiency over traditional floating-point formats by utilizing variable-length fields.
A crowd-sourced mapping pipeline that utilizes Bayesian B-spline fusion for high-definition map generation using consumer-grade sensors, enhancing localization and mapping accuracy.

A unified language-pivoted pretraining framework designed for heterogeneous multi-modal remote sensing object detection, which decouples modality alignment from downstream task learning to enhance optimization stability and generalization.
An undirected graph derived from a directed graph where edges are defined based on a linear order of vertices, used to study the directed clique number.

BadRSSD is a novel backdoor attack targeting the representation layer of self-supervised diffusion models, allowing for stealthy manipulation of semantic representations through poisoned samples with triggers in Principal Component Analysis (PCA) space.

BALI is a branch-aware framework that integrates large language models to improve the inference and verification of loop invariants, enhancing the precision and scalability of reasoning about iterative algorithms.
A ranked preference aggregation method based on a modified PageRank algorithm that consistently identifies the Condorcet winner in ranked choice elections without damping.
A technique used to improve the upper bound for the Euclidean Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) by leveraging specific crossover strategies that enhance the efficiency of tour length estimations.

A module used in time-frequency domain dual-path models for source separation that compresses frequency information by encoding features for predefined subbands, emphasizing low-frequency parts to improve efficiency in high-sampling-rate tasks.

The Bandwidth Coloring Problem generalizes graph coloring by imposing minimum separation constraints between adjacent vertices, relevant in frequency assignment applications. This paper presents a systematic study of SAT encodings for BCP, proposing a unified framework with multiple encoding methods.

B.term: Bank Risk Interlinkage with Dynamic Graph and Event Simulations (BRIDGES)
B.definition: BRIDGES is a framework designed to analyze systemic risk in banking systems, particularly in BRICS countries, by utilizing dynamic graphs and event simulations to capture complex interdependencies among banks.
A Bayesian framework designed for decomposing time series data into trend and seasonal components, offering robust estimation even in the presence of abrupt changes.

Bauplan is a code-first lakehouse framework that employs typed table contracts, Git-like data versioning, and transactional runs to ensure safe and reproducible operations in data analytics and AI environments.
A probabilistic framework for signed distance function fusion that incorporates view planning on CPU, addressing the limitations of traditional volumetric fusion techniques in conveying uncertainty.

The Bayesian Adversarial Multi-Agent Framework is designed for AI for Science tasks, coordinating multiple LLM-based agents to enhance reliability and reduce error propagation in multi-agent workflows.
An equation that reformulates stochastic optimal control problems using Bayesian methods, incorporating observable side information to derive optimal policies.
A framework that utilizes Bayesian confidence propagation to enhance the performance of neuromorphic solvers, particularly in real-time applications.

Bayesian Model Averaging is a statistical technique that combines predictions from multiple models to improve accuracy and account for model uncertainty, particularly useful in analyzing profitability in energy sectors.
A study exploring the correspondence between Bayesian networks and graphical representations of proofs in linear logic, aiming to develop a proof-theoretical account of Bayesian inference.

A probabilistic model-based optimization technique that is particularly effective for hyperparameter tuning in machine learning models, enhancing performance through systematic exploration of parameter spaces.

A method utilizing Gaussian Processes to automate the search for optimal scheduling configurations in heterogeneous multi-core architectures, addressing multi-objective optimization between energy efficiency and latency.

B.term: Bayesian Optimization with Block Coordinate Descent
B.definition: This scalable framework for base station configuration utilizes Bayesian optimization combined with block coordinate descent to optimize parameters while mitigating the curse of dimensionality.

An approach for developing autonomous AI agents in mobile networks that focuses on real-time adaptation and transparency in decision-making, overcoming limitations of traditional deep reinforcement learning methods.
A statistical approach for constructing and rebalancing index-tracking portfolios that incorporates uncertainty quantification and operational constraints.

B.term: Bayesian Uncertainty Analysis Framework for Multi-Agent Debate (MAD)
B.definition: A framework that decomposes total predictive uncertainty into epistemic and aleatoric components, enhancing the understanding of information exchange in multi-agent debate scenarios.
An approach to address the challenges posed by diverse user devices in beam-based communication, emphasizing the need for machine learning algorithms to consider hardware differences.
A parameter derived for generalized uniform rectangular arrays (URAs) that quantifies the spatial region where near-field beams can be focused, enabling enhanced spatial degrees of freedom.

BeamVLM is a generative model that leverages vision-language models to predict communication beams between unmanned aerial vehicles and ground stations, enhancing the semantic understanding of dynamic environments for improved generalization.

A novel machine learning framework that learns interpretable and identifiable optimization structures from data, facilitating the modeling of both single optimization problems and hierarchical compositions.

A framework that utilizes vision-language models to create 3D layouts that prioritize human behavior and spatial requirements, ensuring that generated scenes accommodate user-specific anthropometric data.

This technique focuses on identifying and correcting systematic behavioral biases exhibited by large language models (LLMs) in economic and financial decision-making tasks. It draws on insights from cognitive psychology and experimental economics to enhance model performance.
A framework designed to quantify how LLM-based agents update their expectations based on heterogeneous signals, integrating behavioral economics with Kalman filtering techniques.
A framework that utilizes large language models as calibrated measurement instruments to assess behavioral parameters like loss aversion and herding, providing insights into asset pricing models.

A comprehensive benchmark suite designed for evaluating algorithms in stationary Mean Field Games, focusing on discrete-time and discrete-space settings to assess robustness and generalization in multi-agent systems.
A comprehensive benchmarking and evaluation framework for assessing the performance of large language models in high-level synthesis code generation, facilitating the transition from RTL to HLS.

This benchmark consists of 180 tasks designed to evaluate the performance of Large Language Models in generating syntactically correct and functional ABAP code, focusing on the effectiveness of compiler feedback for iterative improvement.
This benchmarking study evaluates various deep learning models for time-series anomaly detection across multiple heterogeneous cloud telemetry datasets to assess their generalizability.
A multistage approach developed for transcribing Bengali long-form speech, utilizing fine-tuned models and speaker diarization techniques to address challenges in low-resource language technology.

A set of community benchmarks for long-form Bangla automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speaker diarization, including a long-form ASR corpus and a speaker diarization corpus aimed at realistic multi-speaker content.

Bespoke OLAP refers to the synthesis of workload-specific database engines that eliminate structural overhead by optimizing for fixed analytical workloads, leveraging LLM-based code synthesis to reduce manual engineering efforts.
A machine learning-based framework for asset selection and portfolio construction that uses a sparse graphical model to identify conditionally independent or negatively correlated assets.
An advanced architecture for reconfigurable intelligent surfaces that improves beamforming gain by utilizing group-connected configurations in multi-user MIMO communication.
A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating code agents, focusing on cross-repository reasoning and domain-specialized problem solving.
An extension to the Border Gateway Protocol that facilitates the advertisement of Delay-Tolerant Network endpoint reachability information, aimed at simplifying configurations in DTN communication.

A hard physical constraint used in a neuro-fuzzy framework for quantum error attribution, preventing classifiers from attributing impossible output distributions to noise, thus improving diagnostic accuracy.

An advanced Monte Carlo simulation technique that enhances the valuation of corporate bonds in financial networks by effectively handling rare default events and capturing higher-order network effects through a two-level sampling strategy.

An innovative radar architecture that utilizes a bi-static configuration with Intelligent Reflective Surfaces (IRS) to improve target detection and parameter estimation in non-line-of-sight scenarios.

Bialgebraic Abstract GSOS is a categorical framework that models the operational and denotational semantics of programming and process languages, ensuring that bisimilarity is a congruence and coincides with denotational equivalence.
A speech-augmented benchmark dataset for assessing bias in multilingual audio language models, featuring balanced demographic attributes and extensive audio recordings.
A linear programming relaxation for the Steiner Tree problem that provides improved integrality gap bounds, recently shown to be at most 1.898.

An ultra-lightweight neural network hardware accelerator inspired by Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks, designed to operate without multiplication by replacing nonlinear functions with binary, learnable thresholds.
A framework for modeling optimization problems where decisions are made sequentially by multiple players with conflicting objectives, extending beyond NP into the polynomial hierarchy or PSPACE.

A bilevel optimization approach that strategically reweights training domains to minimize validation loss, optimizing domain weights in an outer loop while adjusting model parameters in an inner loop.

A dual-level optimization approach that combines a soft actor-critic agent for real-time antenna positioning with zero-forcing precoding design, addressing the challenges of dynamic antenna placement in mobile user environments.
A framework that enhances the efficiency of querying large language models by selectively leveraging explanation features, reducing computational costs associated with LLM queries.
An interaction approach for teleoperation that specifies assembly tasks through hierarchies of relative and absolute object constraints, enhancing remote manipulation capabilities.

An interaction technique in extended reality (XR) that allows users to specify remote assembly tasks through hierarchical constraints, facilitating teleoperation with relative and absolute object positioning.
A neural network architecture designed for binaural personal sound zone rendering, utilizing simulated acoustic transfer functions to improve generalization in sound reproduction.
A computational model that mimics the insect visual system to convert dense visual inputs into sparse, discriminative codes, employing a self-supervised contrastive learning objective.

BioEnvSense is a human-centered security framework that employs a hybrid CNN-LSTM model to analyze biometric and environmental data, enabling context-aware security decisions to prevent behavior-driven cyber incidents.
A security framework that combines a hybrid CNN-LSTM model to analyze biometric and environmental data for context-aware security decisions.
BioFabric Motifs are visual representations of complex networks that utilize effective vertex and edge ordering to expose salient patterns, allowing for clearer understanding of large graphs.
A neuro-inspired approach that uses living neural circuits as a computational substrate for reservoir computing, leveraging their spontaneous and stimulus-evoked activity for pattern recognition.

A learning rule that integrates neurobiological principles into neural network training to enhance generalization, adaptation, and learning efficiency, mimicking the mechanisms of biological neural systems.
A resource-efficient bioacoustic foundational model designed for Internet-of-Things (IoT) applications, focusing on reducing computational costs while maintaining performance in bioacoustic tasks.

This framework utilizes 3D human reconstruction from video data for gait analysis, extracting biomechanically meaningful markers and integrating them with OpenSim for accurate joint kinematic estimation.

A neuro-symbolic framework that integrates probabilistic planning with deterministic finite state machines, designed to enhance scientific planning in laboratory environments by ensuring a Design-Verify-Rectify workflow.
A mathematical model used to represent spatially distributed supply and demand nodes, where edges form based on the proximity of demand nodes to supply nodes within a defined service range.

A method for analyzing quantization error in threshold level quantizers, establishing a relationship between uniform quantization and bipolar PFM to describe the spectral properties of quantized signals.
A measure of the shared information fraction between a system's observations, actions, and outcomes, essential for evaluating the effectiveness of complex systems under varying conditions.

BIRDTurk is the first Turkish adaptation of the BIRD Text-to-SQL benchmark, created through a controlled translation process that maintains the logical structure of SQL queries while adapting schema identifiers to Turkish.

BiScale is a two-tier energy optimization framework for disaggregated LLM serving that jointly optimizes placement and dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) to enhance energy efficiency while meeting strict service level objectives.

A concept used to separate polynomial complexity classes based on bisimulation-invariant queries, which are defined in the polyadic mu-calculus. This approach leverages model checking techniques to establish relationships between different complexity classes.
A design methodology that enables real-time few-shot learning on edge AI hardware with arbitrary fixed-point bit-widths, overcoming limitations of traditional design environments.

Bithoven is a high-level language designed to enhance the usability and security of Bitcoin smart contracts by bridging gaps in state management and resource liveness, addressing critical vulnerabilities in the UTXO architecture.

A hardware architecture that supports runtime-reconfigurable multi-precision quantized multiplication, enabling efficient computation in neural network accelerators by adapting precision dynamically across layers.

The Blind Code Rate Recovery Metric is a new measure for evaluating the quality of code rate recovery in linear block codes, derived from rank-based estimation techniques, particularly useful in high noise conditions.

Blindfold is an automated attack framework targeting embodied Large Language Models (LLMs), exploiting the misalignment between linguistic instructions and physical outcomes to reveal vulnerabilities in real-world actuation.

A novel algorithm designed for monitoring the longitudinal power profile of fiber-optic links, which processes data in blocks to reduce complexity and latency compared to traditional least squares methods.
A tensor structure used in structure from motion that captures more information than essential matrices, enabling the recovery of multiple camera configurations through Tucker decomposition.
A set of optimization techniques that utilize block-coordinate strategies to reduce memory consumption in image restoration tasks while maintaining high performance.
A family of novel bounds that generalize PAC-Bayes bounds, providing a framework for estimating expected generalization error based on subsets of training data.
An NP-hard problem involving the arrangement of blocks of varying widths and masses to maximize overhang, with connections to other optimization problems.

This framework leverages blockchain technology to establish a resilient trust mechanism for cross-domain service orchestration in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) networks, facilitating secure and efficient collaboration among multiple UAVs.
A routing approach that utilizes blockchain technology to enhance security and stability in low-altitude intelligent networks, particularly for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs).
A lightweight profiling tool that measures the Blocking Ratio to differentiate between genuine I/O wait and contention caused by Python's Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) in edge AI systems.

Bloom Filter Look-Up Tables are a mechanism for managing cryptographic keys securely in distributed databases within the Web3 ecosystem, enhancing data privacy and scalability in decentralized systems.
A variant of the QuasarRAT malware that utilizes DLL side-loading techniques to bypass security defenses during multi-stage infections targeting specific regions.

A layout-aware multimodal retrieval system designed for large-scale engineering repositories, capable of detecting drawing regions and applying region-restricted OCR to produce structured metadata from legacy documents.
A comprehensive benchmark for detecting false and synthetic content across 79 languages, combining human-written and LLM-generated samples to enhance detection tools for low-resource languages.
An end-to-end tool for verifying Timed Security Protocols using SMT-based bounded model checking and multi-agent modeling, facilitating the analysis of timed interactions in security protocols.

Brown-von Neumann-Nash (BNN) dynamics is a classical model in evolutionary game theory repurposed to achieve regularization-free last-iterate convergence in zero-sum games, simplifying the convergence process without the need for hyperparameter tuning.

A three-layer architecture for decision-making in repeated games that integrates an echo state network for implicit inference, a cognitive filter for strategic tools, and a metacognitive governance layer.
Bayesian Optimization Genetic Algorithm, a framework that combines evolutionary search with Bayesian optimization to efficiently navigate the protein sequence space for desired characteristics.
A framework that integrates evolutionary search with Bayesian optimization to efficiently explore the sequence space for designing novel proteins with desired characteristics.
A transformer-based language model specifically fine-tuned on bond market news, designed to provide sentiment signals that integrate with financial forecasting models.

A measure derived from Boolean entropy that quantifies the amount of information that can be obtained about a random variable through its Boolean representation, particularly in the context of the Boolean Central Limit Theorem.
A data-driven online learning method that adaptively selects the look-back window size for financial risk forecasting based on historical data and realized scores.
A strategy for generating synthetic triplet data to optimize embedding models for low-resource languages, leveraging large language models to bridge the data gap.

The Boreas Road Trip dataset is a multi-sensor dataset for autonomous driving, comprising 60 sequences collected over 9 diverse real-world routes, aimed at evaluating algorithms under varying traffic and weather conditions.
An architecture for an anthropomorphic social robot that utilizes a large language model to enhance human-robot interaction by conveying non-verbal social cues.
An architecture designed for predicting the Short-Time Objective Intelligibility (STOI) metric, incorporating convolution blocks to enhance performance without requiring clean reference speech.
A novel Ising machine architecture that utilizes a tunable parameter to modulate spin dynamics, allowing for improved optimization by balancing escape from local minima and rapid convergence.

A technique that improves the efficiency of logic-based explanations for neural network decisions by tightening neuron bounds and eliminating unnecessary binary variables, significantly reducing explanation time.
A strategic mechanism used in metaheuristics to manage the exponential growth of non-dominated solutions in multi-objective optimization problems, enhancing solution diversity.

A formal class of locality-preserving deterministic operators that act on graph-indexed state systems, allowing for incremental updates with a computational cost independent of the system's total dimension.
A novel logical framework that enhances traditional modal languages by incorporating intuitionistic and modal relations to better express resource availability and scoping in program semantics.
A pruning test for exact Euclidean AkNN joins that utilizes partitioned spatial datasets to optimize join evaluations by leveraging row group statistics.
A task allocation algorithm for multi-core real-time vehicle control systems that integrates blocking time estimation and resource similarity analysis to minimize scheduling failures.

An omnifunctional brain foundation model that is jointly pretrained on fMRI, EEG, and MEG data, capable of processing both unimodal and multimodal inputs to leverage complementary spatiotemporal dynamics in neuroscience tasks.
BrainFuse is a unified infrastructure designed to integrate realistic biological modeling with core AI methodologies, addressing the incompatibility between AI frameworks and neural simulation tools.
A high-fidelity EEG foundation model that employs Dual-Domain Residual Vector Quantization for improved signal reconstruction, addressing the challenges of low signal-to-noise ratios in EEG data.

An OS abstraction that enables AI agents to perform agentic exploration by providing copy-on-write state isolation and a structured lifecycle for process management, including atomic commit and rollback semantics.

An exact algorithm designed for solving the Moving Target Vehicle Routing Problem (MT-VRP) that incorporates a novel dominance criterion in its pricing subproblem to efficiently manage moving targets and time-dependent travel costs.
A model that generalizes the geometric rough path framework, allowing for the approximation of higher-level signatures through iterative compositions of lower-level signature maps.

A multi-agent framework for seamless brand integration in text-to-video generation, addressing challenges such as maintaining prompt fidelity and ensuring brand recognizability while embedding advertiser brands into generated videos.
An exploratory prototype that spatially decomposes webpages into movable panels within a mixed-reality environment, allowing for mid-air and surface-attached interactions.
An audio deepfake detection framework that leverages fine-grained breath information to enhance generalization and detection accuracy against deepfake audio.

A self-supervised learning framework for extracting representations from CAD models to recognize machining features, utilizing a masked graph autoencoder based on geometric Attributed Adjacency Graphs.
A quantization module used for implementing fixed-point data processing in edge AI hardware, allowing for customizable bit widths during few-shot learning tasks.
A system designed to enhance parasport training by integrating a reconstruction pipeline for 3D play sequences and an embodiment-aware visualization framework for player tracking.

B.term: Broadband Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface Channel (BRISC) Dataset
B.definition: A dataset comprising channel state information (CSI) measurements collected at 5.53 GHz using a 256-element reconfigurable intelligent surface, aimed at facilitating research in wireless communication.

A framework used in constructive mathematics to study decidability properties, allowing for nuanced classifications of propositions based on Brouwer ordinals, extending traditional notions of decidability.
A new stochastic activation function for LSTM networks that enhances gradient stability and learning performance in noisy financial time series by leveraging properties of Brownian motion.
A mathematical framework for parallel machine scheduling optimization that reduces computational complexity from exponential to polynomial by using adaptive temporal discretization.

A type of uncertainty set in robust optimization where each parameter has an interval of possible values, and the total deviation from their lower bounds is constrained. This concept is particularly relevant in two-stage robust selection problems.

BuffCut is a buffered streaming graph partitioner that enhances the quality of partitioning in streaming scenarios by combining prioritized buffering with batch-wise multilevel assignment to reduce edge cuts.

An advanced method for resource allocation in wireless systems that leverages machine learning to simultaneously allocate multiple resources, addressing the limitations of single-resource allocation in reliability-critical applications.
A cloud-native OLAP engine designed for efficient multimodal retrieval and incremental computation, addressing challenges in data analytics infrastructures for modern enterprises.

S.definition: A generalization of the Hamiltonian cycle problem that requires visiting each vertex of a graph with specific hop constraints between consecutive vertices, introducing new complexity considerations.
An ultra-low bitrate sound effect compression model that leverages diffusion autoencoders and offline quantization techniques to achieve high-quality audio compression down to 0.096 kbps.

A dataset and evaluation framework designed to infer speaking voice character attributes in literature, enhancing synthetic audiobook narration by assigning plausible voices to fictional characters based on their attributes.
A self-supervised learning framework for mean-opinion-score (MOS) prediction that employs spectral augmentation to enhance performance across multi-rate speech samples.
An agentic AI framework designed for end-to-end formal verification through multi-agent collaboration, addressing challenges in robustness and reliability in complex tasks.
A specialized Deep Neural Network architecture developed to accurately model the SABR framework for interest rate volatility, addressing limitations in traditional analytical approximations.
A new metric designed to assess the transferability of pre-trained large language models across different domains by leveraging spatial and contextual features without additional training.
A safety-generalizable large language model designed to enhance safety in task planning for robotic systems, capable of adapting to novel safety constraints across multiple domains.
A system-level co-design for managing KV-cache sharing in large language model inference that mitigates timing side-channel vulnerabilities while optimizing cache management.
A machine-learning-based scanner designed to detect malicious Python pickle files, providing a lightweight alternative to complex policy generation and code instrumentation.

A framework that formalizes safety decision-making in vision-language models through a structured protocol involving a planner and responder, enhancing multimodal safety by integrating user intent and visual evidence.
A hierarchical visual navigation framework that enhances safety and generalization in mobile robots by integrating learned guidance with safety-constrained control.
A contrastive dataset containing pairs of safe and unsafe prompts used to train models for improved safety in generative tasks, particularly in Text-to-Image generation.
A framework for retrieval-augmented question answering that constructs a chunk-level graph to facilitate multi-hop evidence retrieval across heterogeneous data sources.

A two-layer decision architecture that integrates Large Language Models with fuzzy Control Barrier Function verification and graph-structured knowledge retrieval to enhance UAV decision-making under dynamic conditions.

A trainable low-bit attention mechanism that quantizes multiple attention matrix multiplications while maintaining fine-tuning performance, aiming to bridge the performance gap with full-precision attention.
A framework that leverages semantic consistency to reweight samples in recommendation systems, effectively reducing noise from implicit feedback and improving prediction accuracy.

SAILOR is an energy-efficient, ultra-lightweight RISC-V architecture designed for IoT security, balancing compact area and integrated cryptographic support to mitigate performance and security trade-offs in constrained environments.

Stylometry-Assisted LLM Analysis (SALA) is a method that combines quantitative stylometric features with large language model reasoning to enhance authorship attribution while assessing deanonymization risks in textual data.

Samaritan AI agents are designed to function as either unconditional fair proposers or strict receivers within a bipartite hybrid population model, particularly in contexts like hiring and negotiation, emphasizing the importance of fairness in asymmetric social structures.

An open benchmark designed to evaluate the capabilities of Large Language Models to escape from isolated sandbox environments, implemented as a Capture the Flag evaluation using a nested sandbox architecture.

Low-degree sandwiching polynomials are used to approximate target functions while providing bounds on function values, enhancing learning under distribution shifts and contamination. This method improves degree bounds for fundamental function classes.
A non-parametric method for constructing smooth and arbitrage-free option price surfaces, utilizing linear programming for model calibration to market quotes.
SARA is a memory allocation strategy designed for mixed-criticality systems, addressing memory competition issues in edge devices to prevent performance degradation and missed deadlines.

The Sequential Adaptive Residual Quantization (SARQ) framework dynamically allocates code lengths based on item path entropy to improve retrieval efficiency and generalization in semantic ID-based generative retrieval systems.
A novel architecture for spoofing-robust automatic speaker verification that employs score-aware gated attention to dynamically modulate speaker embeddings based on countermeasure scores.
A locality-centered technique for selective token attention in transformers, reducing computational complexity by narrowing the attention scope to relevant tokens.
An approach to designing satellite constellations for global quantum networks, focusing on optimizing satellite orbital parameters to maximize entanglement generation rates.

This approach enables efficient deployment of UAV-mounted Base Stations (UAV-BSs) in dynamic environments without the need for extensive training, focusing on user satisfaction and adaptability to heterogeneous distributions.
A programming technique that enhances cache performance in high-performance attention kernels by reducing L2 cache misses, validated on NVIDIA GB10 hardware.
A framework that accelerates kernel memory access from userspace by reducing the overhead of traditional system call interfaces.
A framework that integrates hardware and algorithm design to overcome the limitations of traditional graphics pipelines in generative game engines, facilitating high-resolution neural simulations.

A method for automated test generation that leverages large language models to improve coverage rates by eliminating covered code and enhancing the generation of unit tests for complex software methods.
An extension of Gaussian processes designed to handle multiple correlated functional tasks and functional covariates, enabling principled uncertainty quantification in complex mechanical systems.
A method for attitude estimation using only scalar measurements from inertial sensors, incorporating gyroscope bias compensation for improved stability.
A theoretical framework connecting wavelet transforms with spiking neural networks, utilizing leaky integrate-and-fire neurons to implement discrete mother wavelets.

S.term: Scaling Law for Bandwidth Under Quantization
S.definition: A derived law that relates the bit depth of Analog to Digital Converters (ADCs) to the effective bandwidth of signals with $1/f^α$ power spectra, showing that each additional bit can approximately double the bandwidth under certain conditions.

A theoretical exploration of scaling laws that govern the trade-off between model quality and training costs in high-dimensional linear regression, particularly focusing on the effects of quantization.
A methodology for assessing the security exposure of IoT devices by analyzing scan data to identify population-level risks based on network configurations.
A structural parameter for directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) that respects edge directions, introduced to study parameterized complexity in graph problems, particularly in phylogenetic networks.

A spatial context attention mechanism designed for progressive Gaussian splatting, enhancing the efficiency of storage and transmission of 3D scene representations by optimizing the bitrate of Gaussian attributes.
A novel preprocessing method proposed for auditory attention decoding that enhances the performance of electroencephalography-based systems by capturing relevant features in auditory signals.

A robustness evaluation method for large language models (LLMs) that assesses their sensitivity to variations in natural language descriptions of coding tasks, particularly in the context of code generation.

A novel simulation paradigm that allows for the ex-ante evaluation of urban traffic regulation policies by modeling both direct and indirect impacts on mobility systems, leveraging advances in data availability and computational power.
A pilot-free aggregation primitive for over-the-air federated distillation that provides phase-invariant estimates of weighted soft-label averages while mitigating noise-energy offsets.

S.term: SCENE (Semantic-aware Codec Enhancement with Neural Embeddings)
S.definition: A lightweight pre-processing framework that enhances perceptual fidelity in video by integrating semantic embeddings to mitigate compression artifacts from standard codecs.
A hierarchical agentic framework that generates simulation-ready indoor environments from natural language prompts, addressing the need for diverse and complex indoor spaces for robotic training.
An autonomous driving benchmark that incorporates multi-objective specifications and formal environment models to evaluate the performance of autonomous vehicles in complex traffic scenarios.
A generalization of matrix norms used in optimization problems, particularly for finding low-rank solutions in linear inverse problems.

SchedCP is a framework that enables autonomous Large Language Model (LLM) agents to optimize Linux schedulers by decoupling the semantic reasoning from execution, addressing the semantic gap in operating system scheduling.

SciDataCopilot is an agentic data preparation framework designed to facilitate the use of raw experimental data in AI-driven scientific discovery, addressing challenges related to data heterogeneity and domain expertise.
A unified open infrastructure that integrates 293 million scholarly papers from eight sources using DOI normalization while preserving source-level schemas, built on DuckDB and Parquet files.

A logical framework that introduces a segment modality and syntactic restrictions on data comparisons, providing characterizations for languages recognized by nondeterministic register automata with guessing.

ScrapeGraphAI-100k is a large-scale dataset created for LLM-based web information extraction, containing 93,695 examples collected from real-world extraction events across diverse domains and languages.
A stochastic optimization scheme that utilizes scattering transform coefficients with random paths to improve the efficiency of perceptual quality assessment in machine learning tasks.
An innovative transparent antenna array integrated into the screens of mobile devices, designed to enhance performance in ultra-high frequency bands and mitigate user-induced blockage issues.
A fixed-vocabulary tokenization method for digital ink that decomposes pen movement into unit pixel steps, enabling efficient representation and compression of handwritten text.
SDA2E is a Sparse Dual Adversarial Attention-based AutoEncoder designed to refine decision boundaries in anomaly detection tasks, particularly for imbalanced datasets.

A federated prompt learning approach that addresses local-global discrepancies in multi-party settings by refining subspaces and controlling divergence, allowing for heterogeneous client data adaptation.
A reference framework for estimating the carbon footprint of LLM inference, focusing on prompt-level carbon measurement to promote sustainability in AI practices.
SEAnet is a deep learning architecture designed for data series similarity search, utilizing Deep Embedding Approximation (DEA) techniques to improve performance on high-frequency and noisy datasets.
A framework for path-centric reward shaping in agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation training, enhancing sample efficiency and addressing sparse outcome rewards.

A technique identified in the Adam optimizer that normalizes the second moment of gradients, providing a theoretical basis for its superior convergence properties compared to stochastic gradient descent (SGD).
A novel method for identifying groups in overlapping multi-agent swarms by analyzing agent trajectories to infer latent group structures without ground-truth supervision.

A novel closed-form expression derived for the achievable secrecy rate in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) visible light communication (VLC) systems, considering peak and average intensity constraints.

Secure aggregation is a technique used in federated learning to protect client privacy by concealing individual updates during the model training process. It ensures that sensitive information is not leaked through inference attacks.

S.term: Secure and Interoperable Architecture for EHR Access Control
S.definition: A comprehensive architecture leveraging blockchain and IPFS for secure access control and sharing of electronic health records, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.

A framework designed to enhance the security of deep neural network (DNN) accelerators by minimizing hardware resource demands and off-chip memory access overheads, ensuring data confidentiality and computational integrity.
A framework designed to enhance security in Robot Operating System 2 (ROS 2) environments, which is vulnerable to supply chain attacks that can exfiltrate sensitive data.

A framework that automates the transformation of static anime illustrations into manipulatable 2.5D models by decomposing a single image into fully inpainted, semantically distinct layers with inferred drawing orders.
A framework that enhances the Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) for extracting high-fidelity contours from Scanning Electron Microscope images using a few-shot learning approach.
SELEBI is a signal-adaptive phase vocoder algorithm that reduces percussion smearing artifacts in time-stretched audio signals while maintaining stability and quality.
Quantitative theorems that establish necessary internal structures for artificial agents to achieve low average-case regret in decision-making tasks under uncertainty.
These theorems provide a quantitative framework for understanding the necessary internal structures required for artificial agents to achieve low average-case regret in uncertain environments.
A method that enhances uncertainty sets by identifying calibration examples that are exchangeable with test examples, particularly in interventional contexts.

SSA is an innovative attention mechanism that replaces traditional self-attention with a closed-form operator derived from the Kuramoto model, allowing tokens to be represented as oscillators with learnable frequencies and phases.
An approach for processing probabilistic skyline queries in distributed edge computing environments that adapts to dynamic conditions, improving efficiency and reducing latency.
A phenomenon observed in large language models where the confidence of the model's responses changes systematically during multi-turn conversations, influenced by prior outputs.

A framework for scalable distributed submodular bandit optimization that enables agents to coordinate under realistic communication constraints, focusing on one-hop communication and minimal message sizes.

A technique that reduces uncertainty in the output of black-box AI systems by generating multiple outputs for the same input and assessing their consistency, thus providing a reliability level for the system's predictions.
A technique for dimensional aspect-based sentiment analysis that enhances prediction reliability by executing a LoRA-adapted large language model multiple times and retaining only consensus tuples.

S.term: Self-dual Stacked Quantum Low-Density Parity-Check Codes
S.definition: These codes facilitate fault-tolerant quantum computation by allowing the implementation of transversal Clifford gates, constructed by stacking non-self-dual qLDPC codes to enhance encoding rates and distances.
SERP is a replanning approach for embodied navigation that allows robots to learn from recent experiences and evolve their action models in real-time, enhancing resilience in complex environments.

The Self-Healing Router is a fault-tolerant orchestration architecture for LLM agents that prioritizes routing decisions over reasoning, utilizing parallel health monitors to manage tool outages and risk signals.
A technique for enhancing reasoning capabilities in vision language models by refining reasoning paths through self-improvement, particularly in the context of visual question answering.
This method enhances spiking neurons by generating an internal prediction current based on input-output history, improving training efficiency and performance in Spiking Neural Networks.
A defense mechanism against backdoor attacks in Multimodal Diffusion Language Models, aimed at mitigating vulnerabilities while maintaining model performance on clean inputs.
A technique for constructing graph instances that reveal the algorithmic hardness of the clique problem by ensuring that both instances with and without a $k$-clique share identical properties.
An algorithm designed for leader election in anonymous networks that achieves self-stabilization with low memory complexity, addressing challenges in distributed systems.
An inference-time re-ranking method for diffusion planners that penalizes dynamically inconsistent plans, enhancing the robustness of offline reinforcement learning.

This technique employs unsupervised domain adaptation to improve visual 3D pose estimation of drone racing gates by enforcing geometric consistency, utilizing real image sequences annotated with drone odometry.

A paradigm that leverages vast amounts of unlabeled data to train models for speaker recognition, addressing the challenges of dependence on human-annotated data and improving generalization under unseen conditions.

S.term: Self-Supervised Learning for Non-Linear Inverse Problems
S.definition: A novel approach extending self-supervised learning to recover audio and images from clipped measurements, addressing challenges in training without ground truth references.

A technique that leverages self-supervised learning to extract speaker characteristics from speech data, analyzing how different dimensions of feature vectors represent attributes like pitch and gender.
An abstraction used for complex multi-query analytics over property graphs, which describes the graph schema and operations to improve the efficiency of natural language graph query generation.

A communication paradigm that focuses on conveying the intended meaning of messages rather than just the bit-wise transmission of data, addressing the challenges of semantic representation and extraction.

Semantic Communication (SemCom) is a paradigm shift in wireless communication that focuses on transmitting task-relevant semantics instead of mere symbols, enhancing efficiency but also introducing new vulnerabilities that require AI-driven defenses.
A model for collaborative data structures that enables explicit, local-first conflict resolution in asynchronous work environments, avoiding reliance on centralized coordination.

Two novel unsupervised metrics designed to evaluate the faithfulness of Large Language Models (LLMs) by analyzing information transformations and entropy measures in the context of question-answering tasks.
A framework designed for satellite-terrestrial networks that prioritizes task-relevant information over exact bit recovery, enhancing communication efficiency in low SNR conditions.

A failure mode in large language models (LLMs) where the model disregards locally defined semantics in favor of globally learned conventions, leading to incorrect reasoning in contexts with redefined terms.
An advanced approach to Flowchart Question Answering that enhances semantic and logic relationship understanding between nodes, improving the accuracy of answers derived from flowcharts.

Semantic Scheduling is an advanced scheduling approach that utilizes semantic analysis of processes to make context-aware scheduling decisions, prioritizing tasks based on their urgency and importance rather than traditional metrics.
A framework for object navigation that utilizes a persistent memory structure to consolidate past observations, enhancing performance in lifelong navigation tasks.
A tree-structured memory module designed for conversational AI that allows agents to access and update structured memory efficiently, outperforming traditional flat memory approaches.

A paradigm for sixth generation (6G) networks that utilizes both specialist and large models to enhance the perception and utilization of channel semantics, improving performance in massive MIMO systems.
An unsupervised video summarization method that leverages semantic information to enhance keyframe selection, improving the stability and coherence of generated video summaries.

This technique addresses the limitations of traditional viewport prediction in 360-degree video streaming by utilizing semantic understanding to anticipate user attention shifts, thereby reducing rebuffering stalls during high engagement moments.
An advanced mixed-format quantization method for video diffusion transformers that optimizes per-block quantization formats to maintain semantic and temporal coherence.
A generative vocoder that synthesizes waveforms directly from semantic latents, addressing limitations of traditional Variational Autoencoders in audio generation.
A self-evolving hierarchical multi-agent system designed for industrial IoT predictive maintenance, which adapts to changing operational conditions while maintaining interpretability and efficiency.

Feed-Forward Semantic 3D Gaussian Splatting, a framework for reconstructing generalizable semantic fields from sparse image inputs using a dual-branch architecture to extract color and semantic features.
An advanced channel estimation algorithm for XL-MIMO systems that operates in the near-field region, adapting to the unique characteristics of spherical wavefronts for improved performance.

S.term: Semi-Markov Framework for Predictive Runtime Analysis
S.definition: A hybrid method that combines runtime tracing with probabilistic modeling to estimate worst-case execution times in real-time systems, enhancing temporal constraint detection.
A method developed for Image-Text Sentiment Analysis that balances intra-modal and inter-modal sentiment relationships using a two-step strategy for improved representation quality.
Graphs whose vertices are points in real space, with adjacency defined by semialgebraic predicates, allowing for compact adjacency labeling schemes through polynomial partitioning methods.
A technique used to convexify nonconvex structures in pricing models, particularly in discrete and nonlinear markets, allowing for efficient pricing through dual variable analysis.
SemLoco is a Reinforcement Learning framework designed to enable legged robots to avoid obstacles in cluttered environments by integrating high-level semantic understanding with low-level control.

SemPipes is a declarative programming model that integrates LLM-powered semantic data operators into tabular machine learning pipelines, allowing for natural language specifications of data transformations.
A data preprocessing system designed to optimize cache partitioning and data sampling for multimedia ML model training, reducing bottlenecks in the data ingestion pipeline.

The SC3 closed loop integrates sensing, communication, computing, and control processes in industrial automation, enhancing decision-making while addressing security vulnerabilities in wireless communications.

This automated system detects and analyzes potential sensitive information leaks from publicly accessible URLs, employing lexical filtering and dynamic rendering techniques to quantify the extent of such exposures.

SEP-YOLO is a novel framework designed for transparent object instance segmentation that integrates a dual-domain collaborative mechanism and a Frequency Domain Detail Enhancement Module to improve segmentation accuracy in challenging conditions.
A form of logic used for reasoning about programs that manipulate pointers and mutable data, providing a framework for verification in the context of heap memory.
A novel lossless data compression method utilizing discrete latent transformers and reinforcement learning to optimize data storage and transmission without losing integrity.

S.term: Sequence-to-Sequence Learning for CNC Axis Reconstruction
S.definition: A machine learning approach that reconstructs CNC axis positions from accelerometer data, addressing security risks in machining process monitoring.
A series of transformer-based models designed for specific tasks in music source restoration, including music source separation, denoising, and dereverberation.

A scheduling variant for belief propagation decoders in quantum low-density parity-check codes that processes check nodes in a fixed order to stabilize message updates and improve error correction performance.
A method for continuous value prediction that utilizes regression techniques to handle complex and long-tailed data distributions, improving performance in recommendation systems.
A modeling language designed for automatically verifying the serializability of concurrent programs, ensuring that concurrent executions are equivalent to some serial execution.
A trustless and scalable reward settlement protocol for federated learning on permissionless blockchains, designed to minimize economic friction in decentralized environments.

SEval-NAS is a search-agnostic evaluation mechanism for neural architecture search that converts architectures into strings and embeds them as vectors to predict performance metrics, facilitating flexible evaluation across different hardware.
An efficient zero-shot visual language navigation framework that combines slow and fast cognitive collaboration to reduce latency and computation costs in navigation tasks.
