A comprehensive dataset designed for evaluating the perceptual quality of Singing Face Generation, addressing the lack of singing face datasets and the challenges posed by the domain gap between singing and talking.

SGAgent is a suggestion-guided framework for repository-level software repair that integrates large language models to analyze, localize bugs, and generate patches, addressing the reasoning gap in existing repair approaches.

SGMA (Semantic-Guided Modality-Aware Segmentation) addresses Incomplete Multimodal Semantic Segmentation (IMSS) by integrating semantic guidance to improve segmentation performance despite missing modalities.
Spectrogram-Guided Phonetic Alignment, a four-stage pipeline that enhances phonetic alignment for audio language models, improving the accuracy of Shapley value explanations.
A variant of physics-informed neural networks that employs shallow architectures for solving nonlinear PDEs, optimized using the Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm for efficient parameter tuning.
A derivative-informed neural operator framework designed for shape optimization under uncertainty, enhancing the efficiency of PDE-constrained shape optimization by encoding geometric variability.

A methodology that separates the short-term energy (gain) and normalized structure (shape) of audio signals in neural audio codecs, enhancing robustness to global signal level variations and improving bitrate-distortion performance.
A framework for continuum robots that enables geometry-aware control by encoding robot shapes from visual inputs, enhancing interaction with complex environments.
A scalable image-conditioned 3D editing framework that addresses the trade-off between visual controllability, geometric consistency, and scalability in 3D asset manipulation.
A novel mechanism for concurrent stack implementations that combines sharding and fetch&increment strategies to enhance performance and reduce contention.

Shared program state is a programming abstraction that facilitates interoperability between natural language code and formal programming languages, streamlining the integration of LLMs with traditional coding practices.
An innovative approach for efficient multi-LLM serving that enables cross-model sharing of decode execution, optimizing resource utilization in disaggregated settings.
A video generation framework that facilitates multi-agent shared world modeling, incorporating diverse scenes and interactive trajectories to enhance collaborative video generation.
A framework for stock selection and portfolio optimization that utilizes the Sharpe ratio to identify stocks with strong risk-adjusted performance while considering liquidity constraints.

A mathematical modeling framework that extends traditional neural networks to handle complex relationships in data, showing superior performance in biomedical applications compared to conventional graph neural networks.

A discrete gauge-theoretic framework that replaces the traditional single-global-dictionary approach with a collection of local semantic charts, each representing a context with its own feature space and information-geometric metric.
A precision-aware 1D feature-driven CNN accelerator designed for low-energy acoustic detection in UAVs, utilizing a shared multi-precision datapath.

A shift-tree is a new data structure used in dynamic edge coloring that tracks multiple possible recolorings of a graph, enabling efficient maintenance of proper edge colorings with minimal recoloring during edge updates.
The Sequential Hierarchical Integration Network (SHINE) is an architecture for reconstructing speech-silence sequences from MEG signals, addressing challenges in cognitive neuroscience.
A framework that reformulates ship trajectory prediction as a text-to-text generation problem, utilizing dynamic prompts to guide large language models in predicting ship movements.
A technique for identifying short races in observed execution traces, where a race type R is detected within a specified window size w, allowing efficient monitoring of concurrent programs.

A monitoring framework designed to detect short races in observed traces, specifically for race types such as happens-before and sync-preserving races. The framework includes efficient detection algorithms that optimize space usage.
A technique for enhancing time-domain cochlear transmission line models by correcting for short-wave effects, improving the simulation of basilar-membrane displacement.

The shuffle model of differential privacy (Shuffle-DP) enhances privacy protection by locally perturbing client updates and globally anonymizing them through shuffling, although it introduces vulnerabilities to adversarial attacks.

A novel 'Intent-to-Operation' framework that allows users to express interaction intents through natural eye-hand motions in Virtual Reality, reducing learning costs and improving interaction error tolerance.

SIDSense is an edge AI framework designed for database-free operation of TV White Space networks, aimed at enhancing disaster-resilient connectivity in vulnerable regions by ensuring compliance and operational continuity.
A pretraining method for vision-language models that aligns medical imaging data with textual reports using contrastive learning techniques to improve retrieval and classification tasks.
A Semantic-Grounded Instruction-Driven Generative Multi-Task Recommender designed to adapt to evolving trends and diverse recommendation tasks by grounding item entities in general semantics.

A hardware-aware heterogeneous quantization method for edge deep neural network inference that optimally allocates different bitwidths to layers based on their robustness, improving resource usage and accuracy.
A scalable in-generation watermarking technique for AI-generated videos that enables blind extraction, ensuring content protection without degrading video quality.
Signal First-Order Logic (SFO) is a formalism for expressing real-time specifications over hybrid systems, enhanced with a robustness-based quantitative semantics for richer property evaluation.

A numerical approach for solving multi-dimensional linear-quadratic stochastic control problems by transforming them into deterministic convex quadratic polynomial optimization using linear signature functionals.
A compact binary structure representing a graph, consisting of a rooted binary tree and 2-colored edges, used to compute shortest-path trees efficiently.
A variant of stochastic gradient descent that utilizes sign information for updates, exhibiting unique scaling laws under specific feature decay conditions.
A theoretical framework analyzing the performance of SignSGD in linear regression under power-law random features, identifying unique effects such as drift-normalization and noise reshaping.
A security risk in large language model systems where adversarial instructions embedded in automatically generated URL previews can lead to unintended data exfiltration without user awareness.
An ultra-low power wearable system designed for EMG-based silent speech recognition, enabling noise-robust and privacy-preserving human-computer interfaces.
An adhesive-free, solvent-reversible bonding technique for integrating silicone with glossy paper, enabling robust interfaces for soft robotics applications.

A scalable benchmark environment for evaluating distributed coordination in multi-agent systems, focusing on the Communication-Reasoning Gap across various algorithmic tasks and communication complexity levels.
A system that simulates A/B testing using persona-conditioned AI agents, allowing for rapid design evaluation without reliance on real user traffic, thus speeding up the iteration process.

SIMPLE-ICS is a virtualized industrial testbed designed for realistic end-to-end emulation of Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs) across integrated IT, operational technology (OT), and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks, facilitating comprehensive cybersecurity research.

Simplicial Normal Form (SNF) is a concept derived from topological relational theory that characterizes the acyclicity of functional dependencies in relational schema design using homological invariants.

An extension of homotopy type theory that incorporates directed morphisms, allowing for the composition of types known as Segal types, which enhances the expressiveness of type systems in formal proofs.
SimRecon is a compositional scene reconstruction framework that utilizes a 'Perception-Generation-Simulation' pipeline to create object-centric representations from real-world video inputs.
A data augmentation strategy that uses simulated ATFs to improve own voice detection in hearing aids, allowing for effective machine learning without complex hardware setups.
The Simulation-Based Inference framework utilizes neural networks for parameter estimation in large-scale agent-based models, addressing computational constraints in exploring parameter spaces.

A novel approach that frames augmented reading as a simulation-based optimization problem, utilizing resource-rational models to systematically evaluate text user interfaces and improve comprehension.

S.term: Simulation-based Policies with Asymmetric Real-world Residuals (SPARR)
S.definition: A hybrid approach combining simulation-trained policies with real-world residuals to improve robotic assembly performance by bridging the sim-to-real gap.

An agent powered by large language models that generates and optimizes deep neural network (DNN) accelerator simulators from natural language descriptions, utilizing techniques like In-Context Learning and Chain-of-Thought reasoning.
QCL is a technique that utilizes quantum circuits to model the dynamics of volatility time series, providing a novel approach to capturing volatility asymmetry in financial data.

S.term: Single-Source Negative Weight Shortest Paths Algorithm
S.definition: A randomized algorithm that solves the single-source shortest paths problem on directed graphs with negative weights, achieving nearly linear time complexity for dense graphs.

S.term: Single-Type Binary Variables in Unit Commitment
S.definition: A formulation of the unit commitment problem that utilizes a single type of binary variable to improve the efficiency of power production scheduling.
A Bayesian framework that characterizes the geometry of the loss landscape in machine learning, linking local learning coefficients to generalization properties.
An optimization technique for tree-structure indexes in CXL-based heterogeneous memory, enhancing performance by adapting to hardware characteristics and memory semantics.

SIREN is an AI-driven framework that enables voice-driven perception for UAV-assisted networks, facilitating the integration of voice communications into automated network management for emergency response.
A framework for single-image reflection removal that generates a synthetic dataset through path tracing, leveraging a Large Multimodal Model to enhance reflection removal accuracy.
SIRUP is a diffusion-based virtual upmixer for steering vectors, enabling highly-directive spatialization from first-order ambisonics data using a latent diffusion model architecture.

A novel architecture for a Rydberg atomic receiver that utilizes six-wave mixing to enable wideband radio frequency-to-optical quantum transduction, overcoming bandwidth limitations of conventional electromagnetically induced transparency.

SkeFi is a framework for cross-modal knowledge transfer in wireless skeleton-based action recognition, utilizing non-invasive sensors like LiDAR and mmWave to improve performance in challenging environments.
A novel framework for generating editable dynamic 3D Gaussians from monocular video input, using a hierarchical articulated representation to separate rigid and non-rigid motion.

In the Sketch&Patch++ framework, Gaussians are categorized into Sketch Gaussians, which capture high-frequency features like edges, and Patch Gaussians, which represent broader, smoother regions, enabling efficient structure-aware 3D Gaussian representation.

Sketch&Patch++ is a hybrid representation framework that categorizes Gaussian functions into Sketch Gaussians for high-frequency features and Patch Gaussians for low-frequency regions, mimicking traditional artistic techniques in 3D representation.
A framework that transforms 2D storyboard sketches into coherent 3D multi-human animations with fine-grained control over various motion parameters, utilizing a sketch-driven diffusion prior.
A predictive model for touch pointing success rates that accounts for the influence of screen edges on tapping accuracy, particularly for targets placed near screen boundaries.

This framework utilizes skill-based autonomous agents powered by Large Language Models to extract high-fidelity datasets from unstructured scientific literature, automating the curation process and reducing human error.
A benchmark designed to evaluate the ability of LLM agents to learn and reuse higher-level tool compositions, focusing on their capacity to manage long-horizon workflows with recurring structures.

SkillFortify is a formal analysis framework designed to enhance the security of agentic AI skill ecosystems by providing guarantees against supply chain attacks, moving beyond heuristic-based detection methods.
A benchmark that evaluates the vulnerability of large language model (LLM) agents to prompt injection attacks through skill files, highlighting the risks associated with extending agent capabilities.

S.term: Skin in the Game Experimental Design
S.definition: A novel experimental design where decision-makers incur direct costs when making choices for others, revealing differences in risk and time preferences compared to self-interested decisions.

A large-scale dataset designed for skin tone classification that addresses biases in existing datasets by providing granular, annotated examples across a spectrum of skin tones, facilitating fairer machine learning applications.
A learned, compact representation for skinning weights in 3D models, utilizing a FSQ-CVAE to optimize the rigging process by capturing intrinsic sparsity.

A high-fidelity 3D head reconstruction method that utilizes multi-view normal prediction to generate detailed head geometries quickly, overcoming limitations of traditional photogrammetry and optimization-based methods.

A scalable 3D photonic in-memory tensor core architecture designed for real-time AI inference, overcoming limitations of conventional photonic architectures through innovative design in topology and wavelength routing.
A framework for managing inference tasks across heterogeneous computing environments (on-device, RAN-edge, cloud) while ensuring service level agreements (SLAs) are met in real-time AI applications.

SLAM-LLM is an open-source multimodal large language model framework designed to support speech, language, audio, and music processing, facilitating the development of customized models for diverse audio-language tasks.
A novel framework for lip-to-speech synthesis that utilizes a hierarchical subspace latent diffusion model to directly map visual lip movements to a continuous latent space.
A method for reconstructing latent states and nonlinear restoring forces in multidimensional systems by utilizing periodic multisine excitation and relaxed sensing requirements.
A category of language models that are resource-efficient and suitable for on-device deployment, particularly evaluated for their effectiveness in human-robot interaction scenarios.
An approach that utilizes small language models to drive auto-parallelization in compilers for heterogeneous systems, improving performance over traditional heuristic-based methods.
This concept involves designing robust information strategies in multi-agent systems that guide agents to achieve perfect coordination through a simple threshold rule based on their utilities.
A transformer-based surrogate model for scalable mesh-free aerodynamic simulations that predicts physical quantities directly from raw geometries, avoiding the computational cost of mesh generation.

Tools designed to detect vulnerabilities in smart contracts, which have been evaluated primarily on synthetic datasets, raising concerns about their effectiveness in real-world applications and the trust developers place in them.

A SmartNIC is a network interface card designed to offload data processing tasks such as decoding and query execution, significantly reducing the computational load on CPUs and improving query performance in data lakes.

SMDIM is a diffusing strategy for efficient long-sequence diffusion modeling in symbolic music generation, combining global structure construction with light local refinement to reduce training and inference costs.
An algorithmic framework that generates datasets for rapid recalibration of option pricing models, integrating with supervised machine learning models for efficient valuation.
A method for estimating quantile parameters using a generalized method of moments framework that incorporates non-linearities and dependent data, enhancing computational tractability and consistency.
A model for cross-domain recommendation that addresses the user cold-start problem by leveraging correlations between items across different domains.
A property of neural networks that use smooth activation functions, allowing them to achieve optimal approximation and estimation rates by increasing width alone.
A self-attention-based multi-scale object detection algorithm specifically designed for snap detection in automated assembly, enhancing robustness and precision in challenging visual scenarios.
An adaptive mixed-precision training framework for large language models that optimizes subbyte precision during pretraining, enhancing model quality and training efficiency.
A learning-based approach for efficient design space exploration in High-Level Synthesis (HLS), aimed at identifying optimal hardware architectures under constraints.
An agent-based model that simulates wealth and income distributions, focusing on the effects of consumption on wealth inequality through a dimensionless parameter.
A game-theoretic framework that adjusts decision-making in multi-agent systems by balancing individual objectives with group welfare through a social weight parameter.
A domain-specific language developed for analyzing hardware documentation, enabling more accurate descriptions of hardware semantics and security properties.

Semantic-Oriented Distributional Alignment is a novel paradigm for generative recommendation that utilizes probability distributions over multi-layer codebooks, enhancing information retention and joint optimization between tokenizers and generative recommenders.
A reinforcement learning algorithm designed for continuous action spaces, utilized for optimizing cryptocurrency portfolio management by maximizing cumulative returns.
An advanced symbol detection technique that balances performance and complexity in MIMO systems by effectively mitigating inter-user interference.
A physics-inspired learning technique for non-line-of-sight imaging that reconstructs scenes from indirect measurements, utilizing shadows cast by hidden objects.
A differentiable reparameterization layer that integrates hard constraints into deep learning, addressing gradient saturation issues by mapping predictions onto constraint boundaries.
A novel standard for diskless booting that allows computers to boot from any location without the need for local storage, simplifying the user experience.
This proposed architecture aims to create an ecosystem of reusable and interoperable NLP tools for requirements engineering, addressing the challenges of tool comparison and long-term sustainability.
A testbed designed to quantify the resilience of Wi-Fi networks against deauthentication attacks by evaluating various wireless security configurations.

SOLAR is a recommendation system that employs SVD-optimized lifelong attention, reducing the computational complexity of attention mechanisms in Transformers while preserving the softmax mechanism for effective credit assignment.
A method for providing explainable justifications in mission planning by deriving explanations directly from the optimization model, ensuring faithfulness and causal attribution.
A topology-aware tokenization method that combines vector quantization with Self-Organizing Maps, enabling discrete generative models to maintain semantic structure in token space.

Sonar-TS is a neuro-symbolic framework designed for Natural Language Querying of Time Series Databases, utilizing a Search-Then-Verify pipeline to improve the retrieval of meaningful events and intervals from extensive temporal records.
SongSong is a music generation model that restores ancient Chinese SongCi by predicting melody and generating singing voice and accompaniment, showcasing advancements in music generation.
A dynamically-updating benchmark for open Lean tasks sourced from real-world formalization projects, aimed at improving the usability and capability of AI provers in formal mathematics.

An open-source singing voice synthesis system designed for high-quality, controllable singing generation based on symbolic musical scores or melodic representations, enhancing practical deployment in real-world workflows.

This training approach utilizes spatial audio and sonification as primary feedback channels for orientation and mobility instruction, aiming to standardize and enhance learning experiences for blind and low-vision individuals.
A method for performing delay-synchronous channel sounding using off-the-shelf multi-antenna WiFi devices, addressing synchronization stability and reference power level challenges.
A privacy attack in federated learning where a central server attempts to identify the client associated with a specific data point used in training, raising concerns about data privacy.
A decentralized weighted bandwidth allocation system that leverages network telemetry to compute and enforce bandwidth allocations in large-scale cloud environments without per-flow tracking.
An algorithmic approach that provides a space-space trade-off for directed st-connectivity in the catalytic space model, optimizing workspace and catalytic memory usage.

Spacecube is a fast inverse hyperspectral georectification system that implements a complete pipeline to efficiently georectify hyperspectral data, addressing the slow and artifact-prone nature of traditional methods.

SPADE is a SIMD Posit-enabled compute engine designed to enhance the efficiency of deep neural networks by supporting multiple Posit formats within a single architecture, balancing numerical precision and energy efficiency.

SPARe stands for Stacked Parallelism with Adaptive Reordering, a fault-tolerance framework designed for large-scale LLM pre-training systems that minimizes restart costs by masking node failures during gradient synchronization.

A learning approach in transformers where the model incrementally integrates information from multiple past positions, characterized by varying statistical significance and evolving from competitive to cooperative dynamics among attention heads.
A technique in spiking neural networks that involves training transmission delays in axons or dendrites, significantly improving performance on temporal tasks while reducing computational overhead.

S.term: Sparse Bayesian Functional Deep Neural Network
S.definition: A novel neural network architecture that integrates sparse Bayesian methods with deep learning to analyze complex functional data, allowing for adaptive region selection and improved interpretability.

A design approach for precoding codebooks that utilizes sparse structures on the Grassmann manifold to achieve low complexity and power efficiency while maintaining performance comparable to optimal codebooks.

This problem involves finding a one-dimensional representation of a binary matrix that allows constant-time access to its entries, with NP-completeness established for certain configurations, highlighting challenges in efficient data storage.

S.term: Sparse Recovery with ℓ∞ Error Guarantees
S.definition: A variant of sparse recovery focused on estimating the support of k-sparse signals in high-dimensional spaces, providing a provable separation between oblivious and adaptive models.

Sparse Spike Encoding is a technique that utilizes spiking neural networks to process information as sparse binary spike trains, significantly reducing energy consumption in human activity recognition tasks.

An algorithm developed for channel estimation in beyond diagonal reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (BD-RIS), utilizing orthogonal matching pursuit for greedy support recovery in compressive sensing frameworks.
A class of bimatrix games characterized by constant sparsity, where computing an ε-approximate Nash equilibrium is proven to be PPAD-hard, particularly for 3-sparse games.
A technique used in financial bond similarity search that enhances the predictability of spread curves by embedding categorical attributes, outperforming traditional methods like one-hot encoding.
A scalable framework for automatically generating large-scale benchmarks for Table-Text question answering tasks, designed to facilitate complex reasoning across multiple data sources.
A framework that enables machines to interpret and reason about dynamic auditory scenes by inferring object motion and positional changes from stereo audio inputs.

S.term: Spatial Autoregressive Modeling of DINOv3 Embeddings
S.definition: This approach employs spatial autoregressive modeling on DINOv3 embeddings to enhance unsupervised anomaly detection by capturing spatial relationships between patch embeddings, addressing limitations of existing methods.

Spatial dataflow architectures are designed to enhance computational efficiency by managing data movement explicitly within the chip, minimizing reliance on global shared memory and improving throughput.

Spatial-Temporal Workload Orchestration is a technique for optimizing the serving of large language models by addressing the heterogeneous nature of incoming workloads, allowing for better resource allocation and performance.

This technique integrates data-driven information into convolutional dictionary regularization, allowing for improved interpretability and robustness in image reconstruction methods through learned sparsity maps.
A cognitive benchmark designed to evaluate spatial understanding in large language models by isolating genuine spatial reasoning from statistical language heuristics.

S.term: Spatiotemporal Feature Alignment and Weighted Fusion Framework
S.definition: A framework that addresses the challenges of collaborative perception in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) by aligning features across vehicles in a spatiotemporal context and applying weighted fusion to improve decision-making.

A vulnerability in speaker recognition systems where score-based impersonation attacks exploit latent-space optimization within generative models, allowing attackers to impersonate speakers with improved efficiency compared to traditional methods.
A data augmentation strategy that transforms single-speaker recordings into realistic multi-speaker dialogues, enhancing automatic speech recognition for conversational speech.
SInMT is a framework that integrates multi-task learning for joint speaker recognition and spoofing detection, addressing the influence of speaker identity on detection systems.

SpecDef is an algorithm designed to control convergence rates in open-weight foundation models by leveraging spectral reparameterization, providing both theoretical and empirical guarantees for slowing optimization in non-adversarial settings.
An extension of evolutionary prompt search that maintains multiple high-toxicity prompt niches in parallel, allowing for a diverse exploration of distinct failure modes in large language models.

SpecIBT is a formally verified defense mechanism against speculative control-flow hijacking, combining hardware-assisted control-flow integrity with compiler-inserted speculative load hardening to enhance security against Spectre vulnerabilities.
A technique aimed at improving fine-grained open-world classification by promoting specificity in predictions without sacrificing overall correctness, leveraging intrinsic domain knowledge of models.
An agentic framework that generates specifications from RTL designs with a formal verification feedback loop, improving the accuracy and consistency of design documentation.

An agentic framework designed for generating specifications from RTL (Register Transfer Level) implementations, featuring a formal verification feedback loop to ensure design intent is accurately captured.
An interactive framework for generating program specifications, utilizing multi-turn prompting to iteratively refine postconditions and improve accuracy in specification generation.

The spectral condition for maximal update parameterization (μP) under joint width-depth scaling provides a unified framework for stable feature learning and hyperparameter transfer across generative foundation models.

A framework grounded in Random Matrix Theory (RMT) and Heavy-Tailed Self-Regularization (HTSR) designed to evaluate crash classification models beyond traditional accuracy metrics, revealing overfitting tendencies.
A framework for ensuring privacy in generative models by focusing on low-frequency components of images, thereby mitigating the risk of memorizing sensitive training examples.
A training-free method for attention steering that modifies key embeddings using spectral decomposition, enabling efficient prompt highlighting without the need for full attention matrix storage.

A loss-level framework that enhances diffusion model training by incorporating differentiable Fourier- and wavelet-domain losses, promoting frequency balance and multi-scale structure in generated outputs.
SSA is a universal framework for multispectral and hyperspectral image fusion that allows models to adapt to arbitrary spectral channels and spatial scales.

SpectroFusion-ViT is a lightweight framework for speech emotion recognition that utilizes a compact Vision Transformer architecture to balance accuracy and efficiency, particularly for low-resource languages.

A four-stage pipeline that combines Connectionist Temporal Classification forced alignment with spectral boundary refinement to improve phonetic alignment in audio language models, addressing challenges in tokenization.
A lightweight framework for reference-free web phishing detection that utilizes hierarchical graph autoencoding to improve detection accuracy without relying on external knowledge bases.
A framework for evaluating the quality of speculative design through an information-theoretic lens, focusing on the generation of knowledge and the strategic use of provotypes.

An advanced decoding technique that parallelizes the operations of speculative decoding and verification, allowing for faster inference in autoregressive models by predicting likely outcomes during verification.
Speculative tool calls are a novel optimization technique for language models that anticipates the need for external tool interactions during inference, aiming to reduce performance bottlenecks.
A diffusion autoencoder designed for speech tokenization that balances semantic understanding and acoustic reconstruction, achieving high fidelity with low bit and token rates.
A model that integrates speech recognition and speech synthesis to perform effective speech-to-speech style transfer for voice impersonation, generating realistic audio samples.
SpeechMMLU is a benchmark designed to evaluate the performance of end-to-end speech-language models, focusing on bridging the gap between acoustic signals and linguistic understanding.

A deep learning model designed for predicting scanpaths in visual attention studies, utilizing a Fully Convolutional Neural Network with differentiable fixation selection and unsupervised domain adaptation.

A derivative-augmented lookup table representation for spherical functions that improves the efficiency of real-time applications by addressing the quality-performance trade-off in existing sampling methods.

The Spherical Latent Motion Prior (SLMP) is a two-stage method for learning motion priors in physics-based humanoid control, designed to overcome limitations of existing approaches such as variational autoencoders and adversarial motion priors.

A fundamental concept in non-archimedean functional analysis, referring to spaces where every bounded sequence has a limit, crucial for understanding the structure of p-adic numbers and their applications.

The Spiking Bayesian Neural Network (SBNN) framework integrates stochastic threshold neurons and device variability to leverage noise as a functional resource, achieving high accuracy in neuromorphic computing tasks.

A type of neural network that more closely mimics biological neural networks by using spikes for communication, offering advantages in energy efficiency and computational requirements for applications like Alzheimer's disease detection.

S.term: Spiking Neural Networks Mapping on Neuromorphic Hardware
S.definition: A framework addressing the NP-hard problem of mapping spiking neural networks onto neuromorphic hardware, focusing on efficient partitioning and placement of neurons.

A miniaturized neuromorphic visuotactile sensor designed for high-precision dynamic tactile imprint tracking, utilizing a custom event camera module and an unsupervised denoising network for enhanced tracking performance.
A new matrix parameter that combines blocky rank's combinatorial structure with linear-algebraic flexibility, enhancing the analysis of matrices with both combinatorial and algebraic characteristics.
A learned sparse retrieval model that produces generalizable sparse latent representations, specifically designed for multilingual retrieval tasks in cross-lingual settings.
A GPU interprocess communication framework designed for Gaussian splatting, enabling real-time rendering of radiance fields with high fidelity and complex lighting effects.

A federated learning technique that divides a model into lightweight client-side and compute-intensive server-side components, enabling efficient training while preserving data privacy on edge devices.

A distributed machine learning approach that allows clients to offload part of their training to more powerful helpers, focusing on minimizing training time through optimal client-helper assignments and task scheduling.

A novel architecture for internet routers that spatially divides incoming fiber connections and distributes them across smaller independent switches without intermediate optical-electrical-optical (OEO) conversion, aiming to achieve high data throughput.
A framework for panoptic scene reconstruction that utilizes 3D Gaussian Splatting to model object instances explicitly, enabling view-consistent 2D masks and refined object boundaries.

SplitLight is an open-source toolkit designed for the exploratory analysis of recommender systems datasets, enabling researchers to make data preparation decisions measurable and comparable, thus improving reproducibility.
A compiler-based defense mechanism designed to prevent data memory-dependent prefetcher side-channels, enhancing security against vulnerabilities in memory access patterns.

Spoof-SUPERB is a benchmark for audio deepfake detection that systematically evaluates self-supervised learning models across various architectures, providing a standardized framework for assessing their effectiveness in security-critical applications.
A benchmark specifically designed for evaluating self-supervised learning models in the context of audio deepfake detection, facilitating fair comparisons across various architectures and datasets.
SPPAM stands for Signature Pattern Prediction and Access-Map Prefetcher, a cache prefetching technique that predicts future memory accesses to improve system performance.

A fine-tuning framework that enforces cross-frame consistency losses to learn pose-invariant rigs from mesh sequences, addressing the limitations of traditional rigging methods that rely on a canonical rest pose.

Spritz is a flexible sender-based load balancing framework designed for low-diameter networks, utilizing standard Ethernet features to enable adaptive topology-aware routing and efficient path exploration.

SQLAgent is a two-stage framework leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) for SQL query generation, separating knowledge acquisition from query generation to improve performance in complex database environments.
A linear-time PEG packrat parser that efficiently handles left recursion and provides optimal error recovery, utilizing cycle detection and state tracking for performance.
A framework designed to mitigate poisoning attacks in privacy-preserving federated learning by addressing the challenges posed by heterogeneous data and compromised clients.

An interactive web platform that transforms sustainability disclosure standards into auditable knowledge graphs using a large language model-centered pipeline, integrating automatic standard identification and robust triple parsing.
A method for audio-visual separation of speech and background noise using generative inverse sampling, leveraging dedicated diffusion priors for unsupervised recovery of underlying sources.
A framework for generative spatio-temporal evolution in multi-agent communication topologies, allowing agents to adapt workflows dynamically rather than relying on static structures.

SDA is a minimal, non-equilibrium framework for modeling evolutionary dynamics that generates endogenous selection through stochastic interactions and differential persistence, without relying on genes or predefined fitness functions.

Stabilized Adaptive Loss is a technique for improving the training of Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) by addressing issues of unbalanced training and inaccuracies in solutions for stiff problems.

A concept in coalition formation that focuses on finding stable partitions of agents based on their preferences, particularly in scenarios where agents can change their valuations, minimizing reconfiguration costs.

A theoretical framework suggesting that biological evolution operates through multi-layered self-encoding and decoding processes, akin to stacked autoencoders, enabling exploration of diverse evolutionary possibilities.
A technique in environmental sound classification that aggregates complementary acoustic descriptors into richer input representations, enhancing the performance of CNN models.

Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces (SIM) are an innovative architecture comprising multiple programmable metasurface layers that enhance beamforming capabilities in wireless systems, enabling efficient multi-band transmission.

SIMs are a novel enhancement for cell-free massive MIMO systems, aimed at improving simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) performance by optimizing access point operating modes.
A framework for analyzing Gaussian signaling games under linear sensitivity mismatch, providing insights into equilibrium structures in both noiseless and noisy signaling scenarios.
An economic model that analyzes the interaction between liability sharing and staffing levels in AI-assisted online medical consultations, influencing service quality and physician behavior.

A unified audio codec that integrates semantic information from self-supervised learning models into its architecture, addressing the balance between acoustic fidelity and semantic information in audio compression.
An innovative approach to floating-point satisfiability that integrates SMT solving with numerical optimization through a three-stage process of increasing precision.

An econometric technique that evaluates the impact of interventions by comparing changes over time between a treatment group and a control group, accounting for staggered implementation across different regions.
A framework for white blood cell classification that addresses appearance variations in datasets by utilizing instance-balanced sampling and decoupled training strategies.
An automated verification tool that extends support for floating-point numbers in Scala, enabling the verification of mathematical functions and their correctness against actual implementations.
A framework that models cost overruns in nuclear power plant construction by analyzing the roles and impacts of key stakeholders, revealing discrepancies in cost allocation.

The STAR method is a post-processing technique for selecting a representative community partition in complex networks, addressing issues of modularity degeneracy that lead to instability and interpretability challenges in community detection.
A resource management framework designed for STAR-RIS-assisted augmented reality systems in the Metaverse, optimizing communication efficiency between users and servers.

An emerging architecture alternative to transformers for large language models, which analyzes expressiveness through fragments and extensions of linear temporal logic. The performance of SSMs varies significantly based on their underlying gating mechanisms.
A method for recovering impulse responses in nonlinear environments, which captures distinct aspects of nonlinearity by interacting shocks with their signs and lagged states.
A Sparse Transition Matrix-Accelerated Trie Index designed for efficient constrained decoding in LLM-based generative retrieval, optimizing performance on hardware accelerators.
A compiler-integrated approach that reduces the overhead of dynamic data race detection by eliminating redundant instrumentation of memory accesses through static analysis.
A system for real-time facial avatar animation that predicts blendshape feature vectors using statistical methods, optimized for low-power VR applications.
An approach for assessing the functional correctness of AI systems by connecting business requirements to a measure of statistical confidence that accounts for model performance and variability.

A framework for analyzing the complexity of smoothed agnostic learning, focusing on learning halfspaces under Gaussian perturbations. The results provide insights into the limitations of learning algorithms in the presence of noise.

S.term: Steepest Descent Method in Function Space
S.definition: An optimization technique applied to shape optimization problems involving semilinear elliptic partial differential equations, demonstrating global convergence in the Lipschitz topology.
A hierarchical benchmark for evaluating the controllability of large language models across various behavioral domains, structured to connect high-level intent with concrete textual outputs.
A privacy-preserving framework for face forgery detection that employs fine-grained steganographic techniques to protect facial data during transmission, mitigating risks of data interception.

S.term: Steiner Forest Algorithm for H-Subgraph-Free Graphs
S.definition: A classification of the computational complexity of the Steiner Forest problem on graphs that do not contain a specific subgraph H. The algorithm identifies two new classes of graph structures that allow for polynomial-time solutions.
A classification of the computational complexity of the Steiner Forest problem on H-subgraph-free graphs, identifying new classes of graph structures that allow polynomial time solutions.

STELLAR is an autonomous storage tuning engine designed for high-performance parallel file systems that leverages large language model (LLM) reasoning to optimize parameter configurations efficiently, often achieving near-optimal settings within just a few attempts.

An innovative audio container format that enables efficient lossless audio storage by encoding music as independently encoded stems, achieving significant file size reduction while maintaining adaptability.
Stemphonic is a diffusion-/flow-based framework for flexible multi-stem music generation, allowing for the simultaneous production of musically-synchronized and isolated instrument audio clips.

An open-source vision-language-action model that incorporates future 3D spatial geometry and 4D historical representation to enhance spatial reasoning and decision-making in robotic manipulation tasks.
An analytical tool designed to interpret reasoning processes in Large Language Models by capturing critical step-level information, such as reasoning direction and semantic transitions.

A structure-texture guided pruning method for Visual AutoRegressive models that optimizes computational efficiency by focusing on the structural coherence of earlier scales while pruning high-frequency textures in later scales.
Stereo-Inertial Poser is a motion capture system that combines stereo cameras with sparse IMUs to achieve metric-accurate motion capture while addressing issues of occlusion and drift.
StethoLM is an audio-language model specifically designed for cardiopulmonary auscultation, enabling instruction-driven clinical tasks and improving interpretability in sound analysis.

An automated multi-agent framework for end-to-end GPU program generation that employs specialized agents to optimize both GPU kernel efficiency and host-side settings, enhancing overall performance in ML workloads.
A fuzzing technique that dynamically assembles API usage constraints at runtime, allowing for more expressive and scalable bug detection in software libraries.
A toolbox for generating Stochastic Barrier Functions for safety verification of discrete-time stochastic systems, utilizing Sum-of-Squares optimization techniques.

An analysis framework that examines the interaction between differential privacy noise and adaptivity in optimization, providing insights into the convergence behavior of private optimizers like DP-SGD and DP-SignSGD.

The Stochastic Discount Factor is a unified framework that links predictive signals and cross-asset spillovers, maximizing the Sharpe ratio to provide an interpretable measure of asset relevance and predictive influence across different markets.

A framework that connects firm-level predictive signals and cross-asset spillovers through the stochastic discount factor, enhancing investment decision-making by identifying predictive influences across assets.

A theoretical framework that models systems as stochastic processes governed by differential equations, incorporating multiplicative noise and cross-subsystem interactions to analyze sustainability dynamics.
A computational problem focused on minimizing expected total weighted completion times for stochastic jobs on parallel machines, with significant challenges in achieving optimal solutions.

A mathematical formulation used to optimize the location of charging stations for electric trucks under uncertainty, maximizing covered electric freight flow while considering demand and grid capacity.

A method for modeling tactic proposals in formal theorem proving as a stochastic policy within a finite-horizon deterministic Markov Decision Process (MDP), allowing for improved representation learning in interactive proof assistants.
A network design problem that incorporates uncertainty, extending traditional Steiner Tree Problem heuristics to address stochastic optimization challenges.

A class of Hamiltonians for which ground state preparation is efficiently solvable on quantum computers, yet poses challenges for classical algorithms, highlighting the limitations of classical sampling methods.
A computational model used for data compression and algebraic complexity, where computations are represented as sequences of operations, enabling efficient solutions for algebraic equations.
A framework for analyzing the complexity of tensors, proven to coincide with quantum functionals, thus resolving a long-standing open problem in the field.

A fully prospective prediction tournament framework that evaluates the strategic foresight capabilities of AI models using real-time crowdfunding projects, allowing for the assessment of AI versus human judgment in uncertain environments.

A concept introduced to enhance the generalizability of collaborative agents in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning by incorporating a principled inductive bias that promotes cooperation with unseen partners.
An approach in reinforcement learning that leverages the planning and reasoning capabilities of large language models to enhance exploration in language-action spaces.

StraTyper is an automated semantic type discovery system that annotates columns of tabular datasets with semantic types using pre-trained models, overcoming limitations of existing methods that require predefined labels.

Stream functions are a novel extension of the Function-as-a-Service model designed to efficiently handle short-running, lightweight, and unpredictable streams requiring stateful processing, utilizing an iterator-based interface for seamless inter-event logic.
An execution paradigm for neural networks designed to handle irreversible input streams, enabling epoch-free learning and persistent temporal state management.

StreamLoD-GS is a Level-of-Detail (LoD) based Gaussian Splatting framework designed for Streaming Free-Viewpoint Video (SFVV) reconstruction, addressing challenges related to rapid optimization and high-fidelity reconstruction under sparse constraints.

StreamShield is a resiliency solution for Apache Flink that ensures operational stability and rapid recovery from failures in large-scale distributed stream processing systems, addressing challenges in production environments.
Stress Response Strategies are frameworks used to evaluate how cities respond to crises, focusing on their adaptive capacities and urban development trajectories.

Stretto is an execution engine for LLM-augmented data systems that formulates query planning as a constrained optimization problem, balancing runtime efficiency and accuracy across data processing pipelines.

A measure of the compressibility of a string, defined as the minimal length of a compressed representation of the string. It is used to analyze the efficiency of data structures and algorithms in terms of space usage.
A library that facilitates reasoning about monoidal categories using string diagrams, providing tools for visualizing and editing these diagrams to streamline formal proof processes.

A framework that utilizes the string method to explore the geometry of learned distributions in diffusion models by computing continuous paths between samples, enhancing the understanding of their latent space.

A set of positions in a string that ensures local consistency by selecting at least one position in every length-τ window, which is crucial for efficient string processing applications like data compression and text indexing.
A problem in the second level of the total function polynomial hierarchy (TFΣ2) that has been shown to have natural existence principles, with implications for computational complexity.

An extension of the Maximum Independent Set problem to hypergraphs, where edges can connect multiple vertices, focusing on finding strongly independent sets and introducing new data reduction rules for efficient solving.
Strong variational sufficiency is a sufficient condition for local optimality in optimization problems, ensuring stability of optimal values and solutions under parameter shifts.
A newly defined class of functions that extends the concept of invexity, providing a framework for analyzing optimization problems with interval-valued functions.

Structural Bias (SB) in multi-objective optimization refers to systematic preferences of an optimization algorithm for specific regions of the search space, which can affect dominance relations and diversity preservation.
A method for multi-view multi-label feature selection that leverages structural entropy to learn high-order correlations in data, addressing challenges in convergence and local optima.
A statistical technique that validates hierarchical pathways among multiple factors by integrating latent variable measurement and path analysis, often used in consumer behavior studies.

A concept introduced to describe the long-term alterations in a neural model's base behavior due to shared-parameter adaptation, indicating that task-specific objectives can intertwine with the model's representational identity.
This memory mechanism enhances large language models by enabling long-horizon reasoning through a finer granularity of memory storage and retrieval, addressing the mismatch in instance-level memory.
A variant of gradient descent designed to optimize softmax self-attention parameters efficiently by leveraging the equivalence of regression problems to nonconvex matrix factorization.

An approach for symbolic piano accompaniment that utilizes a lightweight transformer for high-level style planning and a retrieval mechanism for note-level realization based on harmonic and structural compatibility.
A method for recognizing text in complex layouts of historical documents, specifically designed for Ancient Greek critical editions, utilizing a large-scale synthetic corpus for training.
A triangulation framework that integrates raster or segmentation-derived boundaries into a triangular grid for stable PDE discretization, optimizing for parallel execution and determinism.

Structured human data refers to the persistent production input of human evaluation, judgment, and auditing that enhances model reliability and expands the range of tasks AI can perform, challenging the notion of a future where automation is complete.
A declarative SQL-inspired language designed for managing context in LLMs, featuring token management, automatic query optimization, and integration of retrieval-augmented generation.

A framework for controllable generation of dynamic interactive content that encodes user input and environmental conditions into a structured semantic state space, enabling coherent and stable content creation.

StyleStream is a real-time zero-shot voice style conversion system that separates linguistic content from style attributes, enabling high-quality voice transformation without prior training on specific styles.
Non-autoregressive architectures evaluated for their effectiveness in zero-shot text-to-speech synthesis, focusing on spontaneous speech and enhanced audio prompts for improved prosodic naturalness.
Innovative methods for integrating teleportation functionality into stylus interactions in virtual reality, enhancing user experience by allowing intuitive mode switching and gaze-based orientation.

An adaptively secure committee-based method for common coin tossing in asynchronous networks, designed to mitigate the influence of Byzantine parties while reducing the cost of generating random values.

Subgradient projections are used in convex optimization to solve the sum-rate maximization problem in wireless networks by projecting onto nonlinear spectral radius constraint sets, facilitating the handling of complex optimization landscapes.

A phenomenon where language models exhibit bias towards specific concepts through the introduction of semantically unrelated tokens in prompts, potentially leading to bias transfer in multi-agent systems.

An optimization problem focused on maximizing a submodular function while adhering to constraints imposed by a supermodular cost function, which presents unique challenges compared to traditional constraints.

The problem of maximizing a submodular function while adhering to supermodular cost constraints, which presents unique challenges compared to traditional constraints like cardinality. This area remains less understood and is significant for optimization in data mining.

A technique that adapts pre-trained neural network models to new dynamical systems with limited data by using a modified version of the Extended Kalman Filter. It allows for effective model transfer while requiring minimal training data.

A Reinforcement Learning environment framework that models system attack paths by simulating the sequence of processes executed on a Windows operating system, facilitating dynamic analysis of malicious processes.
A method used to solve non-convex optimization problems in integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems, maximizing the system secrecy rate under various constraints.
An algorithmic approach that constrains the output of large language models to follow specific suffix templates, ensuring the generated responses are easily parseable.
A unified framework for simplifying retrieval benchmarks, providing automatic end-to-end evaluation and dynamic indexing to enhance reproducibility and comparability in information retrieval tasks.
A task framework for complex autonomous research that integrates structured decomposition, wide retrieval for diverse perspectives, and iterative queries to resolve uncertainties.
An interdisciplinary approach that applies the generalized transfer operator of dynamical systems theory to stochastic differential equations, integrating concepts from algebraic topology.

A novel paradigm for enhancing reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs) that optimizes reasoning while preserving previously learned knowledge, utilizing the implicit regularization from Direct Preference Optimization.
A novel metric developed to quantify marriage market imbalance under monogamy, incorporating effective fertility rates adjusted for early-life mortality to reflect actual cohort progression.

An ensemble framework that utilizes reinforcement learning to dynamically select and schedule surrogate models during optimization processes, enhancing performance in expensive multi-objective problems.

Surrogate-assisted Neuroevolution is an evolutionary framework that employs surrogate models to optimize complex control problems in water distribution systems, enhancing the management of disinfectant levels.
A training approach for spiking neural networks that avoids surrogate gradients while maintaining temporal precision, addressing challenges in low-latency responsiveness.
A design lens focused on creating technologies that foster children's long-term engagement with social issues, aiming to transform anxiety into meaningful civic action.

A focus on the environmental impact of code generated by large language models, emphasizing the importance of generating efficient code to minimize energy consumption and resource usage during deployment.
A data-driven framework for sparse sound field reconstruction using singular value decomposition (SVD) to enhance spatial selectivity in hybrid spherical-linear microphone arrays.
A novel attention mechanism that optimizes Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) for lifelong learning in recommendation systems, reducing computational complexity while maintaining performance.

SWE-ABS is an adversarial framework designed to strengthen test suites through a two-stage pipeline, which includes coverage-driven augmentation using program slicing and mutation-driven adversarial testing to expose weaknesses in software patches.
SWE-Adept is an LLM-based framework that utilizes two agents for deep codebase analysis, focusing on issue localization and systematic test-driven code modification.
SWE-Hub is a unified production system that addresses the challenges of training and evaluating software engineering agents by providing executable, scalable, and realistic data.

A post-training framework that reframes software repair as an expert-protégé collaboration problem, enabling small language models to selectively seek guidance from a stronger expert model during decision-making.

SWE-rebench V2 is a language-agnostic collection of software engineering tasks designed for reinforcement learning, providing a scalable and reproducible environment for training software engineering agents.

A fast and general framework for depth-guided 3D reconstruction that utilizes a Neural Depth Correction field to produce cross-view consistent depth maps, improving the quality of generated point clouds.

The Switch-Matrix Benchmark evaluates performance drift in multi-turn LLM systems by measuring the effects of model switching during interactions, providing insights into context mismatch and model reliability.
Techniques developed to reduce the tendency of large language models to provide user-affirming responses, enhancing critical engagement in conversational AI.
This design approach focuses on ensuring transmitter anonymity at the physical layer by utilizing physical-layer characteristics to prevent the receiver from inferring the transmitter's identity.

An architecture that enforces permutation equivariance at the architectural level, enabling neural networks to solve reasoning problems like Sudoku more effectively by ensuring consistent solutions under symbol permutations.

A new architecture that enforces permutation equivariance at the architectural level, improving the performance of neural networks on structured reasoning tasks by ensuring identical solutions under symbol symmetries.
A novel Transformer architecture designed to handle interchangeable tokens in input data, ensuring that models can generalize to unseen symbols while maintaining semantic integrity.
Techniques developed for CF-GKAT that leverage on-the-fly symbolic methods via SAT solvers to improve decision-making processes in automata.

Symbolic Functional Decomposition is a method of breaking down complex functions into simpler components through functional reconfiguration, applicable in various fields including machine learning and formal language theory.
A novel symbolic method for encoding Petri net markings using generalized intervals on vectors, aimed at addressing the state space explosion problem in model checking.
An extension of Reward Machines that allows for the representation and learning of non-Markovian rewards in reinforcement learning without requiring manual labeling for each environment.

This approach introduces a formal theory for symbolic specification and reasoning about quantum data and operations, aiming to enhance the scalability and efficiency of automated verification tools in quantum computing.

A protocol enabling a user to retrieve a message from multiple servers without revealing the message index to any individual server, ensuring both user and database privacy in graph-replicated systems.
A framework that reformulates transformer representations and attention mechanisms to operate on invariant relational quantities, reducing internal redundancy and improving efficiency.
Sym-HGNN is a graph neural network that incorporates the dihedral symmetry of tensegrity robots into its message-passing process to improve contact state estimation from proprioceptive measurements.

A biologically inspired protection system for microgrids that utilizes neuromorphic principles, where distributed energy resources act as neurons to process local disturbances without requiring continuous communication.

A method for consistent, parametric relighting across multiple uncalibrated views of a static scene, enabling precise control over light intensity and color based on a single reference edit using a multi-view diffusion transformer.

SyncTrack is a synchronous multi-track waveform music generation model that captures the unique characteristics of multi-track music by establishing a common rhythm across tracks through track-shared modules.
A concept advocating for the understanding of security properties in distributed systems not in isolation but as synergistic combinations that enhance overall system trustworthiness.
A hybrid analytical-ML framework for predicting GPU performance, addressing the need for accurate models that generalize across diverse hardware and complex workloads.

SyntAGM is an end-to-end system that translates natural language problem descriptions into PyOPL models, utilizing a generate-compile-assess-revise loop and grammar awareness through exposure to the PyOPL BNF grammar.

SynthCharge is an electric vehicle routing instance generator that produces feasibility-screened instances of the electric vehicle routing problem with time windows, enabling learning-based optimization and benchmarking.
An unsupervised training framework for hyperspectral single image super-resolution that utilizes synthetic abundance data to enhance spatial resolution without requiring ground truth data.
A secure beamforming technique for virtual MIMO broadcast channels that maximizes channel capacity while mitigating the impact of hybrid wiretapping environments.
A statistical technique used to construct counterfactuals for comparative analysis, particularly effective in evaluating the effects of interventions by comparing treated and control groups.

A method that utilizes synthetic data to improve multi-hop reasoning capabilities in large language models, addressing the challenges posed by traditional data sources that are often limited or prone to errors.
A technique that leverages synthetic data to enhance product retrieval capabilities for long-tail knowledge-intensive queries in e-commerce, addressing challenges in query diversity and intent.
A causal inference technique that combines synthetic control methods with difference-in-differences to estimate treatment effects in observational studies.
A framework for generating synthetic financial data using methods like ARIMA-GARCH, VAEs, and TimeGAN, evaluated for fidelity and temporal structure.

S.term: Synthetic Generator for Volatility Law Manifolds
S.definition: A tool used to create synthetic data for training reinforcement learning agents on volatility surfaces, aiding in the exploration of no-arbitrage laws in financial modeling.

An adversarially-curated mini-internet framework designed to diagnose the epistemic weaknesses of language agents by simulating unreliable content sources and evaluating their robustness against adversarial ranking.

Synthetic-Child is an AIGC-based synthetic data pipeline that generates photorealistic training images for child posture estimation, eliminating the need for real photographs and ensuring privacy compliance.
A pipeline for single-view 3D reconstruction of ships that leverages synthetic data for training, requiring only a single view during inference to facilitate real-time maritime monitoring.
An initiative to port a syscall interception library to the RISC-V architecture, facilitating the development of a functional software ecosystem for RISC-V processors.
A framework for analyzing complex systems and their dynamics over time, particularly useful for understanding policy and resource interactions in sectors like construction in developing countries.
A method for comparing continuous-time and discrete-time linear systems by determining if continuous trajectories can be realized from discrete inputs.

S.term: System-Level Isolation for Mixed-Criticality RISC-V SoCs
S.definition: A framework addressing the safety and security challenges of integrating heterogeneous RISC-V systems-on-chip, ensuring system-level isolation for applications with varying integrity levels.

System-level threat monitoring is a proactive approach to managing the security risks associated with LLM-enabled applications, addressing the expanded attack surface and reliability challenges introduced by non-deterministic AI behavior.

A framework that identifies and analyzes the unique attack surfaces introduced by deploying large language model agents on IoT hardware, highlighting emergent failures such as coordination-state divergence.

N.definition: The n-sided PL condition is a set of criteria that extends the Polyak-Łojasiewicz condition for finding Nash equilibria in nonconvex general-sum games. It facilitates the use of first-order gradient-based algorithms like block coordinate descent in these complex game scenarios.

A lossless compression system that integrates a transformer language model with ensemble context modeling and high-precision CDF coding, achieving superior compression results for natural language text.
A small-scale multitask multimodal language model that unifies emotional intelligence tasks through a cognitively inspired three-level hierarchy, bridging the gap between perception and interaction.
A benchmark dataset designed to partition questions based on the presence of answers in pre-training data, facilitating the understanding of knowledge encoding in large language models.
A hierarchical framework for synthesizing soundtracks for long-form videos, utilizing emotion as a high-density compression of narrative logic.

A framework that integrates qualitative content analysis principles for annotating narratives in a structured manner, reducing errors by representing events as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) with causal relations.

NaSh is a natural language shell designed to leverage large language models while incorporating guardrails to help users recover from unintended outputs, aiming to enhance usability and safety in command-line interfaces.
A model for coalition formation among agents that allows for overlapping coalitions and operates under partial information, enhancing strategic collaboration in multi-agent systems.
A class of dynamics in game theory that converge to Nash equilibria, with implications for the complexity of computation in nondegenerate games.

The Nash-Sutcliffe loss is a negatively oriented counterpart to the Nash-Sutcliffe efficiency, defined as L_NS = 1 - NSE, providing a consistent measure for evaluating forecasts across multiple time series.
An estimate of the economic impact of carbon emissions on society, which varies with per capita income and population size, highlighting the net liabilities of carbon-intensive countries.

N.term: Natural Motion Trajectory for AMO Satellites
N.definition: A novel approach for station keeping of Areostationary Mars Orbit (AMO) satellites that employs a fuel-free natural motion trajectory, enhancing fuel and computational efficiency.

Natural privacy filters enable the exact composition of differentially private mechanisms with adaptively chosen privacy characteristics, accounting for the entire privacy profile of every query to enhance utility for a given privacy budget.

NE-Dreamer is a decoder-free model-based reinforcement learning agent that utilizes a temporal transformer to predict next-step encoder embeddings, optimizing temporal predictive alignment in representation space without requiring reconstruction losses.
An OS-centric abstraction for Near-Data Processing that facilitates the integration of disaggregated memory systems, aiming to overcome barriers to the adoption of this technology.

A concept in stable matching theory that allows for slight modifications of agent capacities to ensure desirable outcomes in matching settings, particularly when traditional stable matchings are not available.
A system that employs UAV swarms to create a movable antenna capable of near-field communication, utilizing a non-uniform spherical wave model to enhance aerial-to-ground link performance.

N.term: Near-Field Dictionary Design for Hybrid MIMO
N.definition: A novel approach for channel estimation in near-field ultra-massive MIMO systems that utilizes a tailored grid design, optimizing measurement reconstruction for ground-located user equipment.

An algorithm that constructs suffix trees in near-real-time with double logarithmic worst-case guarantees on update time per letter, enabling efficient computation of string problems such as longest repeating suffix arrays and minimal unique substrings.
A computational problem in election theory that determines if a nominated candidate can win against all possible nominations from other parties under various voting rules.
An extension of spectrum-preserving string sets that utilizes branching structures to efficiently represent strings with matching k-mer spectra, enhancing storage and traversal in genomics.
The first fuzzing framework specifically designed for nested virtualization, which systematically targets the complexities and attack surfaces introduced by nested hypervisors.
A class of Boolean functions characterized by a flat magnitude spectrum under the nega-Hadamard transform, with applications in cryptographic design due to their optimal spectral properties.
An optimization problem that involves assigning labels to vertices in a graph to maximize local rewards based on the closed neighborhood of each vertex.

NeighborMAE is a self-supervised learning framework that exploits spatial dependencies between neighboring Earth Observation images during Masked Image Modeling, enhancing representation learning by leveraging rich contextual information.
A multi-agent framework designed to convert neural radiance field (NeRF) research papers into executable code for Nerfstudio plugins, utilizing context-free grammar to ensure code correctness.
NESSiE, the NEceSsary SafEty benchmark, is a lightweight evaluation tool for large language models that identifies safety-relevant failures, serving as a necessary condition for model deployment.

N.term: Nested Named Entity Recognition from Flat Annotations
N.definition: A method for learning nested named entity recognition using flat annotations, employing techniques such as string inclusions and entity corruption to enhance model performance despite limited nested resources.
A training methodology for human-AI teaming that allows agents to adaptively learn from each other, improving their ability to generalize across different contexts.
A collision-finding problem that demonstrates the superiority of adaptive queries over non-adaptive ones, revealing complexities within the PWPP class and its implications for Turing reductions.
A framework for dynamic management of cache and backend storage devices in networked environments, optimizing I/O performance based on real-time network feedback.
A comprehensive guide for optimizing the NetLogo platform to efficiently run large-scale agent-based models on cloud infrastructures, focusing on memory management and execution strategies.
An extension of manifold Ricci curvature concepts to discrete structures such as graphs and hypergraphs, applicable in various fields including social networks and deep learning models.

NeuEdge is a framework that integrates adaptive spiking neural networks with hardware-aware optimization techniques for efficient edge AI deployment, focusing on energy efficiency and low-latency inference.
A type of model that provides interpretable predictions by using a cardinally-minimal subset of input features, ensuring provable guarantees for explanations.

A research area focused on integrating algorithmic reasoning capabilities into neural architectures, particularly using graph neural networks to enhance their ability to learn and execute discrete algorithms.
Neural Audio Codecs are designed for compressing audio for storage and transmission, and are utilized in spoof detection by resynthesizing waveforms to simulate attacks in text-to-speech systems.

A flexible neural system for estimating budget shares in continuous allocation problems, incorporating state-dependent preferences and habit formation through an exponentially weighted moving average.

A flexible neural network architecture designed for estimating budget shares in continuous budget allocation, utilizing KL divergence minimization and incorporating habit formation through state-dependent preferences.

A framework for modeling overdispersed point process data using neural stochastic differential equations, facilitating efficient posterior inference and intensity estimation without relying on traditional MCMC methods.
A technique for reconstructing high-resolution material parameters in digital twins by leveraging neural networks to solve ill-posed physical inversion problems related to electromagnetic fields.

A lightweight screen-space post-processing technique that simulates the visual effect of tessellated geometry while rendering only low-polygon meshes by leveraging neural networks for contour refinement.

A paradigm that reformulates action prediction in Vision-Language-Action models from discrete waypoints to continuous action function regression, addressing issues of rigid sampling rates and quantization artifacts.

N.term: Neural Implicit Representations for SAR Imaging
N.definition: A technique using neural networks to model surface scattering in synthetic aperture radar imaging, improving the quality of reconstructed 3D imagery from incomplete data.
A sample-efficient method used to estimate single-site conditional probabilities in discrete denoising diffusion processes, enhancing generative modeling over discrete state spaces.

A neural-based technique for real-time rendering of diffuse global illumination that utilizes a compact pre-computed model to overcome aliasing artifacts and high memory consumption associated with traditional irradiance volume methods.
A novel class of noise designed for neural stochastic differential equation frameworks, capable of capturing both long- and short-memory characteristics in time series data.

A hybrid framework that integrates neural networks with ordinary differential equations to model complex dynamical systems, such as antagonistic pneumatic artificial muscle dynamics, by embedding physical structure into the learning process.
A framework that generalizes Neural Cellular Automata to dynamic particle systems, allowing for continuous position updates and heterogeneous dynamics based on learnable neural rules.

N.term: Neural Quantum Support Vector Data Description (NQSVDD)
N.definition: NQSVDD is a classical-quantum hybrid framework for one-class classification that integrates a classical neural network with trainable quantum data encoding, enabling end-to-end optimized hierarchical representation learning.
Neural Routing Solvers leverage deep learning to address vehicle routing problems by learning implicit heuristic rules from data, thus reducing the reliance on traditional handcrafted heuristics.
Deep learning-based solvers for vehicle routing problems that learn implicit heuristic rules from data, reducing reliance on traditional handcrafted heuristics.

A unified Vision Language Model (VLM) for license plate recognition that integrates state classification and vehicle attribute extraction into a single forward pass, reducing latency and architectural complexity.
A neural network-based approach for computing Wasserstein geodesics between probability distributions, leveraging a minimax optimization framework.
A machine learning approach to formal reasoning that aims to automate the proof of Verification Conditions in program verification, addressing the limitations of existing Automated Theorem Provers.
A multi-view-based method for reconstructing high-fidelity 3D animal fur geometry using a strand-based representation, addressing challenges such as fine-scale details and view-dependent appearance.

A hybrid approach that combines data-driven methods with symbolic reasoning to enhance predictive maintenance systems, addressing limitations such as the need for large labeled datasets and lack of generalizability.

This pipeline integrates knowledge-graph embeddings, approximate nearest-neighbour search, and SPARQL-driven semantic filtering to provide advanced recommendations for cultural heritage knowledge graphs.
A proposed interdisciplinary field that integrates neuroscience insights into artificial intelligence development, focusing on areas such as embodiment, communication, and learning mechanisms.
