A bidirectional transformation technique that allows for managing updates across multiple views of a shared source state, ensuring correspondence and compositional integrity.

A novel waveform design for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems that combines linear frequency modulation (LFM) pulse signals with orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) for enhanced communication and sensing performance.

A method of implementing program transformations in functional logic programming by defining transformations that may not cover all cases, allowing for more compact and comprehensible implementations.
A technique for mining episode rules from event sequences that relaxes strict ordering constraints, allowing for unordered events in rule antecedents and consequents.
A design principle for community projects that emphasizes simple, visible routines to facilitate participation and collaboration among members.

An improved branch-and-bound strategy for enumerating maximal bicliques in bipartite graphs, achieving better worst-case time complexity than existing methods. This approach is particularly useful in applications like E-commerce and transaction networks.

The PARWiS algorithm determines a winner among items using active pairwise comparisons under budget constraints, employing spectral ranking and disruptive pair selection. It includes variants like Contextual PARWiS and RL PARWiS for enhanced performance.

PASAR, or Parametric-Sensitivity Aware Retransmission, is a framework designed to optimize AI model downloading in mobile networks by prioritizing the transmission of parameter packets based on their impact on inference accuracy.
A phase-aware scheduling algorithm designed to optimize the serving of reasoning-based Large Language Models by distinguishing between reasoning and answering phases to improve Time-To-First-Token.

PASTA (Program AnalysiS Tool Framework for Accelerators) is a modular framework that abstracts low-level profiling APIs and deep learning frameworks, providing a unified interface for runtime event analysis in heterogeneous computing environments.

Patchwork is a unified framework for Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) serving that addresses efficiency bottlenecks in deploying heterogeneous computational pipelines involving large language models and external knowledge sources.
An optimal-control-based particle filtering method for state estimation in hybrid systems, utilizing saltation matrices to manage uncertainty during contact events.

A linear programming relaxation for the Directed Steiner Tree problem in layered graphs, achieving an integrality gap of O(l log k). This method simplifies the approach to obtaining state-of-the-art bounds without complex hierarchy machinery.
A technique that leverages rescaling symmetries in ReLU neural networks to improve training dynamics by aligning kernel parameters through a geometrically motivated criterion.
A training strategy that leverages rescaling symmetries in ReLU neural networks, providing a geometrically motivated criterion to improve training dynamics and align kernel functions.

PathSpace is a method for efficient SLAM that utilizes B-Splines for rapid continuous map approximation in constrained environments, enhancing the semantic reasoning capabilities of autonomous vehicles.
A visualization tool that encodes the interaction dynamics between therapists and patients during rehabilitation therapy, supporting the development of a Synthetic Therapist model.
A technique for generating real-time video commentary that considers both the content and timing of commentary to enhance viewer engagement and accessibility.

PBLean is a method for importing pseudo-Boolean proof certificates into Lean 4, leveraging reflection to ensure soundness and scalability for large proofs. This integration allows for efficient verification of complex logical statements within the Lean theorem prover.

P.term: PC-LLM (Power Control using Large Language Models)
P.definition: PC-LLM repurposes pre-trained large language models to address power control in wireless networks, utilizing a physics-informed framework to enhance decision-making in high-interference environments.

P.term: PC-LLM (Power Control with Large Language Models)
P.definition: A physics-informed framework that repurposes pre-trained large language models for power control in wireless networks, utilizing an interference-aware attention bias to improve computational efficiency in high-interference scenarios.
An enhanced first-order solver for large-scale convex quadratic programming problems, incorporating algorithmic innovations like Halpern-type acceleration and adaptive updates.

PDP, or Prototype-guided Dual-pool Prompting, is a novel framework for Incremental Object Detection that decouples prompts into shared and task-specific pools to mitigate prompt degradation during continual learning.
A bug localization framework that reconstructs causal chains in sequential hardware designs, addressing challenges in timing misalignment and error propagation.

PedaCo-Gen is a human-AI collaborative video authoring system that incorporates pedagogical principles to enhance instructional video creation, featuring an Intermediate Representation phase for interactive refinement.
A novel framework for solving non-self-adjoint topology optimization problems by incorporating a convex nonlocal perimeter approximation scheme, ensuring solution consistency across scales.

A benchmark framework designed for evaluating the reasoning capabilities of large language models through a set of constraint-satisfaction problems known as pencil puzzles, which are closely related to NP-complete problems.
A novel objective function introduced to enhance the robustness of Autoencoders against anomalies by focusing on the distribution of loss values across samples.
A property of permutation polynomials that ensures optimal resistance to c-differential attacks, characterized using difference distribution tables.

The concept of a Perfect Hypervisor refers to a theoretical hypervisor that can replicate the behavior of bare metal execution without any observable overhead, which has been proven to be impossible in practical scenarios.
A set of isolation mechanisms, including NVIDIA's MPS, MIG, and Green Contexts, that ensure predictable inference times in safety-critical applications using deep learning models on edge GPU systems.
A method to optimize QUIC performance by enabling in-network assistance while maintaining end-to-end encryption, addressing the limitations of traditional TCP-based Performance-Enhancing Proxies.
A concept in which algorithmic predictions influence the data distributions they are trained on, leading to a feedback loop that can stabilize model performance in dynamic environments.
An online performance troubleshooting system designed for large-scale model training, utilizing fine-grained profiling to diagnose performance issues in production environments.
A methodology for allocating a specific percentage of battery-based solar systems within residential areas to mitigate power quality issues caused by distributed energy resources.
A decoding method that utilizes the permutation automorphism group of linear codes to correct errors in received messages, particularly effective for algebraic geometry codes.

This framework transforms representational similarity metrics into calibrated scores with statistical guarantees, addressing confounding effects due to network scale in neural network representations.

A multi-agent system designed for the discovery of perovskite materials, facilitating closed-loop workflows that integrate literature retrieval, data integration, experimental design, and synthesis while maintaining physical constraints.
A method in Topological Data Analysis (TDA) that studies the shape of data by analyzing the connectivity and features of data points across multiple scales.
A set of strategies and tools designed to enhance the performance and reliability of programs utilizing persistent memory, addressing challenges such as cache volatility and store reordering.

PerSoMed is the first large-scale, balanced dataset for Persian social media text classification, containing 36,000 posts across nine categories, aimed at addressing the lack of resources in this domain.

A framework that generates and validates conversational personas for AI agents by analyzing social media interactions, utilizing techniques such as k-means clustering and retrieval-augmented generation.
A recommendation framework that employs offline reasoning to enhance user-item relevance understanding using large language models, reducing latency in real-world applications.
These agents facilitate negotiation between user preferences and automated driving behaviors in traffic, addressing conflicts that arise in automated mobility systems.

P.term: Personalized Calibration Strategies in Conformal Prediction
P.definition: Personalized calibration strategies enhance conformal prediction methods in healthcare settings, addressing distribution shifts and improving coverage guarantees for clinical predictions.

A method for estimating the Personalized PageRank (PPR) in undirected graphs, which calculates the probability of a random walk starting from a source node terminating at a target node, with a focus on time complexity and accuracy.
A method in stochastic gradient descent (SGD) that accounts for perturbations in both forward and backward passes, leading to a geometric amplification of errors through sequential operators.
A pose-free diffusion model for generating music aligned with dance movements, utilizing visual features from dance videos to enhance performance across diverse scenarios.

Property Graph Constraints (PG-Constraints) are a subset of constraints designed for graph databases, focusing on denial constraints and recursion, enabling automata-based structural analyses of errors in property graphs.

PhantomRun is an automated repair tool for compilation errors in embedded open-source software, focusing on resolving hardware dependencies, syntax errors, and build-script issues to streamline Continuous Integration processes.
A concept describing how the introduction of AI into workflows alters the dynamics of task delegation and verification, impacting worker efficiency and institutional evaluation.
An extension of Variational Autoencoders that uses a latent-conditioned Phase-Type distribution as its decoder, enabling the modeling of heavy-tailed data distributions.
An algorithm that implements the phenotype-first framework, allowing organisms to adapt through genetic innovation and learning, emphasizing the role of organismal agency in evolution.

An algorithm that enhances the Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW) for approximate nearest neighbor search by applying Principal Component Analysis (PCA) filtering to reduce dimensionality and improve search efficiency.

Phoenix is a modular pointer analysis framework for C/C++ that integrates various alias analysis algorithms into a single interface, facilitating easy comparison and composition of analyses while highlighting precision-performance trade-offs.

A phenomenon observed in self-supervised speech models where phonological features can be represented as linear directions within the model's representation space, revealing insights into the structure of phonetic information.
The first benchmark designed for personalized intent-driven photo retrieval, constructed from authentic personal albums to evaluate retrieval systems beyond visual matching.

A reimagined network fabric for machine learning datacenters that utilizes optical circuit switches to optimize communication, overcoming limitations of traditional electrical switches in high-radix environments.
A physics-aware framework for novel-view acoustic synthesis that integrates spatial geometry modeling with vision-language semantic priors to enhance spatial audio fidelity.

PhysFusion is a dual-stream radar and vision fusion framework designed for detecting water-surface objects, utilizing physics-informed techniques to enhance perception in challenging maritime environments.

PhysGraph is a physically-grounded graph transformer policy that models bimanual dexterous manipulation as a kinematic graph, allowing for more effective handling of the complex dynamics involved in tool use.
An architecture that implements Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks using reconfigurable nonlinear-processing units to enable efficient hardware realization of learnable nonlinear edge functions.

An approach that emphasizes the importance of choosing appropriate physical representations for relations in Datalog evaluation, moving away from one-size-fits-all solutions to optimize execution plans.
A methodology that incorporates physical laws into neural network training, used here to estimate coolant velocity for effective thermal management in MOSFETs.
A reinforcement learning approach that incorporates physical and geometric constraints into value function estimation, enhancing the learning process in complex environments.

A framework designed to enhance the quality of motion data captured through video-based methods, focusing on physical realism and production readiness by addressing artifacts introduced during capture.
An approach that employs physics-informed probabilistic representations for lossless dataset compression, ensuring distributional fidelity in scientific analysis.
An interpolation method for exterior sound field estimation that utilizes Gaussian processes with a point source reproducing kernel, allowing flexible estimators optimized from recordings.

An unsupervised sound source tracking method that combines a variational encoder with a physics-based decoder, incorporating geometric constraints into the latent space through pairwise time-delay likelihoods.
A unified approach for reconstructing and simulating dynamic 3D scenes that integrates visual rendering with physical simulation using convex primitives governed by continuum mechanics.

PIGTN is a novel detection model that utilizes graph-based representations to enhance attack detection in smart grids, optimized through a multi-objective genetic algorithm for strategic sensor placement.
A neural network architecture that reconstructs local sound fields from sparse acoustic data to infer complex surface impedance, enabling accurate acoustic characterization.
A framework that integrates physical laws into neural networks through soft constraints in the loss function, enabling the modeling of complex systems governed by partial differential equations.

A neural network model that integrates physics principles to estimate vocal-tract area functions and radiation coefficients for singing voice synthesis, ensuring consistency with physical laws during training.
A model for speech emotion recognition that integrates physiological insights into vocal amplitude and phase dynamics to enhance interpretability and performance in humanoid robots.
A novel method for sparse-view Gaussian splatting that enhances view synthesis by utilizing dense π^3 initialization to improve rendering quality and speed in real-time applications.

A control strategy for wireless networked control systems that generates control commands based on plant states and control-aided channel predictions, eliminating the need for real-time channel state information.
A novel framework that replaces explicit pilots in short packet transmission with coded pilots, enhancing reliability and efficiency in ultra-reliable low-latency communication.
A novel architecture for high-frequency wireless communications that utilizes position-adjustable antennas and waveguides to enhance signal coverage and localization.
A type of antenna used in multigroup multicast communications that optimizes placement and resource allocation to enhance max-min fairness in multiple access schemes.

An innovative system designed to mitigate line-of-sight blockages in wireless communications by dynamically repositioning pinching antennas along a dielectric waveguide, optimizing user signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio.

PINEAPPLE is a framework that combines physics-informed neural networks with an evolutionary search algorithm for rapid and interpretable parameter inference in lithium-ion battery electrodes, aimed at improving battery management.
A physics-informed neural network framework designed for accurate wireless channel estimation under pilot constraints, integrating physical interpretability with data-driven approaches.

A three-phase pipeline that constructs schema-specific natural language SPARQL benchmarks for RDF knowledge graphs, utilizing techniques like reverse querying and retrieval-augmented prompting to improve query performance.
A framework designed for the automation of reusable high-throughput network applications on FPGA, enhancing the agility of network infrastructure in response to evolving demands.

PiPNN (Pick-in-Partitions Nearest Neighbors) is an ultra-scalable graph construction algorithm that utilizes a novel online pruning technique called HashPrune to enhance the efficiency of approximate nearest neighbor indexing.
An ultra-scalable graph construction algorithm for Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search that utilizes a novel online pruning method to enhance query performance while minimizing construction time.
A large-scale dataset consisting of over 23 million statements in propositional logic along with their corresponding proofs, designed to benchmark reasoning models focusing on length generalization.
A lightweight network used for extracting compact, task-relevant semantics from raw sensory data in multi-robot systems, enhancing communication efficiency in 3D environments.
A knowledge distillation framework for large audio-language models that aligns audio embeddings and output logits, enhancing the efficiency of speech emotion recognition models.

A novel algorithm that utilizes Message Passing Networks to create latent node embeddings for telemetry-aware greedy routing, improving network routing efficiency without solving the all-pairs shortest paths problem.
A computational problem involving counting weighted homomorphisms of planar graphs, with a dichotomy theorem that classifies the problem as either polynomial-time tractable or #P-hard.

An extended Actor-Critic architecture designed for creative 3D modeling, where a Planner coordinates tasks, an Actor executes them, and a Critic provides feedback, integrating human supervision for enhanced modeling processes.

A novel algorithm that enables agents to learn tasks through observation and interaction without predefined rewards or demonstrator actions, focusing on world modeling in data-constrained environments.
A mobile application that employs conversational AI to assist users in plant care, promoting well-being through interactive engagement with their plants.
A multimodal system that enables AI-supported narrative co-authoring in mixed reality, allowing users to manipulate virtual characters and props to create stories.
A method for visual pragmatic repair in natural language database querying that addresses input ambiguity by utilizing incremental clarification through minimal interaction.
A method for restoring Landsat images that facilitates the analysis of river dynamics over time, enabling the uncovering of historical surface water changes.

PluRel is a framework designed to synthesize multi-tabular relational databases from scratch, incorporating schema structure and primary-foreign key connectivity to facilitate the training of Relational Foundation Models.
A simulation tool within the PLUTO code framework designed for predicting non-thermal emissions from shock-accelerated particles in astrophysical flows, leveraging Lagrangian particle methods.
PocketGS is a mobile scene modeling paradigm that enables on-device training of 3D Gaussian Splatting, addressing resource constraints while maintaining high perceptual fidelity in 3D scene modeling.

PoCo is a technique that enhances coverage-based seed selection methods in fuzzing by systematically removing conditional statements that obstruct deeper seed selection, thereby improving fuzzing effectiveness.

P.term: POEM (Proximal Policy Optimization with Evolutionary Mutations)
P.definition: A modified version of the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm that incorporates an adaptive exploration mechanism inspired by evolutionary algorithms to enhance policy diversity.

P.term: Point Vortex Dynamics on Closed Surfaces
P.definition: An extension of point vortex dynamics theory to closed surfaces, providing a comprehensive guide for computational fluid simulations with improved speed and accuracy.

PointCoT is a benchmark designed for explicit 3D geometric reasoning in multimodal large language models, addressing the challenges of geometric hallucinations by emphasizing intermediate logical steps in 3D point cloud understanding.

PointSLAM++ is an advanced RGB-D SLAM system that employs a hierarchically constrained neural Gaussian representation to enhance structural consistency and localization accuracy in real-time 3D reconstruction, particularly in noisy environments.
An analysis of the convergence behavior of optimal transport mappings and potential functions when approximating target measures with unbounded support.

This concept explores the vulnerabilities of learned indexes to poisoning attacks, where maliciously injected keys can degrade model accuracy and performance, particularly in linear regression models over cumulative distribution functions.
A mathematical function synthesized from an occupancy map to enhance dynamic collision avoidance in robotic systems by serving as a control barrier function.
A multi-architecture, containerized ephemeral malware detonation sandbox that allows dynamic malware analysis in isolated environments, enhancing portability and automation.

Polar is an algebraic analyzer designed for automating the analysis of classical and probabilistic loops using algebraic reasoning, capable of handling recurrences, invariants, and loop sensitivities.
An innovative antenna architecture that maintains polarization orientation while enabling 3D rotation, enhancing directional gain for wireless communications.
A tool designed to enforce complex authorization policies in LLM-based agents by modeling system states as dependency graphs to ensure deterministic policy enforcement.
This benchmark consists of annotated user requests that assess compliance with diverse organizational policies, aimed at ensuring safe and reliable interactions with AI systems.

A policy-parametric type system is a framework developed for choreographic programming that enforces non-interference by preventing information leaks from high-security data to low-security observers. It manages both explicit and implicit flows through a program-counter discipline.

PolicySmith is a framework that utilizes large language models (LLMs) to automate the design of instance-optimal heuristics for systems controllers, replacing manual policy design with automated code generation.

A uniform complete equational theory for quantum circuits over d-level systems (qudits), utilizing control functors as primitive categorical constructors to establish soundness and completeness for unitary d-level circuits.

P.term: Polynomial Kernel for Face Cover Number
P.definition: A polynomial kernel that reduces the Face Cover Number problem for planar graphs to a smaller instance, facilitating efficient computation without a fixed embedding.

A novel token-mixing mechanism that serves as a drop-in replacement for multi-head self-attention in speech-to-text models, achieving linear complexity in computation with respect to input sequence length.

A training method for Differentiable Ternary Logic Gate Networks that uses polynomial representations to manage the complexity of potential gates, enabling efficient gradient-based learning in the presence of ternary logic states.

This method generates polynomial inequalities to serve as symmetry breaking constraints in integer programming, reducing redundant searches. It utilizes an arbitrary base polynomial and a specific group of permutations to create effective constraints.

P.term: Polynomial Time Algorithm for Noisy Character Recovery
P.definition: An algorithm that recovers polynomials over finite fields from noisy character values, achieving polynomial time complexity even with a constant fraction of errors in the data.
A mathematical property established for optimal control problems involving infinite-dimensional oscillating systems, providing a polynomial estimate for convergence around steady states.
A function defined over L2 spaces that can be computed in polynomial time, with two natural definitions shown to be incomparable unless complexity class FP_1 includes #P_1.

P.term: Polynomial-time Isomorphism Test for k-generated Extensions
P.definition: An algorithm that determines the isomorphism of certain extensions of abelian groups in polynomial time, improving upon previous algorithms for arbitrary groups.
A computational problem that investigates the feasibility of covering a stain with non-overlapping stickers of identical shapes, classified under polyomino constraints.
A polytopological version of Propositional Dynamic Logic (PDL) that can express common knowledge and its generalizations, demonstrating the finite model property over closure spaces.

Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) is a framework used for modeling decision-making in environments where the state is not fully observable, applied in remote monitoring with energy-harvesting sensors.
A pretraining framework that implements token-wise adaptive pondering, allowing models to allocate additional computation selectively at inference based on self-supervised learning objectives.

This framework develops a Poisson Hamiltonian formulation for optimal control of mechanical systems on Lie groupoids, providing a natural reduced phase space for Pontryagin dynamics through symplectic leaves.

A set of maximum principles established for optimal control of partially observed coupled forward stochastic partial differential equations and backward stochastic differential equations, facilitating control in stochastic environments.
A technique used in continuous-time portfolio optimization that aggregates costate variables across uncertain parameters to maximize investment objectives.
An investigation into the space-time trade-offs for population protocols in sparse interaction graphs, revealing the limitations of existing lower bound techniques in bounded-degree trees.
A technique for rendering portals in virtual reality that aims to make transitions through portals seamless for users while optimizing performance in VR environments.

A pose-guided Gaussian Splatting framework for high-fidelity human novel view synthesis, integrating pose signals into both geometric and temporal stages for enhanced depth estimation and temporal consistency.
Techniques aimed at reducing computational overhead in multi-condition Diffusion Transformers by eliminating redundant attention mechanisms through spatial and semantic alignment.
A module introduced to normalize engagement in video ranking systems, addressing position bias through quantile-based distributions for robust long-term value estimation.

An advanced attention mechanism that incorporates positional information more effectively into sequential models, overcoming the limitations of traditional additive positional embeddings in capturing temporal order.
Novel click models designed for carousel interfaces that account for complex user browsing behaviors, optimizing recommendations based on positional information in swipeable lists.
A data structure designed for efficiently representing and querying large collections of sequences, particularly useful in genomics for haplotype matching and related analyses.
An active learning strategy used to curate a large dataset of acoustic data from marine mammals, employing weakly-supervised methods to identify instances effectively.

P.term: Post Hoc Extraction of Pareto Fronts
P.definition: A method for extracting Pareto front solutions in continuous control tasks, allowing agents to balance multiple objectives after training on single-objective policies.
A research paradigm that seeks to align artificial intelligence development with sustainable and equitable economic practices, advocating for satisficing over optimization.

This novel signature scheme utilizes a chameleon hash function based on the McEliece cryptosystem, allowing authorized modifications of messages while ensuring immutability and security against quantum attacks.
An online calibration approach for complex social simulations that formulates the calibration task as a dynamic optimization problem, continuously updating parameters to match real system changes.
An algorithm that transforms normal form games into approximating games with ordinal potential functions, enabling efficient multi-agent learning through a surrogate reward structure.

Power Particles is a method for fluid simulation based on partial optimal transport that utilizes analytically constructed generalized Laguerre cells to improve computational efficiency and accuracy in free-surface fluid simulations.
A model that characterizes complex market dynamics through power-law scaling of covariance matrices, integrated with deep residual networks for improved estimation in noisy environments.
A framework for the automated discovery of superior reconfigurable power converters, utilizing an analytical approach to explore a vast design space for optimal circuit topologies.

The Partially Ordered Workflow Language 2.0 (POWL 2.0) is an evolution of POWL that introduces choice graphs for modeling non-block-structured decisions and cycles in process modeling, enhancing expressiveness and quality guarantees.

The Physically-Plausible ISP (PPISP) correction module disentangles camera-intrinsic and capture-dependent effects in multi-view 3D reconstruction, providing a physically grounded approach to mitigate photometric inconsistencies.

PPO-LTL integrates Linear Temporal Logic constraints into Proximal Policy Optimization, enabling the systematic monitoring of safety requirements in reinforcement learning applications, particularly in robotics.

PR2 (Personalized Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning) is a reinforcement learning framework designed to enhance personalization in question answering by integrating reasoning with retrieval of personal context from user profiles.
This technique enhances session types by enabling type inference with arithmetic refinements, reducing the annotation burden on programmers while ensuring safe communication in concurrent systems.
A technique used in scientific data compression to remove sequential dependencies and enable high parallelism, though it may introduce artifacts that degrade data quality under certain error bounds.

A method for improving data quality in crowdsourced GUI experiments by screening participants through a preliminary task that assesses their attention and care, thereby enhancing the validity of performance data.

PSUM is the first standardized specification for uncertainty modeling in Model-Based Systems Engineering, providing formally defined semantics for representing and reasoning about uncertainty in engineered systems.
A technique that optimizes flow matching and score-based diffusion by reshaping the geometry of intermediate distributions to mitigate optimization bias caused by ill-conditioned covariance.

A unified framework for search relevance matching and click-through rate prediction that incorporates cross-user preference mining and exposure bias correction to improve personalized relevance modeling.

A framework introduced for beamforming design in amplify-and-forward relay MIMO systems that utilizes predictive models to optimize beamform design while addressing challenges in channel state information acquisition.
An architecture that enhances memory retrieval by utilizing temporal co-occurrence rather than similarity, allowing for more nuanced associations in neural systems.

Predictive Coding Networks (PCNs) are a class of neural networks that minimize an energy function based on network activities, offering a biologically plausible alternative to traditional backpropagation methods.
A technique used for coverage verification in UAV trajectory planning that estimates the likelihood of rare failure events more efficiently than traditional uniform sampling methods.
A domain-specific language designed for predictive modeling on relational databases, facilitating the prediction of future or missing values using machine learning methods.

A communication paradigm where the transmitter and receiver maintain a shared predictive state, allowing the physical channel to primarily convey innovations or corrective information rather than raw data.
A standardized benchmarking framework for evaluating task scheduling and preemption techniques in FPGAs, aimed at improving reproducibility and comparison across studies.

Prefill-Decode Disaggregation is a paradigm that separates the compute-intensive prefill phase from the memory-intensive decode phase in serving large language models, optimizing resource utilization for multi-round inference.

PrefillShare is a shared prefill module designed for key-value (KV) reuse in multi-LLM disaggregated serving, which reduces prefill load and tail latency by optimizing the execution of common prompt prefixes across multiple language models.
A generalization of clustering and partial ordering that seeks to maximize the sum of values for ordered pairs in a finite set, with applications in bioinformatics and social network analysis.

A control technique ensuring convergence to equilibrium of a one-dimensional heat equation within a specified time frame, independent of initial conditions, enhancing transient performance in applications.
A processing-in-memory (PIM) based inference accelerator for large language models (LLMs) that incorporates low-rank adaptation techniques to enhance efficiency and reduce power consumption.
A novel framework for pricing complex data products in data-poor markets, leveraging prior predictive distributions to address challenges in transaction data scarcity.

PRISM-FCP (Partial shaRing and robust calIbration with Statistical Margins for Federated Conformal Prediction) is a Byzantine-resilient federated conformal prediction framework that enhances robustness against Byzantine attacks during model training and calibration through partial model sharing.

PRISM-XR is a framework that enhances privacy-aware collaboration in Extended Reality (XR) environments by utilizing multimodal large language models to create objects and animations while safeguarding sensitive user information.

A tool used to surface mental models and explore privacy concerns, particularly in voice-first ambient interfaces, by customizing interview protocols based on user interactions and privacy considerations.
A challenge in Differentially Private Federated Learning (DP-FL) where clients have varying privacy requirements, complicating the selection of clients for collaborative model training.

A system that enables privacy-aware inference of large language models by splitting the model between a trusted local GPU and an untrusted cloud GPU, ensuring that sensitive data remains on the local device.
A real-time conversational assistant that uses audio and inertial measurement unit (IMU) data to provide step-by-step guidance for procedural tasks while maintaining user privacy.

A method that quantifies the contribution of individual training examples to a model's behavior while ensuring that sensitive information about the data contributors remains confidential, addressing the tension between privacy and utility under differential privacy.
A framework for enabling direct trades among prosumers and consumers in local energy markets while ensuring participant data privacy and considering network-related costs.
An approach that enhances Vision-Language-Action models by leveraging privileged 4D information during training to improve understanding of world dynamics and physical interactions.

PrivMedChat is an end-to-end framework for differentially private reinforcement learning from human feedback (DP-RLHF) tailored for medical dialogue systems, designed to mitigate risks of sensitive information leakage.
A phenomenon where pro-China influencers on social media platforms like TikTok achieve higher engagement rates than traditional state media, impacting public perceptions of authoritarian regimes.
A framework for perceptive humanoid-object interaction that optimizes control policies based on desired root trajectories and real-world deployment conditions.
A dual-system framework for proactive embodied social agents, allowing for synchronized speech and gestures while managing the latency constraints of real-time interactions.
A method for enhancing item-side fairness in interactive recommendation systems by actively guiding user preferences towards long-tail items while maintaining user satisfaction.
A technique that combines probabilistic amplitude shaping with quadrature amplitude modulation formats to enhance continuous variable quantum key distribution systems over linear quantum channels.
An innovative optimization algorithm that treats the optimization landscape as variational, learning circuit parameters iteratively from samples to solve combinatorial optimization problems.
A consensus protocol that operates under a probabilistic failure model, allowing for more nuanced handling of faults in distributed systems compared to traditional deterministic models.

Probabilistic consensus protocols extend traditional consensus mechanisms by incorporating a probabilistic failure model, allowing systems to optimize for cost and performance in the face of complex real-world faults.
A method for achieving fair ordering of events in distributed systems by embracing synchronization errors and using statistical models to compare timestamps.
A framework that simulates simultaneous or future matches to compute qualifying probabilities for outcomes in sports tournaments, addressing limitations in existing match classification models.
A novel sensing paradigm that utilizes probabilistic decision-making for data sampling, inspired by the autonomous nervous system, to improve energy efficiency in sensor networks.
This technique involves the automated formalization of probabilistic requirements from structured natural language, addressing challenges in specifying uncertainties in software-intensive systems.

Probabilistic Retrofitting is a method that transforms pre-trained deterministic models into probabilistic ones using a proper scoring rule, allowing for efficient training while leveraging existing model strengths.
A probabilistic extension of Strategic Timed CTL (STCTL) that is applicable to stochastic multi-agent systems, enhancing the expressiveness of temporal logic in strategic settings.

A plug-in module for text-to-image diffusion models that enables early quality assessment of generated images, improving efficiency by allowing for the selection of high-quality images without extensive post-hoc evaluation.
A system for procedural 3D generation and parametric editing that produces editable 3D models in real-time using a procedural compact graph representation.

A phenomenon observed in large language models where they fail to execute a verifiable, prompt-grounded specification despite having the correct value in context, often manifesting as readout-stage routing failures.
A framework for evaluating Large Language Model-based agents that formalizes agent procedures and assesses their performance across multiple dimensions, including utility and procedural integrity.
A guiding framework for inference in DEEPTHINK systems that utilizes process rewards to enhance the correctness of candidate solutions during reasoning tasks.

PIM architectures enable computation directly within DRAM, addressing the memory wall problem by allowing operations like bit-shifting to be performed without moving data off-chip. This approach enhances performance for various applications, including cryptography.
A phenomenon where college enrollment rates increase during economic upturns and decrease during downturns, influenced by labor market conditions at the time of high school graduation.
A structured, multi-stage AI coding system designed for client-side development that addresses the complexities of real-world engineering constraints and heterogeneous inputs.

This technique allows users to define executable specifications in a flexible manner, enabling customization of testing processes beyond the limitations of traditional property-based testing frameworks.
A lightweight codec for generative image compression that utilizes residual vector quantization to progressively encode image residuals, improving flexibility for low-bitrate transmission.
An interactive approach to visualizing data with large magnitudes by using motion to allow viewers to gradually experience differences in scale, enhancing comprehension.
A mixed-method framework that evaluates the social impact of community cooperation projects by analyzing participation architecture and collaboration networks.

A class of computational problems where the goal is to satisfy certain constraints under specific promise conditions, with implications for robust satisfiability and hardness results in computational complexity.

A class of constraint satisfaction problems where the instance is guaranteed to satisfy at least a certain fraction of constraints, allowing for the study of robust satisfiability under specific conditions.
Types of vulnerabilities in large language models where malicious prompts can manipulate model behavior, necessitating robust security evaluations to safeguard against such risks.
Metrics developed to evaluate the consistency and accuracy of small open-source language models in clinical question answering, focusing on their performance across various prompt styles.
A non-parametric approach to model reasoning that extracts reasoning patterns from a teacher model and organizes them into structured instructions for a student model's system prompt.

This paradigm allows users to express their immediate intent through natural language prompts, enabling recommendation systems to adapt dynamically to user goals rather than relying solely on historical behavior.

PromptPream is a computation-powered preloading paradigm that reduces bandwidth demand by sending compact semantic prompts instead of pixel-level video chunks, effectively minimizing playback stalls in short video platforms.
The first proof calculus designed for reasoning about liveness properties in concurrent programs executed on weak memory models, extending existing proof rules to incorporate memory fairness.
A collaborative workflow for rapid draft generation and verification of mathematical claims, utilizing adversarial verification and explicit provenance to enhance the quality of research outputs.

Variables introduced in a verification framework to predict the number of steps until certain temporal properties hold, aiding in the verification of first-order temporal properties in infinite-state systems.
A technique that predicts future execution behavior of programs to facilitate backwards data-flow analysis, enabling program transformations and optimizations.

Proportion Loss is a regularization term introduced in semi-supervised learning that aligns model predictions with the global class distribution, effectively mitigating bias in class-imbalanced datasets.

A methodology for constructing an index composed of risky and risk-free assets that achieves a fixed target volatility using proportional control, demonstrating improved performance over traditional methods.

A general model for selecting candidates based on voter preferences under constraints, which generalizes committee elections and participatory budgeting, incorporating axioms of proportionality for diverse decision-making.

Proscenium is a dual-layer, large transparent display workspace setup that allows for adjustable separation between layers, enabling innovative interaction techniques through layered information experiences.

A U-Net variant that employs decoder conditioning for proximal-sparse skip feature selection, enhancing medical image segmentation by filtering out irrelevant information while preserving fine spatial details.

The Protean Compiler is an agile framework designed to address the phase ordering problem in compiler optimization by utilizing machine learning to adaptively optimize the compilation phases based on varying benchmarks.
An optimization framework designed to enhance the security resilience of cell-free massive MIMO networks against active eavesdropping by employing protective partial zero-forcing techniques.

Proto is an instructional operating system designed to run on commodity hardware, featuring modern capabilities such as per-app address spaces and multicore support, aimed at teaching OS concepts through engaging applications.
Proto-ML is an integrated development environment (IDE) designed to enhance machine learning solution prototyping by facilitating collaboration and knowledge reuse among stakeholders.
A method for network traffic representation that addresses challenges posed by encryption and evolving protocols by leveraging redundancy across network layers for more efficient classification.
A prototype-based disentanglement text-to-speech framework that separates speaker identity from dysarthric articulation within a unified latent space.
ProtRLSearch is a multi-round multimodal protein search agent that utilizes large language models trained via reinforcement learning to enhance protein analysis tasks in healthcare.
A framework that tracks correction lineage in OCR processes, capturing details such as edit type and confidence to enhance the interpretability of digital humanities texts.
A memory management approach for agents that retains raw logs while allowing for efficient summaries, addressing the challenges of unverified omissions in long-horizon interactions.
An algorithm that minimizes the Kullback-Leibler divergence between steady state and target Gaussian distributions in linear dynamical systems, promoting sparsity through L1 regularization.

A concept where a collection of sets satisfies a proximity gap with respect to a property if members are either close to the property in Hamming distance or only a small fraction are. This is relevant in the context of Reed-Solomon codes and list decoding.

The Prune-then-Merge framework is a two-stage approach for efficient visual document retrieval that combines pruning and merging techniques to optimize the trade-off between compression rate and feature fidelity.
A lightweight spiking neural network architecture derived from SqueezeNet, designed to benchmark accuracy-energy tradeoffs, particularly for edge intelligence applications.

A dual-branch parallel spectral-spatial architecture designed for multi-channel speech separation, processing spectral and spatial features through separate streams to improve performance in dynamic environments.
A randomized algorithm that outputs a fixed canonical solution for the global minimum cut problem, utilizing a graph-theoretic tie-breaking mechanism to enhance performance.

Quantum algorithms that consistently output a canonical solution with high probability for any input, characterized by unique complexities that differentiate them from classical randomized algorithms.
A technique in automatic chord recognition that generates labels for unlabeled audio data using a pre-trained model, facilitating the training of a student model in a two-stage pipeline.

P.term: Pseudo-Labeling and Knowledge Distillation for ACR
P.definition: This two-stage training pipeline enhances automatic chord recognition by generating pseudo-labels from pre-trained models and training a student model on unlabeled audio data.

Algorithms that generate sequences of numbers that approximate the properties of random numbers, particularly useful in derandomization and circuit lower bounds, with optimal constructions over polynomial-size fields.
A framework for Pseudo Seed Quality Enhancement in Unsupervised Multimodal Entity Alignment, addressing challenges in obtaining labeled seed pairs by utilizing pseudo-alignment seeds.
A methodology for defining and characterizing surrogate models in deep learning, focusing on analyzing their Precision, Time, Memory, and Energy consumption to enhance optimization processes.
A Prompt-Tuned Temporal Shift Spiking Neural Network designed for efficient speech emotion recognition, addressing distribution mismatch in self-supervised learning representations.
An experimental economic game that studies cooperation among individuals in a group setting, where participants decide how much to contribute to a common pool that benefits all.

Public-moment-guided Truncation (PMT) is a technique that applies differential privacy to unbounded data by leveraging second-moment information from public datasets to transform private data while maintaining privacy guarantees.

This framework integrates Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs) into deep neural network (DNN) models to provide post-theft accountability and traceability, enhancing security against model theft during knowledge distillation.
A traditional South Indian craft practice that utilizes structured geometric rules for physical data representation, integrating cultural practices with data visualization.
A neural architecture that incorporates learnable oscillatory dynamics for robust continuous-time sequence processing, enhancing state evolution independently of external inputs.
A dyadic messaging prototype that enhances intimate communication through embodied interaction and reciprocity-aware recommendations.

PURE is a preference-aware reasoning framework for explainable recommendation systems that improves the selection of evidence to generate more consistent and convincing explanations aligned with user preferences.
A decentralized optimization method that facilitates learning over directed communication networks, addressing stability and generalization issues through a uniform-stability framework.
A decentralized learning framework that utilizes push-sum methods for optimization over directed communication networks, addressing stability and generalization issues.
A scalable orchestration framework for zero-knowledge proof generation in blockchain systems, addressing timing and reliability constraints to ensure timely proof completion.

PVT-GDLA is a decoder-centric transformer model that employs gated differential linear attention to achieve linear-time complexity while preserving sharp, long-range dependencies in medical image segmentation.
A physics and data-informed model that predicts battery state of charge (SOC) depletion in robots based on time and pulse-width modulation (PWM) duty cycle.

A novel distributed Gaussian Process framework tailored for large-scale multi-robot systems, utilizing sparse variational inference to create compact pseudo-representations for efficient modeling under computational constraints.

A fusion power-plant costing framework developed by the Clean Air Task Force, which incorporates a standards-aligned chart-of-accounts and a physics-to-economics workflow for analyzing costs in Magnetic Fusion Energy, Inertial Fusion Energy, and Magneto-Inertial Fusion Energy.

PyFi is a framework designed for pyramid-like financial image understanding, enabling vision language models to process financial questions in a structured manner, enhancing their reasoning capabilities.
PymooLab is an open-source visual analytics framework that simplifies multi-objective optimization by providing a user-friendly interface for configuration, execution monitoring, and analysis.

A new functional element type introduced for dynamic adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) in three-dimensional mixed-element meshes, designed to connect tetrahedral and hexahedral elements without creating hanging edges.

J.definition: A system designed to translate jailbreak techniques for large language models into executable modules, facilitating immediate evaluation and reproducible benchmarking.
In the context of large language models, jailbreaking refers to a user-centric practice aimed at exposing automated behavior of LLM-powered accounts on social media to disrupt misleading narratives.
A user-centric approach to counteract the manipulation of political discourse by engaging with suspected LLM-powered accounts to expose automated behavior and disrupt misleading narratives.
A benchmark designed to evaluate the resilience of large language models against fake news generation under jailbreak attacks, incorporating multi-lingual and regional perspectives.
A unified framework for joint audio-video generation that enhances the quality and synchronization of multimodal outputs based on textual descriptions.
A tiered memory management system that optimizes access to heterogeneous memory by dynamically migrating pages between fast and slow memory tiers to prevent thrashing.
A method for automatically inferring join relationships in databases using only metadata, represented as adjacency matrices that exhibit high sparsity.
A graph-based algorithm designed for filtered nearest neighbor search, which generalizes across various filter types and query selectivities to improve performance in practical deployments.
A foundational model that jointly generates sound and visual content, enabling a single-model approach for video dubbing that simplifies complex, task-specific pipelines.
An algorithm that simultaneously estimates and compensates for sampling frequency offsets using the Farrow structure, enabling low-complexity solutions for arbitrary bandlimited signals.

A method that combines source coding and channel coding to optimize the transmission of multimedia data over unreliable channels, enabling reliable semantic reconstruction even in the presence of packet loss.
A technique in multi-agent reinforcement learning that integrates learned embeddings of joint state-action representations to enhance data efficiency and coordination among agents.

This framework is designed for cloud-native platforms, focusing on joint load forecasting by representing the system as a coupled entity of service invocation graphs and multivariate load sequences, enabling more accurate predictions of load fluctuations.

A low-complexity phase recovery scheme designed to mitigate laser phase noise and fiber nonlinearity across multiple subcarriers, achieving significant performance gains in long single-span links with Raman amplification.
A modular framework that integrates text-to-speech and audio-to-face models to enhance the consistency of generated audio and facial expressions by reusing intermediate representations.

J.term: Joint Training Across Multiple Activation Sparsity Regimes
J.definition: A training strategy that applies global top-k constraints to hidden activations, cycling a model through various activation budgets to enhance generalization in deep neural networks.
JEPA is an encoder architecture designed to learn representations from offline state sequences, facilitating action-conditioned latent predictions for short horizon transitions.

JEPAs are latent predictive architectures that allow agents to plan and make decisions in a latent space derived from high-dimensional visual observations, enhancing robustness against irrelevant visual variations.
A mathematical framework used to implement high-dimensional unitary irreducible representations of SU(n) in quantum computing, facilitating efficient quantum circuit design.
A reimagined network stack design for Linux aimed at enhancing performance, security, and reliability for high-end networking, particularly for NICs operating at 100 Gbps and beyond.

JSAM (Joint client Selection and privacy compensAtion Mechanism) is a Bayesian-optimal framework designed to address the inefficiencies in differentially private federated learning by optimizing client selection and compensation strategies.

M.definition: M-GENGAR is a methodology for automated detection and mitigation of dependability failures in medical cyber-physical systems using a closed-loop Digital Twin paradigm.
A transformer-based architecture designed for millimeter wave multi-target tracking that incorporates lost target re-acquisition capabilities, improving tracking performance in complex environments.
M2LSimu is a mobility measures-guided multi-prompt adjustment framework designed to enhance human mobility simulations by incorporating population-level coordination mechanisms.
Motion-to-Motion (M2M) latency encompasses the total delay experienced by a driver in connected and autonomous vehicles, including both G2G latency and additional components affecting responsiveness.
Multi-Modal Volatility Network, a deep learning framework that integrates time series data with unstructured news data for improved financial volatility forecasting.
A metadata-augmented microscaling data format designed to enhance low-bit quantization accuracy while maintaining high bit efficiency across large language models.

Mixed-source multi-modal misinformation detection (M3D) is a complex task that involves identifying false information originating from various modalities, such as text and images, particularly in zero-shot settings.
Large-scale, reproducible multimodal datasets for movie recommendations, enriched with multimedia features to support advanced recommendation algorithms.
A multi-agent collaborative navigation framework that enhances long-horizon vision-language navigation by distributing cognitive load across multiple agents to improve decision-making.

MaBERT is a hybrid encoder that interleaves Transformer layers for global dependency modeling with Mamba layers for linear time state updates, addressing the inefficiencies of traditional self-attention encoders in long context modeling.
A process aimed at removing specific data points from a trained model while striving to emulate 'perfect retraining', which poses privacy risks to undeleted data points.
A framework that employs machine learning techniques to optimize ray tracing for radio propagation modeling, significantly reducing computational complexity by intelligently sampling candidate paths.
An extension of microfacet theory to macro-space, allowing for the representation of Gaussian process implicit surfaces by addressing correlation along geometric normals.

Magellan is an autonomous framework that evolves compiler optimization heuristics through a closed loop of generation, evaluation, and refinement, integrating decision logic directly into compiler passes.

A novel multivariate representation of risk that captures the intrinsic structure of potential portfolio losses, enhancing the informative value of risk assessments beyond traditional scalar measures.

A competitive graph coloring game where two players, Maker and Breaker, alternately color vertices to maximize the number of happy vertices, defined as vertices that share the same color as all their neighbors.
A failure mode in over-parameterized networks where the model segregates signal and noise, resulting in harmful overfitting as the noise-to-signal ratio increases.
A failure mode in over-parameterized networks where label noise segregates from signal, leading to harmful overfitting and reduced semantic coherence in learned representations.
A graph-assisted encrypted traffic detection system that employs a Mixture of Experts approach to address the challenges of detecting malicious traffic in the presence of graph drift.
An operating system architecture that utilizes machine learning to dynamically adapt resource allocation based on user demand, aiming to improve kernel decisions.
An architecture that combines the strengths of pre-trained transformers with state space models for efficient extractive summarization, enabling linear-time processing of long documents.
The first deployment of a Mamba-based neural architecture on microcontrollers, featuring a C-based runtime-free inference engine optimized for resource-constrained environments.

A local multi-model pipeline that transforms open source Vision Language Models into mammogram report generators and multi-task classifiers, enhancing privacy and adaptability in medical documentation.
A manifold-constrained adaptive reasoning framework for sequential recommendation that focuses on navigating collaborative manifolds to avoid latent drift during multi-step reasoning.
A method that modifies denoising score matching to account for data distributions defined on manifolds, improving computational efficiency while learning.

The Manifold of Failure framework systematically maps unsafe regions in Large Language Models (LLMs), identifying behavioral attraction basins where vulnerabilities may arise, thus aiding in the understanding of AI safety.
A generative modeling framework that balances support fidelity and sampling efficiency by aligning generative processes with the underlying data manifold.

MAP-Diff is a multi-anchor guided diffusion framework designed for progressive 3D whole-body PET denoising, utilizing clinically observed intermediate-dose scans as anchors to regularize the reverse diffusion process.
An algorithmic approach for motif discovery in computational biology that frames the problem as a quality-diversity task, evolving position weight matrix motifs while preserving biological diversity.
An adaptive strategy for reward modeling in reinforcement learning that focuses on augmenting training data based on the difficulty of estimating rewards, improving alignment in AI systems.
A metric defined to partition tariffs into welfare-improving, trade-off, and revenue-decreasing regions, used in the analysis of the Tariff Laffer Curve for the U.S. trade war.

A structural re-parameterization technique for accelerating ranking model inference in large-scale recommendation systems, optimizing feature fusion matrix multiplication to achieve lossless acceleration.
A modular underwater research facility that serves as a testbed for maritime robotics, integrating simulation, laboratory validation, and real-world conditions for enhanced experimental accuracy.

A new class of randomized processes where an algorithm interacts with a Markovian random evolution by rewinding the chain to previous states, potentially leading to faster convergence or improved learning efficiency.

M.term: Markov Decision Process (MDP) for Machine Maintenance
M.definition: A framework that models the maintenance and repair of heterogeneous machines on a network, aiming to minimize long-run average costs through reinforcement learning.

A variant of communication protocols characterized by their geometric properties, specifically in relation to the extension complexity of polytopes, which can be used to derive upper bounds on the complexity of problems like the matching polytope.
MarODE is an offline evaluation framework designed to assign quality scores to reasoning traces generated by language models, focusing on human-centric notions of reasoning quality.
A framework for uncertainty quantification in clustering that combines density-based clustering with a statistical approach to propagate uncertainty in estimated densities effectively.
MAS-FIRE is a framework for fault injection and reliability evaluation in LLM-based multi-agent systems, focusing on identifying and mitigating semantic failures that arise during agent coordination.

MASFly is a novel multi-agent framework that enables dynamic adaptation of LLM-based systems at test time, utilizing a retrieval-augmented SOP instantiation mechanism to enhance collaboration and adaptability.
A new task for evaluating the quality of segmentation masks in language-referred audio-visual segmentation without relying on ground-truth annotations.
MQA-RefAVS is a new task for evaluating the quality of segmentation masks in language-referred audio-visual segmentation without relying on ground-truth annotations.

This technique enhances audio-visual speech recognition by eliminating the need for mask-based strategies, thus improving robustness against noise interference during feature fusion in high-noise environments.
A class of language models that generate outputs by refining all sequence positions simultaneously, raising questions about their reasoning capabilities in tasks requiring justifications.
A bandit-based prompt optimization technique for Multi-Agent Systems that addresses sample efficiency and topology-induced challenges in optimizing input prompts for enhanced performance.
A large-scale benchmark that evaluates over 50 models across 30 audio-related tasks, providing insights into model performance across diverse audio domains including speech and environmental sounds.
A benchmark designed to evaluate a wide range of auditory capabilities in intelligent systems, focusing on transforming raw audio signals into meaningful embeddings for various tasks.

A technique for improving channel estimation performance in cell-free massive MIMO networks by designing the pilot transmission phase to create inter-access point signal correlations, enabling centralized processing benefits.

M.term: Matched Filter Principle for Order Flow Normalization
M.definition: This principle establishes optimal normalization techniques for order flow data, tailored to the scaling behavior of different trading strategies, enhancing signal extraction for institutional investors.
A framework that guides designers in creating user-centered sustainable materials, focusing on the sensory experiences that contribute to art healing.

Math Battles with AI is a competitive educational format designed to enhance digital literacy in mathematics by using AI agents with increased hallucination likelihood to train verification skills among students.
A class of optimization problems characterized by the absence of constraint qualifications, often tackled using relaxation-based approaches to yield satisfactory solutions.
An LLM-based multi-agent system that automates the discovery and formalization of mathematical folklore lemmas, enhancing the usability of formal mathematics tools like Lean.
A statistical method used to estimate missing values in a matrix, leveraging observed data to infer unobserved entries, particularly useful in counterfactual analysis.

An extension of Stochastic Mirror Descent for matrix parameters that converges exponentially to a global interpolator in the overparameterized regime, relevant for multi-class classification and matrix completion.
An extension of stochastic mirror descent that uses matrix parameters and vector-valued predictions, proving convergence in overparameterized regimes.

Maty is an actor language design that integrates multiparty session types to enhance the reliability of actor communication patterns, preventing costly errors such as communication mismatches and deadlocks.

A concept used in the validation of space robotics in underwater environments, focusing on ensuring mission specifications can withstand environmental disturbances, thereby simulating microgravity conditions effectively.
A measure used to characterize the concentration of decision rules in choice under risk, indicating the diversity or uniformity of decision-making strategies employed by individuals.
A framework for studying optimal error-correcting codes that maximize data recoverability in large-scale computing systems by introducing redundancy.
An ordering technique for nodes in undirected graphs that facilitates the decomposition of graphs into simpler subgraphs, aiding in the solution of complex problems.
A computational problem that seeks to find a common subgraph between two graphs with a specified minimum number of vertices, avoiding isolated vertices.

M.term: Maximum Entropy Testing in Network Models
M.definition: This method employs maximum entropy principles to formulate constrained optimization problems for statistical inference in stochastic network models, enhancing goodness-of-fit and two-sample testing procedures.

A method that establishes an explicit approximation of maxout networks using Transformer architectures while maintaining comparable model complexity, inheriting the universal approximation capability of ReLU networks.
A large-scale benchmark for evaluating fine-grained, multi-condition cross-modal retrieval, designed to assess systems that align and retrieve information across multiple interdependent constraints.

MD-AirComp+ is an adaptive quantization method designed for blind massive digital over-the-air computation, improving the effectiveness of federated edge learning by addressing channel estimation inaccuracies.

An iterative framework for monocular depth refinement that leverages Neural Radiance Fields to enhance depth estimation accuracy through a combination of initial estimates and uncertainty-aware training.
A non-autoregressive ASR framework that leverages masked diffusion models for parallel decoding, addressing the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency in speech recognition tasks.
A multi-granularity sequential modeling approach using hierarchical diffusion models for micro-video recommendation, designed to capture user preferences amidst noisy multimodal content.
A mathematical framework used to model strategic interactions in large populations of agents, where each agent's decision-making is influenced by the average behavior of the population.

A variant of reinforcement learning that scales multi-agent systems by summarizing agent interactions through a mean action, allowing for reduced dependency on individual agent actions and accommodating asynchrony.
A framework that enhances action generation for vision-language-action tasks by resolving noise-induced issues and reducing latency in generating action chunks.
A model for mean estimation where an adversary can replace a fraction of clean samples with shifted versions of the base distribution, affecting sample complexity.
A novel one-step nonparallel voice conversion model that utilizes mean flows for efficient training and inference, improving speech quality and speaker similarity without requiring pretraining.

MEBM-Phoneme is a neural decoder for phoneme classification from MEG signals, utilizing a short-term multi-scale convolutional module and a convolutional attention layer for refined feature aggregation.
MEBM-Speech is a multi-scale enhanced neural decoder designed for robust speech activity detection from magnetoencephalography (MEG) signals, integrating various temporal modeling mechanisms.

A cooperative architecture that leverages Mobile Edge Computing to enhance localization accuracy for automated driving systems by aggregating GPS data from multiple road users and applying probabilistic filters.
A multi-layer non-terrestrial network architecture that integrates multi-access edge computing capabilities to facilitate task offloading in resource-constrained IoT deployments.

MedBeads is an agent-native data infrastructure designed for trustworthy medical AI, where clinical events are stored as immutable 'Beads', addressing context mismatch in electronic medical records and enhancing auditability.

A multi-agent framework designed for full-cycle clinical diagnosis, utilizing a dynamic specialist recruitment mechanism to adaptively assemble clinical agents based on patient-specific symptoms and examination results.
An open-weight, general-purpose vision-language model for biomedicine that integrates clinical-text reasoning with visual data, designed for on-premises deployment to ensure patient privacy.
A general aggregation procedure for leave-one-out prediction that provides a data-dependent measure of generalization across arbitrary datasets and losses.

MERIT is a compiler transformation technique that eliminates branches in intermediate representation instructions to mitigate performance penalties caused by branch mispredictions in modern processors.
Memix is a far-memory system that employs deep-learning-driven prefetching techniques to optimize data fetching from far-memory to local-memory, enhancing application performance in data centers.
A memory-augmented multi-modal LLM agent designed for phishing URL detection, utilizing episodic memories to enhance decision-making against both recurring and novel phishing threats.

An online memory allocator that dynamically maintains object assignments to non-overlapping memory chunks, supporting updates with a total movement overhead defined relative to the size of the object being inserted or deleted.

Memory tiering is a system that optimizes memory usage by organizing data into multiple tiers based on access frequency, aiming to enhance performance by placing frequently accessed data in faster memory tiers.

A novel approach for solving the flexible job-shop scheduling problem (FJSP) that combines deep reinforcement learning with improvement-based methods to iteratively explore neighboring solutions, aiming for near-optimal scheduling in dynamic production environments.

This technique focuses on recognizing mixed emotions by learning from multi-modal physiological and behavioral signals, addressing the complexities of simultaneous affective states in real-world scenarios.

A unified framework for establishing memory lower bounds in multi-pass streaming algorithms, particularly for detecting planted structures like cliques in graphs. It provides insights into memory efficiency for various computational problems.
A defense framework against memory extraction attacks in LLMs, utilizing optimized honeypots to mislead attackers while remaining inconspicuous to legitimate users.
A reference voice bank containing approximately 18,000 high-quality utterances from 124 speakers across multiple MENA countries, designed for training audio large language models (AudioLLMs).
A divide-and-conquer framework designed to overcome GPU memory limitations in neural visual geometry tasks by partitioning unordered image collections into manageable subsets for processing.

MergePipe is a budget-aware parameter management system designed for scalable merging of large language models (LLMs), addressing inefficiencies in existing implementations by treating model parameters in a structured manner.

A system that provides public-key verifiable speech provenance using perceptual fingerprints and Merkle commitments, allowing for localized detection of alterations in speech while maintaining cryptographic proof.
An automated framework for extracting and formalizing mathematical statements in quantum computation, converting them into verified Lean 4 code and back to LaTeX for review.
MerLin is an open-source discovery engine for photonic and hybrid quantum machine learning, integrating linear optical circuit simulations into PyTorch and scikit-learn workflows.
An innovative shape optimization algorithm that employs a probabilistic representation of the shape derivative evaluated through Monte Carlo sampling, eliminating the need for mesh dependency.

The Mesoscale Structure of Innovation Networks refers to the intermediate organizational patterns that emerge from collaboration among inventors and firms, influencing the dynamics of innovation in various domains.

A framework that facilitates communication between distributed systems through message passing, providing essential features like transaction support and resource management for modern cloud applications.
MCE is a bi-level framework that optimizes context inputs for large language models by co-evolving context artifacts and engineering skills, moving beyond static context optimization methods.
A dynamic optimization approach that utilizes reinforcement learning to automate the detection of environmental variations in dynamic optimization problems, enhancing adaptability and performance.
This framework achieves zero-cost specialization in programming languages by utilizing compile-time metaprogramming to repurpose monomorphization without modifying the host compiler.
A next-generation middleware architecture designed for adaptive and scalable robotic systems, enhancing integration and performance while ensuring cross-platform compatibility.
A low-cost passive acoustic metasurface designed for underwater localization, enabling efficient acoustic sensing without the need for large arrays or synchronized anchors.
This architecture infers metapopulation mobility graphs from time-series data, addressing the challenge of joint inference of epidemic parameters and mobility networks in epidemic models.
A generalizable Root Cause Analysis framework for cloud-native systems that integrates meta causal knowledge to improve scalability and adaptability across various system topologies.
A method for performing addition operations on uncertain values represented as intervals, aimed at minimizing imprecision caused by unstable bits in digital circuits.
A computing paradigm that manipulates potential energy landscapes to achieve energy-efficient information processing, focusing on the control of metastable energy minima.
A framework that integrates metasurfaces with neural networks to enable physical-layer computation for lightweight, ultra-low latency edge inference in wireless networks.
An extension of Answer Set Programming that incorporates quantitative temporal constraints, enabling the expression of deadlines and durations while addressing scalability challenges in grounding.

A systematic framework for analyzing cooperation in heterogeneous multi-agent systems, focusing on metrics that characterize efficiency, coordination, fairness, and sensitivity in destructive foraging tasks.
The first benchmark designed for multilingual hardware code completion at the repository level, consisting of training and evaluation datasets targeting various hardware design coding styles.

MI$^2$DAS is a multi-layer intrusion detection framework designed for securing Industrial IoT networks, integrating anomaly-based hierarchical traffic pooling and open-set recognition to effectively identify both known and unknown threats.
MIBURI is an interactive gesture synthesis framework for Embodied Conversational Agents (ECAs) that generates expressive body gestures in real-time, enhancing natural human-like interaction.

An open-source simulator and benchmark suite for evaluating magnetic rolling microrobots in contact-rich environments, featuring an overdamped interaction model and mechanisms for simulating stick-slip effects.

Models that simulate individual-level behaviors and outcomes to analyze the distributional impacts of policies, such as carbon taxation, by accounting for variations in tax designs and population characteristics.
An architecture designed for implementing linear MIMO processing in the analog domain, accounting for mutual coupling effects in radio frequency signals.

Mosaic Input-Specific Differentiable Architecture Search (MIDAS) is a novel approach to neural architecture search that employs dynamic, input-specific parameters computed via self-attention, enhancing the robustness of architecture selection.
