A compositional approach to song generation that conditions accompaniment on symbolic vocal-melody MIDI, enhancing rhythmic and harmonic alignment between singing and instrumentation.

The first instruction-following multimodal large language model designed for symbolic music understanding, integrating a MIDI encoder with a two-stage pipeline for feature alignment and instruction tuning.

MIDI-informed singing accompaniment generation (MIDI-SAG) is a method that conditions musical accompaniment on symbolic vocal-melody MIDI to enhance rhythmic and harmonic alignment in song generation.
MigMate is an open-source VS Code IDE plugin that automates the library migration process in Python projects by building on the capabilities of the MigrateLib command-line tool.

MILD is a proactive framework for multi-intent networks that reformulates intent assurance from reactive drift detection to fixed-horizon failure prediction, employing a gated disambiguation module to identify root-cause intents.
A memory-optimized OLTP engine designed for low-latency object storage, enhancing commit operations with strong consistency and high durability in environments like Amazon S3 Express One Zone.
A unified image restoration framework that employs a dual-level Mixture-of-Experts architecture combined with a pretrained diffusion model to effectively handle various degradation types in images.

The Min-Sum Uniform Coverage Problem involves coordinating a swarm of autonomous mobile robots to achieve a uniformly spaced configuration that minimizes the total distance traveled, operating under the Look-Compute-Move model.

A problem that seeks to compute an allocation that is envy-free up to any item (EFx) while minimizing costs associated with resource allocation. It is proven to be NP-hard even with two agents, highlighting its computational complexity.
A framework for reconstructing dynamic visual perception from EEG signals, addressing challenges of single modality alignment and data scarcity through multimodal integration.
A closed-loop framework that utilizes EEG signals as feedback to optimize visual stimuli for brain modulation, enhancing the design of images to elicit specific neural responses.
A two-stage contrastive training framework designed to optimize graded relevance in e-commerce search retrieval, enhancing the performance of semantic text embeddings for complex queries.
A unified distributed concurrency control mechanism for geo-replicated databases that aims to enhance performance for serializable transactions while minimizing over-coordination and latency issues.

An automated framework that converts Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to High-Level Synthesis (HLS) for efficient deployment on edge computing platforms, focusing on low-latency and real-time processing.

The Minimal Internet Key Exchange (IKE) protocol is a lightweight cryptographic protocol designed for secure key exchange in resource-constrained environments. It aims to reduce the footprint of traditional IKE protocols while maintaining security.
A novel approach that combines efficient re-ranking with cross-encoders, reducing inference costs while maintaining high ranking effectiveness in information retrieval tasks.
Sets in incidence graphs that are minimal in redundancy, with the problem of enumerating such sets being computationally challenging unless P=NP.
A compliance control technique that enables safe physical interaction using only motor current or voltage signals, eliminating the need for force sensors and reducing system complexity.

A theoretical result demonstrating that standard Transformers can achieve the minimax optimal rate in nonparametric regression tasks for Hölder target functions, indicating their effectiveness in approximation.
An NP-hard problem focused on finding a spanning tree in a graph that minimizes the maximum degree of any vertex in that tree, with recent advancements breaking the O(mn) time barrier.
An optimal energy management policy for race cars that is real-time capable and human-implementable, derived using Pontryagin's Minimum Principle and characterized by a bang-bang control structure.
A compact, UAV-deployable tracked ground vehicle designed for exploring confined environments, featuring dual articulated arms and integrated sensing capabilities for enhanced autonomy.

An approach to virtual machine placement in cloud computing that formulates the problem as a dynamic vector bin packing task, aiming to minimize the total usage time of physical machines under various conditions.
MinUsageTime DVBP is an algorithmic framework for virtual machine placement in cloud computing, aimed at minimizing the total usage time of physical machines under various operational settings.
MIP Candy is a modular PyTorch framework tailored for medical image processing, offering a flexible pipeline for data loading, training, inference, and evaluation.
A novel framework that addresses the missing-MRI problem by synthesizing 3D anatomical scans from sparse tabular records for Alzheimer's disease prediction.
A framework that synthesizes 3D anatomical MRI scans from sparse electronic health records, addressing the challenge of modality missingness in Alzheimer's disease prediction.
A compiler and runtime system that transforms multi-GPU model inference into a single high-performance megakernel, optimizing GPU resource utilization through advanced task graph representations.

MOST is a novel tiering-based approach that combines the load balancing advantages of mirroring with the space-efficiency of tiering, dynamically mirroring hot data across storage tiers to optimize bandwidth utilization under I/O-intensive workloads.
A benchmark designed to evaluate the performance of Multimodal Large Language Models in generating missing modalities for product listings on e-commerce platforms.

This framework integrates heterogeneous clinical, radiological, and histopathological information for survival prediction in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer, addressing challenges posed by missing modalities in multimodal deep learning.

MIST-RL (Mutation-based Incremental Suite Testing via Reinforcement Learning) is a framework that optimizes unit test generation by focusing on utility rather than quantity to enhance fault detection.

A Mixture-of-Experts framework for Emotion Recognition in Conversations (ERC) that decouples modality-specific context modeling and multimodal information fusion, utilizing large language models for enhanced utterance-level embeddings.
A VR-based collaborative game designed to explore ADHD through asymmetric co-play, translating ADHD behavioral patterns into shared momentum for youth collaboration.
A strategy in competitive markets where retailers offer mixed bundling alongside price-matching guarantees to optimize demand capture and customer loyalty.

Mixed Choice is a core construct within multiparty session types that allows transient inconsistencies in protocol states among distributed participants while ensuring eventual consistency across all participants.

M.term: Mixed Choice in Asynchronous Multiparty Session Types
M.definition: A framework that introduces asynchronous mixed choice in multiparty session types, allowing transient inconsistencies while ensuring eventual consistency among distributed participants.

M.term: Mixed Reality System for Childbirth Training
M.definition: A mixed reality system that combines virtual guidance with tactile manikin interaction for childbirth training, enhancing learning efficiency and preserving authentic haptic feedback.

A framework that addresses cooperation dilemmas in mixed-motive Markov games, proposing a novel fair objective to balance individual interests with collective outcomes, enhancing cooperation in heterogeneous groups.
A complex task that involves detecting misinformation from multiple sources and modalities, leveraging vision-language models (VLMs) to address the challenges of zero-shot settings.
A deterministic, closed-form toolkit developed for bounding and approximating the differential entropy of mixture distributions directly from component parameters.

A framework that utilizes multiple expert agents based on large language models to assist in interactive planning by providing proxy answers, facilitating the generation of structured Behavior Trees for long-horizon robot execution.
An architecture that decomposes prediction tasks into specialized expert sub-networks, utilizing a finite information rate gating mechanism to optimize communication and generalization trade-offs.

A framework for applying machine learning on heterogeneous, edge, and quantum hardware specifically tailored for particle physics experiments, addressing challenges in data acquisition and real-time inference.
A modular framework designed for optimizing compilers, facilitating the testing of domain-specific languages through random program generation and differential testing.
A random program generator specifically designed to test and evaluate compiler optimizations within the Multi-Level Intermediate Representation (MLIR) framework.

A taxonomy defining four interaction modes (Human-Only, Human-Lead, AI-Lead, and Co-Evolution) for sketch-based design ideation with multimodal large language models, highlighting the allocation of creative responsibility.

MLLM-VADStory is a domain knowledge-guided multimodal large language model framework designed for quantifying and generating insights into video ad storylines, segmenting ads into functional units based on their communicative intent.
MM-Mem is a pyramidal multimodal memory architecture that aims to improve long-horizon video understanding by effectively managing context and memory in multimodal large language models.
A multimodal poisoning attack targeting the metadata of image-text entries in retrieval-augmented generation systems, designed to influence both retrieval and generation processes.

MM-ResGNN (Multi-Modal Sensing Residual-Corrected Graph Neural Network) is a framework designed for millimeter-wave path loss prediction in vehicular communications, utilizing an environment sensing path loss graph to model transmission links.

MMA-RAG^T is an inference-time control framework for multimodal agentic retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) that utilizes a Modular Trust Agent to maintain an approximate belief state. It addresses adversarial strategies by treating adversarial intent as a latent variable.
A novel instance-level protocol introduced for evaluating the reasoning quality of audio reasoning models, focusing on the factuality and logic of reasoning chains.
MMH-Planner is a multi-mode hybrid trajectory planning method for UAVs that dynamically selects optimal planning models based on real-time spatial awareness to enhance planning efficiency.
A comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the multimodal multi-image reasoning capabilities of large language models in real-life scenarios, consisting of 2,646 multiple-choice questions.
A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating large language models on time series understanding and reasoning, featuring a hierarchical taxonomy of tasks and 2,424 time series question answering pairs.

MNN-AECS is an energy-efficient engine-level system solution for decoding large language models (LLMs) on mobile devices, utilizing Adaptive Energy-Centric Core Selection to optimize resource usage without requiring root access or OS modifications.
A framework for multi-objective multi-agent cooperative decision-making that employs deep reinforcement learning to address conflicting objectives in complex environments.
An approach to improve energy efficiency and performance of large language model inference on mobile devices by optimizing dynamic voltage and frequency scaling governors across CPU, GPU, and memory.
An open smartphone-based microscopy dataset for food mold detection and classification, containing 4,941 handheld microscopy images across 11 food types and various conditions.
A transportation model that integrates various forms of transport services into a single accessible on-demand service, enhancing user convenience and optimizing resource use.

Modality-Decoupled Direct Preference Optimization is a framework designed to mitigate cross-modal hallucinations in omni-modal large language models by introducing modality-aware regularization terms.
A neural network architecture designed to implement differentiable modal logic, enabling the learning of knowledge structures and relationships in multi-agent systems.
A training framework that enhances modality immunity in multimodal systems by intentionally collapsing modality information during training to improve robustness.

M.term: Modality Gap-Driven Subspace Alignment Training Paradigm
M.definition: This training paradigm addresses the Modality Gap in multimodal large language models by characterizing its geometric shape to improve alignment and scaling of models.

A technique used in a decoder-only Conformer model for automatic speech recognition that employs disjoint expert pools for speech and text, utilizing hard routing and top-1 selection for efficient processing.
A framework designed to mitigate cross-modal hallucinations in omni-modal large language models by introducing modality-aware regularization terms that enhance modality grounding.

ModalPatch is a plug-and-play module for robust multi-modal 3D object detection that enables reliable detection under arbitrary modality-drop scenarios, addressing challenges posed by transient data interruptions.
A channel access scheme developed for the NR-V2X sidelink standard, allowing connected and autonomous vehicles to autonomously select radio resources for transmission under dynamic conditions.
An architectural framework that transforms computer environments into contextual spaces comprehensible by language models, enhancing the interaction between AI agents and computer interfaces.
A sampling-based approach for Model Predictive Control that optimizes planning and control schemes for autonomous mobile robots, particularly effective for GPU acceleration.

This framework generalizes the concept of model restrictiveness to a broader range of economic models, allowing for the computation of restrictiveness in infinite-dimensional settings using Gaussian process priors and Bayesian nonparametrics.
A lightweight weight-space ensembling technique that averages checkpoints from a single training trajectory to improve model robustness and generalization in low-resource settings.
A computational framework that combines exact simulation of singular kernels with multi-factor approximations to improve the calibration and pricing accuracy of the rough Bergomi model.

A tree-based semi-structured document analysis system that addresses the challenges of natural language question answering over semi-structured documents by improving element extraction and semantic context preservation.
A reconfigurable soft robotic arm architecture that allows for multi-segment stacking and independent control of segments, enhancing adaptability for various applications.
A design approach that allows for the reconfiguration of robots using modular parts to adapt to changing human needs and values over time.

This framework for scientific visualization and simulation in the Java ecosystem emphasizes architectural separation between visualization layers and simulation engines, enhancing modularity and reducing dependency coupling.
A re-engineered version of probabilistic bits (p-bits) that allows for configurable probabilistic activation functions in neural networks, enhancing flexibility in modeling.
A framework for spoofing-aware speaker verification that integrates automatic speaker verification and countermeasures through non-linear fusion, optimizing performance against adversarial attacks.
A framework for constructing spiking neural networks that emphasizes modularity and efficiency, addressing challenges in data and energy consumption.
A framework that modularizes pose estimation by attributing errors to specific sources and applying targeted mitigation strategies, enhancing robustness in robotic manipulation tasks.
A principle emphasizing the importance of modularity in both natural and artificial intelligence, facilitating efficient learning and generalization by organizing computational processes.

MAR is a two-stage framework that optimizes large language models by integrating State Space Models for efficient sequence modeling and applying activation sparsification to reduce computational costs.
A multi-objective evolutionary algorithm designed for neural architecture search that utilizes bi-population strategies and uniform sampling to improve exploration and diversity in the search space.

MoECLIP is a Mixture-of-Experts architecture tailored for zero-shot anomaly detection, which allows for patch-specialized processing of input data, thereby improving the model's ability to detect anomalies in unseen categories.

Motion Group-aware Gaussian Forecasting is a framework for long-term scene extrapolation that employs motion-aware Gaussian grouping and optimization to maintain physically consistent motion in dynamic scenes.
Mogan STEM is a WYSIWYG structured editor that addresses the limitations of TeX in compilation efficiency and user experience, offering features like fast rendering and on-demand plugin loading.

A communication framework that employs non-linear, cross-reactive sensors for detecting and transmitting information about molecule mixtures, addressing limitations of traditional molecular communication systems.

A genetic algorithm-based framework for molecular optimization that utilizes multiple critics, including an ensemble of Gaussian process surrogates and a pretrained text-molecule aligned encoder, to efficiently rank candidate molecules before oracle evaluations.

An analysis of collective behavior exhibited by AI agents on a social media platform, revealing statistical patterns similar to human communities, including heavy-tailed activity distributions and power-law scaling.

MoltGraph is a longitudinal temporal graph dataset designed for detecting coordinated-agent behavior in social platforms, capturing heterogeneous interactions and temporal drift to analyze visibility manipulation strategies.
A Bayesian framework that distinguishes between mean and variance causal graphs, enhancing interpretability in causal discovery from heteroscedastic data.
A sequence of relaxations for polynomial optimization problems that converges in finitely many steps under certain convexity conditions, facilitating the solution of non-convex objectives.
A set of vertices in a graph that, when any edge is removed, ensures that the distance between some two vertices in the set increases, used for monitoring networks against communication failures.

MonkeyTree is a system designed to mitigate network congestion in multi-tenant GPU clusters through job-migration based defragmentation, addressing limitations of traditional network-layer techniques.
This technique utilizes Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to estimate 3D object positions from monocular RGB images, enhancing human-robot interaction through natural language input.

This system allows developers to specify sequencing constraints on smart contract function calls, balancing expressivity with the tractability of block production by enforcing global priorities on transactions.

A theoretical framework that determines the necessity of coordination in organizational tasks based on whether tasks are non-monotonic, providing insights into resource allocation in multi-agent systems.

A method for testing the monotonicity of real-valued functions on directed acyclic graphs, providing lower bounds for query complexity against non-adaptive testers, enhancing understanding of function behavior in graph structures.

A novel algorithm that utilizes Monte Carlo methods for effective step-size selection in active noise control systems, enhancing convergence speed and adaptability to varying environmental conditions.

M.term: Monte Carlo Rendering to Diffusion Curves
M.definition: A method for generating vector graphics from noisy samples produced by a Monte Carlo renderer, allowing for robust extraction of editable vector images with shading information.

An ergodic streaming encoder for automatic speech recognition (ASR) designed for latency-critical applications, optimizing low time-to-first-token and high transcription accuracy on resource-constrained devices.

M.term: More-Than-Human Representation in Crisis Resilience Planning
M.definition: An approach that incorporates non-human species and ecological systems into participatory crisis resilience planning, utilizing design probes such as conversational agents to facilitate discussions on non-human perspectives.
A class of best-response schemes that utilize Moreau smoothing to efficiently learn equilibria in nonsmooth and nonconvex games with expectation-valued objectives.
A post-training framework designed to align agentic language models for safe multi-step tool use, addressing challenges in sequential decision-making.

A novel end-to-end discrete audio tokenizer designed to enhance audio processing capabilities in large language models, overcoming limitations of fixed inductive biases found in previous architectures.
A framework that filters events triggered by instantaneous motion and ego-motion in real time, improving segmentation accuracy and reducing unnecessary event processing in event cameras.
A motion-centric, gradient-based data attribution framework that isolates temporal dynamics from static appearances in video generation, enabling efficient computation of motion-specific influences.
An open-source framework for scalable and synchronized video-audio generation, aiming to improve the quality and efficiency of audio-visual content creation.
A representation of permutations in compressed space that optimizes query time by utilizing long contiguously permuted intervals, significantly enhancing the performance of compressed text indexes.
MoveContinuousSkill is an optimized planning method for skill-based robot programming that enhances motion sequence efficiency by integrating motion sequence optimization into the execution process.
A framework that synthesizes diverse motion patterns from isolated audio events to create controlled training data for models focused on spatial audio understanding.
A hybrid Lagrangian/Eulerian method that eliminates particle-based quadrature during solve time, improving performance by treating particles as carriers of kinematic state and material history.

MPU is a privacy-preserving framework for machine unlearning in large language models, introducing a method for generating multiple perturbed model copies to facilitate secure unlearning without compromising client data privacy.
A privacy-preserving framework for machine unlearning that utilizes multiple perturbed copies of model instances to allow clients to execute unlearning without disclosing sensitive data.
A motion correction system for MRI that utilizes inertial sensors to estimate orientation without integration, effectively eliminating drift and improving image quality during scans.

M.term: MRD Codes Construction via Circular-Shift Operations
M.definition: A method for constructing maximum rank distance codes that simplifies encoding by relying solely on circular-shift operations over finite fields, avoiding complex arithmetic associated with larger fields.

A multiscale transformer-based architecture designed for no-reference image quality assessment, featuring a dual-branch structure that captures fine and coarse details through spatial and channel attention mechanisms.
An OS-level scheduler that enhances GPU multitasking by proactively preparing working sets, addressing the limitations of high bandwidth memory capacity.
Problems that can be defined using Monadic Second-Order logic, with complexity lower bounds established for binary structures encoded by circuits under various restrictions.
MSVBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multi-shot video generation, featuring hierarchical scripts and reference images to assess long-form coherence and narrative appeal.

MTAVG-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multi-talker dialogue-centric audio-video generation, addressing challenges like identity drift and audio-visual misalignment in synthesized multi-participant dialogues.

MTL-VQA is a multi-task learning framework for no-reference video quality assessment that utilizes full-reference metrics as supervisory signals to learn perceptually meaningful features without human labels.
A multi-kernel adaptation framework for few-shot adaptation of Large Audio-Language Models, combining instruction-tuning and contrastive pretraining methods to enhance model performance on new tasks.

MulCovFuzz is a multi-component coverage-guided greybox fuzzer specifically designed for testing 5G protocols, which dynamically collects coverage information to enhance the robustness of security testing in complex mobile networks.

A security approach that integrates multiple protective measures, such as Web Application Firewalls, rate limiting, and dynamic blacklisting, to effectively mitigate low rate DDoS attacks in containerized environments.
An attention mechanism that employs multi-scale grid sampling to improve performance in various vision tasks, though it faces challenges with memory access patterns.
A framework for SQL injection detection that utilizes a multi-agent system to generate diverse requests and mediate responses, enhancing the context for improved detection performance.

A learning paradigm that aims to derive optimal policies from expert demonstrations in multi-agent environments, with a focus on understanding the exploitability of learned policies in relation to Nash equilibria.
An architecture that enables natural interaction between human planners and AI planners, facilitating iterative reasoning and elicitation processes through conversational explanations.
Trading systems that utilize large language models across multiple agents to generate trading strategies, with performance influenced by the communication structure among agents.

This framework integrates fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) with evidence retrieval and bias checks to enhance the reliability of clinical question answering, addressing challenges in verification and confidence signaling.
This framework facilitates trajectory design for UAV fleets in vehicular networks, allowing dynamic repositioning of aerial base stations to maintain connectivity in urban environments.

This model provides a comprehensive framework for studying affective polarization in social media by simulating partisan divisions and integrating various definitions of affective polarization for better interoperability across studies.
A generative-to-decision framework that combines Gaussian Hidden Markov Models with a diffusion generator to produce regime-conditioned scenarios for improved portfolio decisions under regime shifts.
A novel framework for coordinating the movement of multiple autonomous agents over mixed graphs, focusing on asynchronous settings and treating conflicts as soft constraints rather than hard ones.

A system that combines Control Barrier Functions to facilitate safe and efficient lane changes for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles by capturing multi-agent interactions through constructed interaction topologies.
An architectural design for psychotherapy chatbots that utilizes a multi-agent system to adhere to therapeutic protocols, enhancing the structure of dialogue in therapeutic settings.

A planning framework that formulates Signal Temporal Logic (STL) tasks as optimization problems, utilizing penalty functions and Block-Coordinate Gradient Descent to manage multi-agent decision-making complexity.
A computational framework for ensuring fairness in $k$-nearest neighbor ($k$-NN) queries across multiple attributes, imposing count constraints for proportional representation among protected groups.
The first public dataset for conversion rate prediction that features labels generated from multiple attribution mechanisms, facilitating the development of multi-attribution learning approaches.
An approach in integrated sensing and communication that utilizes frequency range 3 (FR3) for effective sensing across non-contiguous sub-bands, addressing dense multipath components.
A framework for interpreting multi-branch deep neural networks used in audio anti-spoofing, focusing on the decision dynamics of individual branches under various spoofing attacks.
A technique that utilizes pinching antennas for multi-cell interference management, significantly reducing power consumption in wireless communication systems.
A technique for improving speech emotion recognition in complex acoustic environments, utilizing a multi-channel approach that integrates deep learning methods for dereverberation and separation.

Multi-CoLoR is a framework for context-aware localization and reasoning across multi-language codebases, enhancing the ability of large language models to navigate complex repositories by considering organizational context and structural relationships.
A scheduling model that decomposes end-to-end timing constraints in autonomous driving systems into local relative deadlines for each sub-DAG, enhancing timing guarantees in complex systems.
A learning paradigm that addresses the challenges of training classifiers from heterogeneous data sources while exploiting shared structures to reduce sample complexity.

An extension of Markov Decision Processes that incorporates multiple probabilistic transition functions for different environments, allowing for more complex decision-making scenarios where the environment is not directly observable.

This framework facilitates seamless cooperation among diverse devices in low-altitude wireless networks (LAWNs), enabling efficient execution of various network functions by assigning specific roles to each device.

A low-complexity framework that utilizes multi-head self-attention (MHSA) to directly estimate the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) in user-centric non-terrestrial networks, reducing computational overhead.

M.term: Multi-Hit Cancer Driver Set Cover Problem (MHCDSCP)
M.definition: A binary classification problem that aims to identify gene combinations driving cancer by maximizing coverage of tumor samples while minimizing coverage of normal samples, formalized as a set cover problem.

A statistical model designed for analyzing multi-layer directed networks, accommodating distinct community structures for sender and receiver nodes. It facilitates the estimation of asymmetric community numbers through a novel goodness-of-fit test.

This framework integrates heterogeneous LLM-based query systems to facilitate semantic querying over multi-modal data, addressing the challenges posed by unstructured data types such as text, images, and videos.

A framework that integrates multiple sensing modalities and employs fusion learning techniques to enhance beamforming in mmWave communication for connected vehicles, addressing challenges posed by dynamic environments.
A novel approach that combines text and audio features to improve topic segmentation in spoken content, leveraging a fine-tuned text encoder and a Siamese audio encoder.

A heuristic designed to optimize the co-allocation of tasks and resources in multicore real-time systems, minimizing resource usage while ensuring schedulability under a preemptive EDF scheduling policy.

A framework that alternates between Speaker Recognition and Word Recognition passes to enhance transcription accuracy and speaker attribution in automatic speech recognition for medical conversations.
A computational framework for optimizing transport problems over multiple periods, featuring theoretical convergence rates and algorithmic improvements for financial applications.
A sentence transformer model utilized for generating semantic similarity scores to predict software vulnerabilities from unstructured attack descriptions in cybersecurity news.

MANDATE is a novel architecture designed for graph fraud detection that addresses the limitations of traditional graph neural networks by incorporating multi-scale neighborhood awareness to enhance global modeling capabilities.
A framework inspired by biological homeostasis that integrates regulation across multiple temporal scales to enhance the robustness and efficiency of artificial neural networks.
A robust framework for fact-checking that utilizes multiple sources and agents to retrieve accurate evidence, addressing the challenges of misinformation through advanced retrieval techniques.

This taxonomy categorizes multi-speaker conversational audio deepfakes into partial and full manipulations, addressing the emerging security threats posed by realistic and accessible audio deepfake technologies.

MSAF is a novel module designed for song aesthetics evaluation that utilizes bidirectional cross-attention mechanisms to enhance the understanding of song attributes and their impact on listener perception.

M.term: Multi-Task and Multi-Stage Query Rewriting Framework
M.definition: A framework that utilizes large language models (LLMs) for enhancing e-commerce search by rewriting user queries through multiple tasks and stages to improve relevance and conversion rates.

M.term: Multi-Task Deep Learning for Delivery Delay Prediction
M.definition: A multi-task deep learning model designed to predict delivery delay durations in complex logistics networks, effectively handling imbalanced data where delayed shipments are rare but critical.
An approach that emphasizes scaling the number of tasks rather than samples per task in model-based reinforcement learning, leveraging shared world models for effective online learning.
An enhanced version of the A* pathfinding algorithm that integrates multi-threading with Recast-based mesh generation and trajectory smoothing for scalable navigation in dynamic game environments.
A class of error-correcting codes derived from multi-twisted Reed-Solomon codes, characterized by their minimum distance properties and efficient decoding methods for correcting multiple errors.
A method for efficiently compressing multi-vector document representations in information retrieval, addressing the computational and storage costs associated with late interaction paradigms.
A technique for improving multilingual embeddings by aligning multiple translations of text across different languages, enhancing cross-lingual representation in natural language understanding tasks.
A dataset and benchmark for hierarchical activity recognition from long-form audio, featuring annotations at multiple semantic levels and supporting fine-grained understanding of human activities.
An extension of omniprediction to multiclass settings, where the algorithm provides suboptimality bounds against a potentially infinite set of comparator predictors.
A directed acyclic graph framework designed to enhance digital forensic education by mapping the relationships between various skills and competencies necessary for cybersecurity professionals.
An innovative indexing technique for large-scale recommendation systems that integrates the learning of item embeddings and their indices, optimizing retrieval quality for newly created items.
M-DMCA is a novel algorithm for efficient node discovery in cognitive radio networks, featuring dual-channel selection and a three-way handshake mechanism to reduce rendezvous time.

MLMQ is a novel data structure designed to optimize the Single-Source Shortest Path (SSSP) problem on GPUs by distributing multiple queues across the GPU's multi-level parallelism and memory hierarchy, reducing synchronization overhead and improving adaptivity to diverse inputs.
A framework for safe robot grasping that employs a dual-patch optimization scheme to reconcile discrepancies between RGB and depth modalities.
A reference-free data selection pipeline for accent adaptation in ASR systems that leverages multimodal consistency to improve training data quality without relying on labeled data.

dLLMs are a class of models that leverage diffusion processes in multimodal contexts, offering advantages such as bidirectional attention and parallel generation, positioning them as competitive alternatives to traditional autoregressive models.
A scalable framework that combines automated extraction of visual, audio, and textual features to analyze their joint contributions in short-form video content.
An integrated approach that combines structured credit variables, climate panel data, and unstructured textual narratives using architectures like LSTM and GRU for credit risk assessment.

This theory evaluates cross-modal reasoning abilities in multimodal large language models by addressing the shortcomings of current benchmarks that contain shortcut questions, ensuring reliable assessments of model performance.

Advanced language models capable of interpreting visual data and providing explanations in natural language, enhancing the analysis of graph data and aligning computational measures with human perception.
A sampling mechanism that integrates photometric, semantic, and geometric priors to enhance the robustness of hierarchical 3D Gaussian Splatting in sparse-view novel view synthesis.
A problem in graph theory where the goal is to determine if a graph contains a multipacking of a specified size, with implications for computational complexity.

A collaborative quantum protocol that allows three or more mutually distrustful users to establish a shared secret key, structured along axes of network architecture, quantum resources, and security models.

M.term: Multiparty Session Type with Mixed Choice
M.definition: A framework that allows transient inconsistencies in protocol states among distributed participants while ensuring eventual consistency, enhancing the robustness of multiparty communication protocols.

Multipath delay estimation involves leveraging channel state information from non-contiguous Wi-Fi bands to improve delay resolution for ranging and sensing applications, particularly in environments with frequency gaps.
A technique for Approximate k Nearest Neighbor (AKNN) search that involves building multiple sub-indexes separately to improve search efficiency in high-dimensional vector databases.

MERA is a tensor network framework that establishes a rigorous equivalence with paraunitary filter banks, enabling the learning of adaptive wavelets while enforcing exact orthogonality for efficient signal compression.

This design framework utilizes orthogonal time-frequency space (OTFS) modulation for integrated sensing and communication in vehicular networks, enhancing target localization and velocity estimation through Kalman filtering.
An advanced model for analyzing complex multivariate event data that incorporates spatial information into the dynamics of event occurrence.

A zero-shot multi-agent system for vulnerability detection that utilizes an ensemble of agents to achieve high recall rates without the need for fine-tuning, surpassing traditional models in performance on benchmark datasets.
A novel framework that integrates the Muon optimizer into scalable generative recommendation systems, focusing on orthogonalized momentum updates to enhance training efficiency.
An open lexical dataset containing over 96,000 word-definition pairs in Arabic, created using a hybrid extraction pipeline to ensure accuracy and clarity.
An open-source, run-centric platform designed for the multimodal safety evaluation of large language models, integrating automatic cross-modal payload generation and multi-turn attack algorithms.

MuseAgent is an interactive multimodal agent that enhances large language models with structured symbolic representations from music scores and performance audio, enabling grounded reasoning in music understanding.
A concept describing the auditory similarity between two music fragments that differ in waveforms, with a method proposed for generating musical metamers using time-frequency scattering.
A manually annotated corpus designed to capture user intent behind music queries, categorizing musical descriptors to improve the effectiveness of recommendation systems.
A dataset that provides semantically rich natural language descriptions of music, aimed at improving alignment in music representation learning tasks.

A semantically rich dataset designed for music representation learning, capturing natural language descriptions of music to enhance alignment between text and audio for various retrieval and generation tasks.
This methodology tests the accuracy of LLM-generated code summaries by injecting targeted mutations into the code and verifying if the summary reflects the actual behavior of the code.
An automated method proposed to quantify competitive relationships among open-source software projects, helping to understand their survival dynamics in a competitive landscape.

MuxTune is a fine-tuning system that enables resource-efficient concurrent execution of multiple Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) tasks in multi-tenant datacenters, addressing GPU underutilization and communication delays.
Multi-View Subject-Consistent Video Generation (MV-S2V) synthesizes videos from multiple reference views, enhancing subject consistency in video generation beyond single-view constraints.

Multi-View Visual-Semantic Representation (MViR) is a framework for fake news detection that captures multi-view visual-semantic features using pyramid dilated convolution and integrates these features through a dedicated fusion module.
A hybrid, weight-stationary Compute-in-Memory accelerator designed for efficient inference of large short-sequence Transformer models, utilizing ultra-dense Charge-Trap Transistors.

F.definition: A computational problem related to graph isolation, which is NP-complete when the graph F is connected, expanding the classical graph domination problem.
A method for integrating diverse data formats into a unified access framework, allowing SPARQL queries to target various data structures beyond traditional RDF.

A method for evaluating the quality of facial images captured under unconstrained conditions, focusing on both visual quality and identity-preserving features essential for reliable identity verification in surveillance.

F.term: Facial Restoration via Alignment, Entanglement, and Disentanglement
F.definition: A method aimed at optimizing facial identity consistency in multimodal editing models by addressing cross-source distribution bias and feature contamination.
An optimization problem that combines facility location and client matching, aiming to minimize costs while maximizing matched client pairs assigned to facilities.
A process utilizing large language models to verify the factual accuracy of knowledge graphs, addressing the challenges of manual verification at scale.

Fact-Flow is an innovative framework that separates the process of visual fact identification from the generation of medical reports using Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), addressing challenges related to factual instability.

Factor Abstraction is a universal interface for manipulating probabilistic models that allows representation-agnostic probabilistic programming, enabling users to mix different model representations seamlessly.

An open-source Python library designed for the systematic computation and analysis of financial factors, featuring a modular API that allows users to define and integrate custom factors into their financial analysis workflows.
A self-evolving agent framework designed for financial alpha discovery, utilizing a modular skill architecture to systematically evaluate and execute investment strategies.

A clustering approach that ensures the distribution of sensitive attributes, such as gender or race, within clusters mirrors that of the overall dataset. This method modifies traditional clustering algorithms like K-means to adhere to fairness constraints.

This generalization of correlation clustering incorporates fairness constraints through the concept of fairlets, providing a framework to analyze clustering problems under various structural graph parameterizations.

FairGDiff is a graph diffusion model that mitigates topology biases in graph generation tasks by applying debiasing methods directly on the graph topology, enhancing fairness in synthetic graph outputs.

An optimization problem that involves selecting k centers from a set of data points in a metric space, ensuring a fair representation from different groups while minimizing the maximum distance to the nearest center.
A method addressing biases in deep feature selection for recommender systems, utilizing trainable gate-based and sensitivity-based approaches to improve feature importance estimation.
A method for adapting voice classification models to new cohorts or devices while addressing partial-label mismatches and demographic biases, enhancing fairness in model predictions.

A novel evaluation framework for multiparty multiobjective optimization that addresses biases in classical performance metrics by considering the fairness of solution sets across different decision-makers.
An approach to designing user-generated virtual worlds that enhances cooperative play experiences for families while addressing real-time harms and risks.
A Linux-based far-memory system that employs deep learning to enhance memory prefetching by decoupling application semantics from runtime memory layout, enabling better prediction of access patterns.
A million-scale dataset that provides exhaustive annotations of visible fashion items within outfits, facilitating the development of generalist fashion intelligence across multiple tasks.
A framework for enabling secure collaboration between organizations on shared datasets while maintaining data confidentiality and high performance, utilizing fast datacenter networks.

FGFRFT is an optimized algorithm for computing the graph fractional Fourier transform, reducing computational complexity to O(2LN^2) while maintaining differentiability, thus enabling adaptive order learning.
A strategy for encoding high-quality 360-degree video for HTTP Adaptive Streaming that reduces encoding time by reusing encoder analysis across different resolutions and quantization parameters.
An algorithm designed for efficiently permuting sparse matrices arising from triangle meshes, focusing on fast partitioning and elimination-tree construction without strict balance requirements.

A technique for training surrogate models that efficiently map unordered vehicle positions to UAV counts and placements in multi-objective UAV placement problems for intelligent transportation systems.
Fast-MCS is a scalable open-source tool designed to evaluate Minimal Cut Sets (MCSs) in large, complex networks, helping to identify critical elements that impact network connectivity.
An FPGA-accelerated technique for optimizing sparse attention in long-context large language model inference, which reduces computational overhead by dynamically limiting token interactions.

A framework that optimizes repository-scale code reasoning by separating exploration from content consumption, utilizing a structural scouting mechanism to improve efficiency and reduce computational costs.
An optimization framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting that consolidates and evaluates effective strategies to enhance performance while maintaining reconstruction quality.
A closed-loop, self-correcting framework for generating physically grounded robotic task curricula, addressing the limitations of traditional open-loop task generation methods.
An enhanced Reduce operation that incorporates a correction phase to tolerate process failures, improving reliability in distributed computing environments.

A force-adaptive model that improves Vision-Language-Action (VLA) systems by incorporating force/torque feedback to handle contact-rich manipulation tasks, addressing the challenges of mismatched sensor sampling rates.

A methodology for integrating thematic satellite allocations into core-satellite portfolio architectures, focusing on robustness-oriented allocation frameworks that account for fixed costs, irreversibility risk, and governance complexity.

Feasible pairings refer to the determination of suitable input-output configurations for decentralized integral controllability in non-square systems, particularly relevant in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) environments.
A framework for personalized federated learning that utilizes a dynamic implicit graph for time-series forecasting, allowing for better handling of client heterogeneity.
A federated learning framework that enables collaborative fine-tuning of large language models across clients with heterogeneous resources using Low-Rank Adaptation.
A clustered federated learning approach that integrates global data and gradient information to enhance model performance in heterogeneous client environments.
A framework for federated cross-domain recommendation that utilizes large language models to enhance personalized recommendations while addressing data privacy concerns and overfitting risks.

A conjecture in constraint satisfaction problems stating that every finite-domain CSP is either in P or NP-complete, recently resolved for finite-domain cases but remains open for countably infinite first-order reducts.

An approach to artificial intelligence that emphasizes decentralized learning and collaboration among agents in wireless networks, addressing challenges of resource constraints and data heterogeneity.

A federated framework for causal representation learning in state-space systems that captures interdependencies among clients while preserving data privacy and enabling decentralized counterfactual reasoning.

Federated Instruction Tuning (FIT) is a collaborative approach for tuning large language models across multiple organizations without sharing private data, addressing the risks of poisoned samples in training datasets.
An approach to mitigate latent space misalignment in multi-agent semantic communications by implementing a protocol that shares semantic equalizers between an access point and user devices.

FedLoRA is a technique for communication-efficient and privacy-preserving fine-tuning of foundation models in federated learning, addressing challenges posed by client heterogeneity and preventing rank collapse.
A method for auditing demographic parity in federated learning environments, utilizing quantile sketches to measure disparities while maintaining privacy and data governance compliance.
A framework for federated learning that enables prompt-tuning across heterogeneous and incomplete multimodal client data, addressing the challenges of semantic alignment in distributed environments.

A recommendation paradigm that trains models across decentralized user devices, addressing privacy concerns while managing challenges related to sparse data and potential data poisoning from malicious clients.
This approach combines federated learning with decision transformers to optimize resource allocation in mobile edge computing environments, enhancing user experience in the metaverse.
A protocol for securely computing U-statistics of degree k in a federated learning setting, which allows for the computation of useful statistics while maintaining privacy.
A method for stabilizing nonlinear partial differential equations using controllers based on finitely many parameters, ensuring computational feasibility for practical applications.
A framework for generating high-quality test cases for programming tasks by leveraging external feedback to iteratively improve the diversity and effectiveness of test case synthesis.
A framework that captures the dynamics of user behavior and algorithmic recommendations over time, accounting for implicit feedback and retraining in recommender systems.

A bilingual (Korean-English) multimodal dataset specifically designed for training and evaluating jailbreak detectors in financial applications, addressing the vulnerabilities of Large Language Models and Vision Language Models.

Fenchel-Young Estimators provide a unified estimation framework for the Perturbed Utility Model, addressing challenges in maximum likelihood estimation by leveraging the convex conjugate structure of the models.
A universal representation framework for many-body fermionic wavefunctions that enables the conditioning of predictions based on Hamiltonian parameters and particle numbers.

This novel attack method targets the reasoning process of medical large language models during supervised fine-tuning by injecting poisoned rationales into few-shot training data, leading to stealthy degradation of model performance.

A reimagined GPU microarchitecture specifically designed to optimize performance for Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) tasks, addressing the computational and memory overheads typically associated with FHE.
A metric that measures the freshness of entanglement in quantum repeater networks, linking slot-level success probability to long-run average fidelity age.

A high-fidelity and detail-preserving one-step diffusion super-resolution framework that utilizes a detail-aware weighting strategy during training to enhance visual quality in image super-resolution tasks.

A system designed to automatically identify core filler-gap constructions in spoken English corpora, facilitating the analysis of children's language acquisition and the role of grammatical knowledge.

An advanced explicit surface reconstruction method that generates a surface mesh interpolating a given point cloud, ensuring preservation of sharp features and fine geometric details even with low-quality inputs.

F.term: Filtered Approximate k Nearest Neighbor (AKNN) Search
F.definition: A hybrid query approach that combines high-dimensional vector similarity with structured attribute filtering, requiring novel cost estimation techniques for optimization.
A search technique that enhances approximate nearest neighbor search by incorporating metadata constraints, enabling more relevant results in vector databases.
An algorithm that dynamically computes the finality guarantees of the Filecoin network, providing a more accurate assessment of tipset permanence based on live chain history.
A structured representation of financial data derived from SEC 10-K filings, used to enhance causal discovery algorithms by providing domain-specific constraints.

A computational representation that models financial structures like securitizations and insurance contracts by separating stochastic inflow generation from deterministic allocation rules, allowing for explicit mapping of inflows to payments.
A subfield of computational complexity theory that studies the precise run times of algorithms for NP-complete problems, aiming to identify the fastest possible algorithms beyond brute-force methods.
A technique for efficiently migrating memory in user-space across non-uniform memory access (NUMA) architectures, optimizing query performance by ensuring data locality.

Fine-Pruning is a biologically inspired algorithm that personalizes machine learning models by mimicking the brain's learning process through pruning, addressing the limitations of traditional backpropagation methods.
A large language model designed for sentiment analysis in financial contexts, enabling the integration of sentiment signals into quantitative trading strategies.
An analysis of how financial technology disrupts traditional banking by increasing competition for deposits and raising deposit rates, particularly affecting small financial institutions.
A financial text-paired time-series dataset designed to capture the complex interdependencies in financial markets by pairing textual information with numerical time-series data.
An FPGA-based hardware accelerator that implements a novel plasticity algorithm for Spiking Neural Networks, enhancing real-time adaptive control through on-chip plasticity.

FireRed-OCR is a specialized framework that transforms general Vision-Language Models into high-performance Optical Character Recognition models, focusing on pixel-precise structural document parsing to enhance industrial OCR applications.
An advanced sampling technique for masked diffusion models that shows promise in generating samples more efficiently compared to traditional methods, particularly in high-dimensional spaces.

F.term: First-Order Logic of Here and There
F.definition: A logic system for automated theorem proving that combines sequent calculus and axiomatic embedding into intuitionistic logic, optimized for analytic proof search.

The FITO paradigm evaluates multimodal large language models by requiring them to construct explicit cross-modal evidence chains within scientific documents, promoting a deeper understanding of content.

A theoretical framework that characterizes the behavior of stochastic gradient descent (SGD) by modeling mini-batch noise as a loss-induced matrix. This approach provides insights into optimal rates and oracle complexity in the context of SGD.
An advanced theory of stochastic gradient descent that incorporates mini-batch noise as a loss-induced matrix, allowing for improved convergence rates and understanding of gradient covariance.
A framework that models mini-batch noise in stochastic gradient descent as an intrinsic matrix, allowing for a diffusion approximation with structured volatility.

FL-HDC-DP is a federated learning framework that employs hyperdimensional computing and differential privacy to minimize energy consumption and enhance privacy in decentralized learning over wireless edge networks.
This technique focuses on identifying flaky tests in software projects by analyzing source code identifiers, aiming to improve automated test execution quality in industrial settings.

An accelerated implementation of Score-Debiasing Kernel Density Estimation (SD-KDE) that utilizes Tensor Cores to enhance performance significantly, achieving up to 47 times faster execution compared to traditional methods.
An advanced framework for evaluating sequences in tasks like recommender systems, which enhances accuracy and resource utilization by enabling parallel evaluation and cross-sequence comparison.
A memory streaming framework designed to optimize the execution of modern deep neural networks on mobile GPUs by dynamically managing memory usage rather than preloading all model parameters.

FLCOA (Five Layers for Cooperation/Coordination among Autonomous Agents) is a framework that conceptualizes the emergence of cooperation and coordination in LLM-based multi-agent systems, focusing on the impact of computational and communication resources on agent interactions.
A unified framework for video generation that disentangles appearance and motion, utilizing a novel 3D control signal to enhance the control and precision of generated video content.

Flexi-NeurA is a configurable neuromorphic accelerator designed for spiking neural networks (SNNs) that allows users to customize neuron models, network structures, and precision levels, enhancing adaptability for diverse workloads.

An innovative array system that enhances wireless network capacity by allowing mechanical beamforming through the movement of passive couplers, improving communication coverage at a low hardware cost.
A composable High-Level Synthesis library that facilitates the rapid development of domain-specific LLM accelerators, allowing for customized inference architectures.
A fine-grained accelerator for 3D Gaussian Splatting that optimizes contribution-aware processing to enhance performance on resource-limited edge platforms.
A novel framework utilizing a generalized Hough transform on the unit sphere to estimate camera motion from monocular video, improving accuracy in high-noise conditions.
The minimum number of flips required to transform one triangulation of a convex polygon into another, a problem proven to be NP-hard.
An NP-hard problem that computes the minimum number of flips required to transform one triangulation of a convex polygon into another, which is isomorphic to rotations in binary trees.

A graph where vertices represent odd matchings of a combinatorial graph, and edges connect matchings that can be transformed into one another by a single reconfiguration step, with complexity results on computing its diameter.

Floe is a hybrid federated learning framework that combines cloud-based large language models with lightweight small language models on edge devices, enabling low-latency, privacy-preserving inference.
A unified framework for generative speech enhancement that models stochastic processes between paired noisy and clean speech signals using principles such as flow matching and score matching.

A simulation-free generative modeling technique that produces samples by solving an ordinary differential equation (ODE) with a learned time-dependent velocity field, effectively interpolating between simple and complex distributions.

A polynomial-time algorithm that computes a near-optimal flow-expander decomposition of a graph by removing a minimal fraction of edges, ensuring that the remaining components satisfy stronger expansion properties.
A framework for 3D style transfer that emphasizes geometric abstraction and structural form, inspired by Post-Impressionist art principles.

FlowPortrait is a reinforcement learning framework for audio-driven portrait video generation that utilizes a multimodal backbone to improve lip-sync accuracy and motion quality through a human-aligned evaluation system.
A technique for mitigating head-of-line blocking in serving systems for large language models by decoupling preemption from prefill scheduling granularity.
A framework that allows agents to create other agents dynamically, reflecting real-world scenarios where the number of agents is variable and can evolve over time.
An adaptive hierarchical memory framework for streaming video understanding that compresses redundant visual memory through a two-stage design, optimizing for dynamic scenes.

An adaptive serving architecture for Large Language Models (LLMs) that dynamically switches between data and tensor parallelism to optimize throughput and latency based on real-time traffic conditions.

F.term: FO and MSO Model Checking on Temporal Graphs
F.definition: A framework for applying logical meta-theorems to temporal graphs, introducing encodings for parameters like vertex-interval-membership width, enhancing the tractability of graph problems.
A theoretical framework for program verification that guarantees all valid theorems can be proven within a system, utilizing first-order logic principles.

Formula-constrained Optimization for Annotating Metabolites (FOAM) is a computational workflow that frames structural elucidation from LC-MS/MS data as an iterative optimization problem, enhancing the accuracy of molecular identification.

Focal-entropy is a novel information-theoretic measure analogous to cross-entropy, specifically designed for class-imbalanced classification problems, providing insights into its finiteness, convexity, and asymptotic behavior.
An inference system designed for Diffusion Large Language Models (DLLMs) that optimizes decoding by focusing on token importance, reducing computational waste during the decoding process.
A component of wearable technology that identifies and captures speech in real-time, trained on public datasets to enhance the detection of social interactions in naturalistic settings.

A hierarchical multi-scale framework for cross-domain image forgery detection that integrates low-level artifact detection, mid-level boundary analysis, and high-level semantic reasoning using cross-attention transformers.
A large-scale dataset specifically designed for detecting missing persons in forest environments, addressing the challenges posed by dense canopy cover that obscures individuals from aerial imagery.
FORGE is a framework that addresses the tensions in sustainable multi-agent crowdsourcing by integrating physics-informed bandit algorithms to balance allocation quality and workforce sustainability.

F.term: Formal Evidence Generation for Assurance Cases
F.definition: A model-based approach for systematically generating evidence for assurance cases in robotic software, integrating formal verification into the assurance workflow.

F.term: Formal Framework for Predicting Distributed System Performance
F.definition: A systematic framework that enables the performance prediction of distributed systems under various fault scenarios, integrating a fault injector and reusable fault libraries for comprehensive analysis.
A framework for automated circuit discovery in neural networks that provides provable guarantees over continuous input domains, enhancing the understanding of neural network behaviors.
Abstract representations of Java methods that detail their security behavior, including conditions for secure invocation and specifications of information flows, aiding in static code analysis.
An efficient training method for spiking neural networks that maintains temporal learning efficiency while significantly reducing computational costs in semantic image segmentation tasks.
A design principle in computing protocols that asserts causation is irreversible and acyclic, influencing the semantics of message passing and system design.

The FITO category mistake refers to a structural error in large-scale AI/ML training systems, where the confusion between temporal snapshots and convergence properties can lead to incorrect assumptions about system states during infrastructure updates.
A technique for short-horizon forecasting in non-stationary financial markets that constructs interpretable causal signals from micro-features like momentum and volatility.

FMAS is a pipeline that generates realistic anomalous samples for industrial anomaly detection without the need for fine-tuning or class-specific training, enhancing the model's ability to detect rare events.

A comprehensive survey on the evolution of foundation models from unimodal to multimodal applications in remote sensing, highlighting their potential to enhance data modeling and interpretation capabilities.

A framework designed to evaluate the geometric disentanglement of emotional, linguistic, and pathological speech features across multiple datasets, facilitating better classification in multilingual clinical settings.
An estimator that utilizes Fourier methods to converge to the volatility's path, accommodating unbounded coefficients and discontinuous price processes.
A mathematical framework for establishing the existence of quantum splines through fourth-order evolution equations, providing a geometric approach to variational problems in quantum control.
A rendering technique that reduces resolution in peripheral vision areas to optimize performance and resource usage while maintaining high-quality visuals in the foveal region.
A biologically-inspired interface that mimics human foveated vision, allowing variable resolution processing for deep vision models to enhance efficiency in computer vision tasks.

FP4 Training is a technique that enables efficient training of large-scale Mixture-of-Experts models on Hopper GPUs by integrating FP4 into existing hybrid training pipelines without incurring costly precision round-trips.
Specialized processing units designed to efficiently handle FP8 (8-bit floating point) matrix operations, enhancing performance for high-performance computing (HPC) and AI workloads.
A holistic evaluation framework for assessing large language models' performance in functional programming, built on the FPBench benchmark of 721 programming tasks.

A unified deep learning framework that integrates Wi-Fi beamforming matrix feedback with anomaly-aware indoor positioning, addressing challenges in dynamic environments and incomplete channel state information.

FPPS is an FPGA-based point cloud processing system that optimizes the iterative closest point (ICP) algorithm for real-time applications in autonomous driving, achieving significant speedups over traditional CPU implementations.
A method for training audio language models to utilize their internal audio representations for precise temporal grounding tasks, improving efficiency and reducing computational costs.
A novel operating system architecture that utilizes Rust to achieve a minimal and sound Trusted Computing Base (TCB), focusing on memory safety and intra-kernel privilege separation.
Fréchet Audio Distance is a metric for evaluating text-to-audio generation that accounts for task-induced biases in encoder embedding spaces, providing a more nuanced evaluation framework.
An empirical analysis revealing task-induced biases in Fréchet Audio Distance, decomposing evaluation into Recall, Precision, and Alignment metrics for fair cross-encoder comparisons.

A method for optimizing material properties in two-dimensional plane-stress compliance problems, utilizing a hierarchy of admissible sets to bridge the gap between classical and composite realizability.
A quantization technique for large language models that allows dynamic activation patterns to be utilized, improving efficiency in memory and computational overhead during model deployment.
A training-free method for arbitrary camera redirection in monocular videos, utilizing geometry-complete 4D reconstruction to create coherent representations from limited input data.
A wireless-first sensing paradigm that repurposes frequency agility into a virtual sensing aperture, enabling two-dimensional spatial sensing with minimal RF front end complexity.

FaSE is a module within the DiffCR framework that refines the prediction process in diffusion-based image compression by focusing on frequency-aware estimations, enhancing image quality at low bit rates.

A preprocessing technique for text compression that organizes tokens based on their frequency, improving the efficiency of lossless compression algorithms by assigning smaller identifiers to more frequent tokens.
A method for assessing ionospheric scintillation risk by scaling L-band GNSS observations to relevant frequencies for Direct-to-Cellular communications, enhancing link quality analysis.

F.term: Frequent Sequence Mining for Image Compression
F.definition: An innovative approach to lossy image compression that employs frequent sequence mining and k-means clustering to enhance the efficiency of the JPEG compression process.
A high-performance crypto-processor designed for the FrodoKEM post-quantum key encapsulation mechanism, featuring a multiple-instruction overlapped execution scheme to reduce latency.
A system that estimates clean speech tokens from noisy speech to improve the performance of automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems by utilizing semantic tokens.
Frontier models are evaluated for their ability to take actions at low probabilities, exploring the implications of such behavior in pre-deployment evaluations and potential evasion of oversight.

Frozen Policy Iteration is a computationally efficient reinforcement learning algorithm that operates under the linear Q^π realizability assumption, suitable for Markov Decision Processes with stochastic elements.
An imaging method that utilizes full acoustic wave simulations to enhance the accuracy of ultrasonic imaging by accounting for nonlinear phenomena, improving upon traditional linear models.

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) allows computations to be performed on encrypted data without needing to decrypt it first, thus preserving privacy in sensitive applications such as Quantum Federated Learning.
An advanced signal processing architecture that utilizes 3D aperture engineering to enhance the resolution and efficiency of array signal processing for radar and communication applications.
A proposed beamforming technique designed to enhance transmission confidentiality in MIMO VLC systems, optimizing the signal sent to legitimate receivers while minimizing eavesdropping risks.

This concept explores the security implications of function inlining in compilers, particularly how this optimization affects static features crucial for binary analysis and potential vulnerabilities.

A generic service-oriented function offloading framework designed for Connected Automated Vehicles (CAVs) that distributes computational tasks between local and remote devices, enhancing computational capacity and energy efficiency.
An API designed to replace the publish/subscribe model in DAG scheduling, allowing subtasks to be expressed as functions with defined input and output edges, enforcing DAG semantics.

Function-Correcting Codes provide error protection for data while ensuring the integrity of specific functions derived from that data, characterized by minimum distances that differentiate function values.
A method for interpretability in machine learning that decomposes model predictions into main effects and higher-order interactions, particularly applicable to categorical inputs.
FCD is a JAX-accelerated framework that performs parametric, continuous optimization on mathematical functions, achieving continuity guarantees through Levenberg-Marquardt optimization.
Functional Urban Areas are micro-founded labor-market regions that encompass urban centers and their commuting zones, representing a significant portion of global GDP and population.

Funky is a full-stack FPGA-aware orchestration engine for cloud-native applications, providing essential orchestration services to improve performance, scalability, and fault tolerance for FPGA workloads.

An advanced Model Predictive Control framework designed for non-linear continuous-time systems, focusing on output tracking within specified error bounds while addressing challenges such as feasibility and robustness.
A novel interaction method for generative AI that enhances the integration of visual and semantic materials in design practice.
A systematic framework for modeling and scheduling fusion patterns in Autonomous Driving Systems, addressing diverse data fusion tasks and their performance implications.
An optimal mapping method that incorporates data fusion to reduce energy and latency in tensor algebra accelerators by keeping data on-chip during computation steps.
FutureOmni is a benchmark designed to evaluate the future forecasting capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models using audio-visual cues, focusing on cross-modal causal and temporal reasoning.
A GenAI-empowered collaborative storytelling system aimed at helping adolescents navigate future uncertainties through structured narrative mechanics.
A recommendation framework that employs linear attention mechanisms to address the challenges of long-term time-aware sequential recommendation, improving efficiency in handling user sequences.
An adaptive shuffle service for distributed data processing that enhances efficiency and fault tolerance by dynamically adjusting to job characteristics and cluster resource conditions.

FuzzySQL is an LLM-powered adaptive fuzzing framework that uncovers hidden vulnerabilities in database management systems by focusing on obscure DBMS features that are often overlooked by traditional fuzzing techniques.

FWeb3 is a federated learning framework that integrates Web3 technologies to provide incentive mechanisms for participants, addressing challenges like coordination efficiency and secure model updates.

Q.definition: An extensible quantum network simulator built on ns-3, designed to evaluate quantum network architectures and protocols while emulating quantum dynamics on classical platforms.
A tokenization method that incorporates data reliability into vocabulary construction, optimizing vocabulary and downstream performance through a bilevel optimization formulation.

A self-attention mechanism designed for predicting video quality of experience (QoE) in encrypted traffic, enhancing performance in video conferencing applications by analyzing encrypted media streams.
A web-based platform for automated essay scoring specifically tailored for Arabic, providing tools for assignment creation, essay upload, and scoring configuration.
A novel framework for unitary synthesis in quantum computing that combines Generative Flow Networks with Transformers to efficiently learn from sparse reward signals.
The first general-purpose static analysis framework for Verilog, aimed at enhancing bug detection and program understanding in hardware design through sophisticated static analysis techniques.

An ontology-grounded framework for constructing interpretable medical text embeddings, where each dimension corresponds to clinically meaningful yes/no questions, enhancing the usability of embeddings in clinical decision-making.

QMAVIS (Q Team-Multimodal Audio Video Intelligent Sensemaking) is a novel long video-audio understanding pipeline that utilizes late fusion of Large Multimodal Models, Large Language Models, and speech recognition models to analyze long-form video content, enhancing applications in sensemaking and video content analysis.
A performance modeling method that partitions the execution configuration space of distributed workflows into regions with similar behavior to ensure Quality of Service (QoS) guarantees.
QSIM is a framework for mitigating Q-value overestimation in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning by employing action similarity to reconstruct temporal-difference targets, enhancing policy stability.

QSpy is a proof-of-concept Quantum Remote Access Trojan that intercepts quantum circuits in transit, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks by installing a rogue certificate authority and proxying outgoing API traffic.
A hybrid lossless compression technique that combines the advantages of Huffman and universal codes to optimize decoding speed and efficiency, particularly for large language models.
A technique for reformulating 0-1 quadratic programming problems, enhancing solver flexibility by incorporating nonconvex quadratic constraints.
An algorithm that achieves a quadratic speedup in finding ε-fixed points of contraction maps, improving the running time from O(log^k(1/ε)) to O(log^{⌈k/2⌉}(1/ε)) for both ℓ₁ and ℓ₊ norms.

Q.term: Quadratic Speedup for Contraction Fixed Points
Q.definition: An algorithmic advancement that finds ε-fixed points of contraction maps with a running time improvement, achieving O(log^(⌈k/2⌉)(1/ε)) for both ℓ₁ and ℓ₊ norms.
This method introduces a novel representation and optimization framework for computing squared geodesic distances on triangle meshes, enhancing accuracy on uneven and coarse discretizations.
A method for learning high-quality Semantic IDs that addresses the collision problem in multimodal item features, focusing on the varying impacts of collisions on recommendation quality.
A small graph that preserves multicommodity flows between a set of terminal vertices in a larger graph, maintaining a specified quality factor, which is essential for graph compression tasks.
An optimization paradigm aimed at discovering a diverse set of high-performing solutions within a specified behavior space, applicable in various domains such as robotics and creative design.
An evolutionary framework for alpha mining that improves automation through trajectory-level mutation and crossover operations, addressing noise sensitivity in financial markets.
