A method that enhances traditional economic growth models by incorporating Lévy noise to account for non-Gaussian stochastic perturbations affecting capital stock and output.
A model that describes liquid-asset returns exhibiting Levy-stable scaling, allowing for the derivation of horizon-correct formulas for various risk metrics like Value-at-Risk and Sharpe ratios.
An agentic framework for structured event timeline extraction from legal documents, employing a dual-agent architecture to identify and score candidate events in judicial outcomes.
A variable scoping mechanism employed in R that resolves variable references based on the structure in which functions are defined, rather than at runtime.
A large-scale dataset for long-form question answering that includes 1.3 million human pairwise preference annotations, aimed at improving evaluation metrics for LFQA systems.

LiaisonAgent is an autonomous multi-agent framework that enhances risk investigation and governance in Security Operations Centers by integrating specialized agents to bridge technical risk detection with business-level governance.
A Lightweight Complex Question Answering system that operates unsupervised to address complex questions across multiple documents without the need for extensive training data.

A framework that synthesizes AI governance and cloud security standards into a lifecycle-staged threat taxonomy, addressing vulnerabilities in hybrid AI-cloud deployments that could lead to cross-layer attacks.
A paradigm for 3D avatar animation that completes sparse monocular observations in kinematic space, utilizing a video diffusion transformer to synthesize high-fidelity expression sequences.
Large language models designed to generate secure scripting languages with minimal resource requirements, enhancing automation while maintaining security.
An architectural enhancement for Remote Procedure Calls (RPCs) that reduces CPU overhead by implementing a lightweight caching mechanism to accelerate data retrieval and processing.
An automated evolutionary method designed to enhance the accuracy and robustness of Skull-Face Overlay (SFO) in craniofacial superimposition by aligning 3D skull models with 2D facial images.
A class of models that utilize linear attention mechanisms, providing a theoretical framework for analyzing the effects of fine-tuning on in-context learning performance.
An oracle used in constrained convex optimization that provides solutions for linear constraints, facilitating the development of algorithms such as Frank-Wolfe methods.

A diagonalization-based optimization technique for Linear Echo State Networks that reduces computational complexity of reservoir state updates from O(N^2) to O(N) by reformulating dynamics in the eigenbasis of the recurrent matrix.
Models trained to reconstruct linear spectrograms from neural representations of vocalized, mimed, and imagined speech, demonstrating generalization across different speech conditions.

An expressive extension of classical temporal logic that allows for the specification of non-Markovian rewards in Markov Decision Processes, utilizing first-order predicates instead of simple Boolean variables. This framework promotes complex task specification over unstructured data domains.
A formulation of mean-field games that incorporates rough common noise, characterized by a Volterra-type formulation for optimal control and state characterization.

A large-scale multi-agent benchmark for evaluating LLMs in psychiatric consultation and diagnosis, providing realistic patient simulations and clinician-verified diagnostic labels for dynamic multi-turn consultations.

LinkVLA is a novel architecture designed to enhance alignment and efficiency in Vision-Language-Action models for autonomous driving by establishing a structural link between language instructions and action outputs.

LintMe is a design probe that utilizes a lightweight domain-specific language (DSL) to create context-specific checks for improving README files, addressing both style and content issues while maintaining authorial agency.
A comprehensive 882-hour multi-speaker Bengali dataset designed to enhance Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and speaker diarization for long-duration audio.
A type of neural network that incorporates learnable time constants to adaptively model temporal dynamics, particularly applied in emotion recognition from physiological signals like EEG.
LiSFC is a lifelong planner for optimizing service function chaining (SFC) in non-stationary networks, utilizing Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) statistics to adapt to changing network configurations.

A comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate agentic systems across 104 real-world challenges, constructed using the Social Perception-Driven Data Generation method to ensure relevance to actual user tasks.
LiveCultureBench is a multi-agent, multi-cultural benchmark for evaluating large language models in dynamic social simulations, focusing on task completion and adherence to socio-cultural norms.
An active-structure neural re-ranking framework designed for exercise recommendation, utilizing graph-based representation to enhance personalized educational content delivery.
A framework that integrates large language models into an evolutionary loop to generate and benchmark continuous optimization problems with controllable high-level properties.

LLaMEA-SAGE is a mechanism for guiding automated algorithm design using structural feedback from explainable AI, enhancing the exploration of algorithm design spaces by utilizing graph-theoretic features.

Theoretical and empirical mechanisms identified that affect the diversity of ideas generated by large language models, highlighting fixation at the individual level and aggregation at the collective level.
A reference framework aimed at accurately measuring the carbon footprint of large language model inference at the prompt level, facilitating sustainability-focused decision-making.
A system that utilizes multiple large language models (LLMs) to collaboratively review and validate code generated from natural language queries, enhancing the reliability of automated coding tools.
A concept evaluating the performance enhancement for novice users utilizing large language models (LLMs) compared to traditional internet resources in biosecurity-related tasks.

A paradigm of Automated Algorithm Design that utilizes Large Language Models to autonomously generate code implementations of algorithms, emphasizing the importance of assessing algorithmic similarity beyond mere syntactic variation.

L.term: LLM-Assisted Multimodal Emotion Data Annotation Toolkit
L.definition: A toolkit that facilitates the annotation of multimodal emotion data by preprocessing and aligning heterogeneous recordings, visualizing them on an interactive timeline, and detecting candidate events for annotation.

An approach that utilizes large language models to detect user experience (UX) smells in Integrated Development Environments by analyzing user-reported issues and classifying them based on a validated taxonomy.
This system leverages Large Language Models to automate the remediation of accessibility issues in web applications, particularly focusing on the challenges posed by dynamic Single-Page Applications.
A framework that reformulates channel state information compression as a masked token prediction task, leveraging large language models for efficient feedback in massive MIMO systems.
A system that uses a Large Language Model to autonomously manage cooperative edge caching decisions across multiple base stations, enhancing content replacement strategies.

A systematic evaluation of four architectures for automated patching using large language models: fixed workflow, single-agent system, multi-agent system, and general-purpose code agents, focusing on their effectiveness in patch correctness and failure modes.
The use of large language models (LLMs) as proxies for human judges in evaluating relevance, exploring their reliability and potential overrating behavior across various evaluation paradigms.

LLM-Derived Control Policies leverage Large Language Models as intelligent mutation operators in an iterative framework for optimizing residential electric vehicle energy management, improving interpretability in vehicle-to-grid systems.

A novel approach that utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to facilitate the hardware-software co-design process for post-quantum cryptography, focusing on optimizing the implementation of cryptographic algorithms like FALCON.
A comprehensive taxonomy categorizing the integration of large language models in compiler development based on design philosophy, methodology, code abstraction level, and task type.

A natural-language-guided scenario generation framework that allows users to generate diverse and controllable power system scenarios by leveraging large language models and frequency-aware flow diffusion techniques.
Components that integrate large language models into relational data processing tasks, enhancing operations like semantic filtering and entity matching while preserving a relational interface.

LLM-FSM is a benchmark designed to evaluate the ability of large language models to recover finite-state machine behavior from natural-language specifications and translate it into register transfer-level implementations.
A method that augments app store ranking systems by integrating large language model-generated relevance judgments, addressing the scarcity of expert-provided textual relevance labels.
A multi-agent architecture that facilitates natural interaction between human planners and AI systems by providing explanations that enhance understanding and trust in automated planning solutions.

The LLM-MLFFN (Multi-Level Autonomous Driving Behavior Feature Fusion via Large Language Model) is a framework that enhances the classification of autonomous vehicle driving behaviors by integrating features from large language models with multi-dimensional driving data.
An interactive tool embedded in virtual reality applications that utilizes large language models to assist users in understanding and navigating privacy policies during app selection.
A formal framework for modeling conversations with large language models (LLMs), focusing on the flow of information and the interactions between AI agents and human prompts.

LLMServingSim 2.0 is a unified simulator designed for heterogeneous and disaggregated large language model serving infrastructures, focusing on the interaction between diverse hardware and software components to enhance scalability and efficiency.

LLMTailor is a tool that optimizes the checkpointing process for large language models by selectively saving only the layers that experience significant updates, thereby reducing storage overhead and resource contention.
An algorithm that modifies n-dimensional Poisson disc sampling for parallel execution in meshless numerical methods, optimizing node generation for adaptive analysis in complex geometries.

A model-native local conformal prediction method for gradient-boosted trees that enhances uncertainty quantification by adapting to heteroscedasticity without the need for auxiliary models or excessive data splits.
A technique for normalizing scores in anomalous sound detection by adapting neighborhood size based on locality preservation, enhancing detection accuracy across varying data densities.

L.term: Local Differential Privacy (LDP) in Molecular Communication
L.definition: LDP is integrated into molecular communication networks to ensure privacy during data exchange, allowing for secure aggregate data analysis in sensitive applications like health monitoring.
A problem that involves enumerating satisfying assignments of a k-CNF formula with specific Hamming weight constraints, leading to new algorithms for k-SAT.

A discussion on the computability of algorithms within the LOCAL model, emphasizing the importance of whether nodes update their state using arbitrary or computable functions in locally checkable labeling problems.
LNDP is a model for achieving differential privacy in graphs, where each node releases outputs based on its local edge list, allowing for accurate aggregation of queries while maintaining privacy.

L.term: Local Second-Order Limit Dynamics for ADMM
L.definition: This framework analyzes the local second-order dynamics of the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) for semidefinite programming, particularly in regions of slow convergence.
A method for controlling time-dependent acoustic and electromagnetic waves in a bounded domain, allowing for strong localization of wave energy in specified regions.
A class of codes that allows for efficient local list decoding with high rate and error tolerance, achieving near-optimal performance in the context of hardness amplification.
A method for detecting distributional changes in time series data while preserving local differential privacy, utilizing randomized response and binary mechanisms for privacy guarantees.
A distribution-free loss-quantile score that provides a per-input reliability score for predictions, allowing for better risk-aware decision-making in machine learning models.
A novel architecture for long-context geometric reconstruction that processes video streams in chunks, leveraging bidirectional priors for high-fidelity reasoning across extended sequences.
An approach that utilizes first-order logic to enhance the transparency of predictive models, such as the Framingham Risk Score, by providing logical explanations for risk categorizations.
An extension of rewriting induction that incorporates existential quantification to prove theorems in constrained rewrite systems, enhancing the expressiveness of logical frameworks.
A proposed stochastic kernel for prediction markets that treats traded probabilities as Q-martingales, aiding market makers in managing belief volatility and risks.
A property of supervised fine-tuning loss functions that contributes to the stability of reinforcement learning optimization by ensuring favorable gradient directionality during training.
Nonconvex regularization methods designed for recovering block-sparse signals with unknown partitions, extending log-sum and Minimax Concave Penalties to the block-sparse domain.
A hybrid framework for event-grounded question answering over long-duration audio, utilizing retrieved, timestamped acoustic event detections to enhance context understanding.
A benchmark designed to evaluate long-horizon agentic programming capabilities in command-line interfaces, addressing gaps in existing benchmarks related to task complexity and evaluation metrics.
A benchmark dataset for evaluating large language models in long-context code question answering, extended with new question-answer sets for COBOL and Java.
LIPT is a framework for monitoring heart failure through speech dynamics, capturing individual symptomatic changes over time to improve patient management.

L.term: Longitudinal Open Dataset of Spanish Public Procurement
L.definition: A comprehensive dataset containing structured information on public procurement contracts in Spain from 2014 to 2024, facilitating large-scale quantitative analysis of procurement dynamics.

A reinforcement learning framework that incorporates verifiable context rewards to enhance the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in long-context scenarios, addressing the limitations of sparse reward signals.

LF2WB is a locomotion framework for legged robots that utilizes forward egocentric depth and proprioception to efficiently navigate complex terrain while walking backward, enhancing stability and obstacle avoidance.
A standardized benchmark designed to measure look-ahead bias in Point-in-Time large language models, evaluating their predictive capabilities in realistic financial workflows.
An enhanced anonymity architecture for routing in satellite constellations that improves metadata privacy and reliability in dynamic network infrastructures.

A technique for content-aware attention manipulation in diffusion-based image editing, allowing for precise spatial and visual control by enabling users to directly modify images without prompts while preserving object identity.
LoPace is a lossless optimized prompt accurate compression engine designed for large language model applications, employing multiple compression techniques to efficiently store and manage prompts.
A miniature, encrypted intercommunication device utilizing LoRa technology for secure, short-range communication in tactical and disaster response settings.
A low rank-based code-rate adaptation technique for neural receivers that integrates lightweight adapters into convolutional layers, enabling efficient adaptability with minimal overhead.

A mathematical model used to describe the dynamic interactions between AI capital, physical capital, and labor, illustrating how these elements influence each other's growth and stability within an economy.
A warm-start framework designed to enhance dual decomposition methods in large-scale two-stage stochastic programming, improving primal convergence through informed initialization.
A retrieval system designed for multilingual tasks that combines LLM-based query expansion with BM25 retrieval and pointwise re-ranking to efficiently handle large document collections.
A remote ECG monitoring system designed for resource-constrained settings, enabling community health workers to perform telemedicine and support early detection of cardiovascular diseases.

A heuristic used to estimate algorithmic thresholds for distinguishing between planted and null distributions based on the closeness of their low-degree moments, facilitating noise-tolerant algorithm design.

A technique for efficient test-time optimization in depth completion that focuses on adapting only the decoder component of a neural network, reducing computational costs associated with traditional iterative denoising methods.
A formulation utilizing the Koopman operator for accelerating the simulation of deformable objects, achieving log-linear time integration through efficient matrix evaluations.

A technique for addressing the Max-3-Cut problem by leveraging low-rank structures in objective matrices, leading to alternative algorithms that improve upon classical semidefinite programming relaxations.

A method utilizing Tensor Train decompositions for efficient interpolation of grid-structured data, enabling high-dimensional computations while maintaining low-rank structure and reducing computational costs.
A method for recovering low-tubal-rank tensors from noisy linear measurements using factorized gradient descent, addressing challenges in optimization when the tubal-rank is unknown.
A generic method for audio-driven lip synchronization in talking head generation, utilizing a U-Net architecture with a residual CBAM for enhanced audio-visual coherence.
An interactive theorem prover for pure Prolog programs that supports negation as failure and unification, designed to formally prove termination and partial correctness.
A multilingual synthetic-speech corpus for cross-lingual spoof detection, containing extensive audio data generated from various TTS systems to evaluate model robustness.
A parameter-isolated architecture for federated sequential recommendation that integrates large language models to enhance next-item prediction while maintaining user data decentralization.
A simplified representation of the coupled environment-body-receiver system in ultra-low field MRI, used to analyze electromagnetic interference and its effects on imaging quality.
A novel architecture that enables self-loading reconfigurable instructions in FPGA-based systems, allowing for dynamic instruction sets that can adapt to specific computational tasks.

An interactive light editing approach for indoor scenes that decomposes lighting into individual sources, allowing independent manipulation of their state, chromaticity, and intensity while ensuring consistency across multiple views.

E.definition: A persistent compiler abstraction that utilizes e-graphs to represent equalities natively in intermediate representations, facilitating program optimization without losing discovered equalities during transformations.

A unified multimodal knowledge graph framework tailored for e-commerce applications, which constructs a specific knowledge graph and learns item representations through graph neural network-based propagation.

E-variable-approximability is a method for constructing e-variables for various distribution classes, enhancing statistical hypothesis testing. This technique builds on the concept of average-bounded randomness tests from Algorithmic Information Theory.

E2E-GNet is an end-to-end geometric deep neural network designed for skeleton-based human motion recognition, incorporating a geometric transformation layer to optimize motion sequences in non-Euclidean space.
A framework aimed at enhancing emotional expressiveness in virtual conversational agents, focusing on the integration of emotional context into responses to improve user engagement.
An energy-aware adaptive antenna control framework utilizing reinforcement learning to minimize energy consumption in cell-free massive MIMO networks while ensuring user spectral efficiency.

eBPF-PATROL is a protective agent that enhances runtime security in containerized and virtualized environments by providing context-aware syscall argument filtering and adaptive enforcement based on application behavior.

ECCO is a framework for compiler optimization that combines interpretable reasoning with combinatorial search, enabling the construction of a Chain-of-Thought dataset to map code features to performance evidence.
EchoVLA is a user-aware Vision Language Action model that integrates real-time user audio instructions with visual data to enhance decision-making in autonomous driving scenarios.

ECLD, or Edge Compact LLM Deployment, is a framework that combines structured pruning, low-bit quantization, and knowledge distillation to create deployable large language models for mobile edge computing environments.
A framework for detecting hallucinations in Large Language Models by assessing the mismatch between a model's semantic entropy and the evidence capacity, significantly reducing hallucination rates.
An enriched dataset that augments traditional Amazon datasets with Product Carbon Footprint metadata, aimed at enhancing sustainability metrics in recommender systems.
An annotated dataset of historical geographic coordinates extracted from 18th-century texts, aimed at improving the retrieval of geographic information from digitized early modern literature.
A problem in software testing focused on achieving white-box coverage of control flow graphs while adhering to specific constraints between vertices, impacting feasibility and complexity.

A method for designing test sets that achieve edge coverage in control flow graphs while respecting additional constraints between vertices, enhancing the feasibility and computational complexity of software testing.

A combinatorial algorithm for clustering datasets modeled as edge-colored hypergraphs, aiming to match node colors with edge colors. This approach presents an approximation factor better than 2, improving upon previous methods.

EdgeFLow is a federated learning framework that utilizes sequential model migration between edge base stations, eliminating the need for traditional cloud servers and reducing communication bottlenecks in distributed learning systems.
A compact graph representation that allows for efficient analysis of massive graph streams, providing unbiased estimators for key graph properties with controllable variance.
This framework allows for imperceptible text-based speech editing by decoupling content editing from acoustic fidelity, ensuring that modified speech segments blend seamlessly with their context.

EditEmoTalk is a framework for controllable speech-driven 3D facial animation that enables continuous emotion editing through boundary-aware semantic embeddings, allowing for smooth emotional transitions in facial expressions.
EditFlow is a benchmarking and optimization framework for code edit recommendation systems that reconstructs developer flows to improve technical accuracy and productivity.
A DiT-based framework for audio-driven video-to-video editing that allows transcript-based modifications of talking head videos, focusing on maintaining motion and lip synchronization.
A society-centric generative cognitive digital twin architecture that simulates social spaces in educational contexts, allowing for dynamic rule injection and complex social dynamics.

EduAIGV-1k is a benchmark dataset and evaluation framework for assessing the quality of AI-generated videos aimed at teaching foundational math concepts, comprising 1,130 videos generated from pedagogy-oriented prompts.

Educational assortative mating refers to the pattern where individuals with similar educational backgrounds tend to marry each other, influenced by factors such as geographic mobility and migration, particularly observed in urban-to-urban migrant populations in India.

EduEVAL-DB is a role-based dataset designed for evaluating and training automatic pedagogical evaluators and AI tutors, comprising 854 explanations for 139 questions across various subjects in K-12 education.

E.term: EEG-Based Hybrid Visual and Motor Imagery
E.definition: This framework integrates EEG-based visual and motor imagery to enable intention-driven robotic control, allowing real-time translation of neural signals into robotic actions for grasping and placement.

E.term: EEG-Based Hybrid Visual and Motor Imagery Control
E.definition: A framework that integrates EEG signals for real-time control of robotic grasping and placement, translating visual intent and motor imagery into robotic actions.
An extension of effective toposes that incorporates effectful computation, providing a framework to study computational processes with associated Lawvere-Tierney topologies.
A congestion-adjusted metric for two-sided platforms that discounts match probabilities based on user attention constraints, enhancing recommendation efficiency.
A measure of the number of independent spatial channels available in a near-field MIMO system, which increases due to the spherical wavefronts present in the near field.
An index introduced for model selection in nested models, providing a measure similar to effective sample size (ESS) to guide decisions on model complexity and variable selection.
Alternative metrics for assessing effective sample size in importance sampling algorithms, linked to Rényi and Tsallis entropy, enhancing the understanding of sample efficiency.
A measure of the efficiency of converting fundamental frequency light into its second harmonic, computable through optimization models with geometric constraints.
A method that utilizes the triangle inequality to optimize suffix comparisons in prescriptive process monitoring, significantly reducing computational costs while ensuring exact retrieval.
A novel framework for single-image super-resolution that optimizes visual quality towards human preferences by avoiding extensive patch sampling and enabling efficient image-level perception.

An end-to-end framework for automated academic poster generation that utilizes semantic-aware retrieval and token-efficient multimodal generation to enhance information density and layout verification.
A web-native neuro-symbolic framework for smart glasses that utilizes a Large Language Model (LLM) to provide assistive AI capabilities, enhancing user interaction with the web in real-time.
An end-to-end framework for accurate egocentric full-body pose estimation in virtual reality, integrating headset motion cues with RGB-D observations for improved tracking performance.
A complete classification of the computational complexity of the planar eight-vertex model, identifying conditions under which the partition function is computable in polynomial time.

EIMC (Efficient Instance-aware Multi-modal Collaborative perception) introduces an early collaborative paradigm for multi-modal data fusion in autonomous driving, enhancing communication efficiency by integrating lightweight collaborative voxels into local modality-fusion steps.

A structured execution graph framework that integrates large language models with dynamic knowledge graphs to enhance decision-making in scientific workflows while maintaining context and auditability.
A simulation framework used to evaluate the benefits of malleable job scheduling in high-performance computing clusters, allowing dynamic resource allocation at runtime.

A framework that formalizes the heuristic elbow method for cluster selection as a rigorous inferential problem, using a normalized discrete curvature statistic to evaluate cluster heterogeneity against a null distribution.

A fast, multiplication-free algorithm for dimension reduction that selects a subset of elements from input data, providing an efficient alternative to traditional methods like PCA, especially in resource-constrained environments.
A novel method for optimizing handover processes in direct-to-device satellite networks by dynamically adjusting the elevation angle threshold to mitigate co-channel interference.
A technology designed to enhance the reliability of direct satellite-to-device communications by integrating supplementary uplink mechanisms to mitigate challenges posed by low elevation angles.
A novel approach that utilizes elicitability to derive path-wise loss functions for training neural networks, enabling efficient approximation of stochastic processes without expensive simulations.

A label-free framework for quality assessment of evolving AI-generated images that constructs positive and negative pairs for evaluating visual quality and prompt-image alignment without human annotations.

ELLMPEG is an edge-based processing tool for large language models that addresses the challenges of cloud deployment by enabling local processing, thereby reducing computational demands and enhancing privacy.

A machine learning-driven framework for automated RF physical synthesis that converts circuit netlists into manufacturable GDSII layouts, addressing limitations in component modeling and routing capabilities.
A novel approach to cross-robot policy learning that incorporates morphological information into transformer-based policies, enhancing robustness and performance across different robotic embodiments.
A benchmark framework for analyzing embodied cooperation among large language model agents in dynamic environments, facilitating the study of collaborative task success.
A discipline-agnostic calculus that treats theories as fixed points of idempotent operators, integrating order-theoretic closure operators with dynamics to analyze composable processes.
A method that combines emission-aware Gaussian representations with path tracing to achieve physically based light transport in indoor scene reconstruction and editing.
A unified framework for Omni-Modal Large Language Models that enhances emotional understanding and expression, addressing limitations in existing models' emotional responses.

A unified framework designed to enhance emotional understanding and expression in Omni-Modal Large Language Models (Omni-LLMs), addressing issues of contextually mismatched emotional responses in complex scenarios.
A hyperbolic hypergraph framework for multimodal emotion and sentiment modeling that utilizes Poincare-ball embeddings and contrastive learning in hyperbolic space to enhance class separation.
A technique for enhancing expressive speech synthesis by merging audios of emotionally congruent text, allowing for the generation of more natural and engaging speech outputs.

A concept examining how the emotional tone expressed by users during interactions with AI, such as ChatGPT, influences the quality of AI responses, demonstrating that positive and negative emotions can enhance AI performance.

A neuro-symbolic dashboard designed to facilitate sustainable and dynamic knowledge exploration, synthesis, and reuse in software and requirements engineering, addressing challenges in literature reviews and data sharing.
A technique used to analyze time series data by decomposing it into intrinsic mode functions, which can then be transformed into graph representations for advanced modeling.

A framework for conditional independence tests that addresses inaccuracies in small samples and model misspecification, providing more reliable frequentist guarantees for causal discovery and feature selection.

EMS-FL is a federated learning approach that employs a mixture-of-experts (MoE) model to optimize performance in satellite-terrestrial networks, addressing challenges of data shortage and computational constraints in edge devices.
A system that automatically generates music synchronized to the emotional and rhythmic content of videos, aiding content creators in enhancing their productions without manual composition.
A model addressing the dynamics of information exchange among robots that can only share information upon meeting, focusing on the design of storage and routing algorithms for such interactions.
A problem concerning the computation of a finite set of solutions for first-order autonomous ordinary differential equations, with a complete validated algorithm presented for its resolution.

A framework that integrates non-sequential Monte Carlo polarization ray tracing with a differentiable transfer-matrix solver for optimizing geometric waveguide displays in augmented reality applications.

A framework for downlink non-orthogonal multiple access that integrates interference-aware and channel-adaptive super-constellations within an autoencoder architecture, directly embedding wireless channel characteristics into training and inference.

A computational technique that accelerates dynamic programming by inverting the Euler equation, specifically adapted for models with Epstein-Zin preferences. This method achieves significant speed and accuracy improvements over traditional value function iteration.
A modeling technique for streaming real-time trajectory prediction that considers the influence of neighboring traffic agents on autonomous vehicle path planning and decision-making.
A method for optimizing data delivery in optical LEO satellite downlinks, balancing delay-sensitive and delay-tolerant service requirements to enhance communication efficiency.
A novel hydrogen electrolyzer design that integrates energy storage to provide grid-forming services, allowing for autonomous real and reactive power support in off-grid renewable systems.
A lightweight, programmable approach to manage CPU resources in softwarized Radio Access Networks (RANs), focusing on energy efficiency while meeting real-time constraints.

Energy-aware Design of Metaheuristics integrates machine learning surrogates into optimization algorithms to address real-world challenges while considering energy consumption, aiming to improve both performance and efficiency.
A framework for continual learning in spiking neural networks that optimizes both accuracy and energy efficiency by integrating experience replay and learnable parameters.
A class of numerical integrators for elastodynamic contact problems that balances energy conservation and stability, addressing limitations of traditional methods in dynamic simulations.
An FPGA architecture for stochastic simulated quantum annealing that utilizes probabilistic bits (p-bits) to optimize energy efficiency while supporting fully connected graphs.
An end-to-end framework for assessing the reliability of deep neural networks on systolic array architectures, designed to facilitate accurate transient fault analysis.

Engineered simultaneity refers to a system design that necessitates the temporal comparison of events occurring at spatially separated locations, exemplified by the National Best Bid and Offer (NBBO) in U.S. equity markets.
A protocol for data dissemination in multi-hop wireless IoT networks that improves goodput by utilizing a distributed delay timer mechanism based on link quality.

The Enhanced Legendre Memory Unit is an advanced architecture for sequence modeling that provides stable performance with fewer recurrent parameters compared to traditional LSTMs, making it suitable for decoding short nonstationary signals.
An improved version of the projection pursuit tree classifier that allows for more flexible splits and class groupings, enhancing performance in complex multi-class classification problems.

A novel wireless architecture that employs intelligent metasurfaces to facilitate surface-wave propagation, significantly enhancing control and reducing attenuation compared to traditional space-wave transmission methods.

A framework that captures the wake-word segment during human-machine interaction to automatically serve as an enrollment reference for target speech extraction, facilitating seamless user experiences.

A theoretical framework for RNA design that bounds the folding probabilities of RNA structures using ensemble-based criteria, allowing for a more interpretable approach to RNA secondary structure design.

A method that combines multiple forecasting models to improve the accuracy of predictions for power quality parameters in transmission systems, particularly useful in managing data heterogeneity and seasonality.
A method in large-scale reinforcement learning that employs multiple policies to enhance exploration but requires careful management of inter-policy diversity to maintain learning efficiency.
A stock trading strategy that combines multiple deep reinforcement learning algorithms, such as Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and Advantage Actor Critic (A2C), to maximize investment returns.
A framework that synthesizes ensemble-based inference using the conditional Gaussian law, providing a finite-dimensional representation for Gaussian processes under conditioning.

Entanglement Learning is an information-theoretic framework that enhances Model Predictive Control (MPC) adaptability by utilizing an Information Digital Twin to monitor and optimize the information flow in real-time.
A method for constructing entanglement-assisted codes from arbitrary quantum codes, enabling efficient communication over erasure channels by utilizing correctable subsets of physical qubits.
A method that establishes a relationship between entanglement entropy and approximation errors in Hamiltonian simulation, improving resource estimation for quantum simulations.
This technique assesses understanding in heterogeneous LLM-based multi-agent systems by measuring entropy, facilitating guided collaboration among agents with varying capabilities.

A technique for neural audio codecs that incorporates an entropy-guided strategy to optimize the grouping of acoustic features, aiming to achieve high-quality speech reconstruction under ultra-low bitrate conditions.

Entropy-Guided Group Residual Vector Quantization (EG-GRVQ) is a technique for ultra-low bitrate neural speech codecs that enhances semantic modeling while ensuring high-fidelity audio reconstruction.

This framework applies entropy-regularization to American options pricing, utilizing a probabilistic approach based on the Doob-Meyer-Mertens decomposition and reflected backward stochastic differential equations to achieve monotone approximations of value functions.
A condition for the convergence of differential entropy under measure convergence of probability density functions, characterized by the uniform integrability of entropy integrands.
A framework that optimizes generalized pinching-antenna systems by considering user mobility and localization errors, enhancing network performance through traffic-aware and geometry-aware metrics.
Large language models that autonomously pursue knowledge goals and shape information environments, raising concerns about reliability and alignment with epistemic norms.
A decision-making framework that estimates issue-specific signal reliability and assigns bounded voting weights, enhancing collective decision-making beyond traditional majority rule.
A method for breaking down mutual information into per-class contributions, allowing for a more nuanced understanding of a model's uncertainty in safety-critical classification tasks.

A benchmark dataset specifically designed for event stream-based visual place recognition, comprising 10K event sequences and 65K event frames to address challenges in low illumination and high-speed motion.
A software-level memory tiering solution designed for hyperscaler workloads using Compute Express Link (CXL), addressing multi-tenancy support and performance variability in memory management.
A physics-based training method for neural networks that leverages equilibrium states to optimize network performance, particularly in realistic architectures with connectivity constraints.

Equilibrium Propagation (EP) is a physics-inspired learning algorithm that extends traditional EP to non-conservative systems, enabling inference and learning in systems with non-reciprocal interactions.
A class of machine learning models that incorporate equivariant inductive bias to achieve high accuracy across diverse material systems, while addressing computational efficiency.

Equivariant machine learning interaction potentials (MLIPs) are models that incorporate equivariant inductive biases to achieve high accuracy in chemical and computational materials science benchmarks while addressing computational cost.

A technique for generating user diagnostics during the verification of probabilistic programs, focusing on slicing for error reporting, proof simplification, and preserving successful verification results.
An enhancement to active automata learning algorithms that leverages knowledge of observable errors to improve the efficiency and scalability of learning behavioral models from system interactions.
A technique introduced to enhance rendering quality in existing 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes by compensating for errors in the rendering process.
A novel framework for designing robust algorithms in insertion-deletion streams, combining multiple linear sketches to achieve sublinear space complexity while maintaining robustness.
A compact audio language model that achieves competitive performance against larger models across various audio understanding benchmarks, utilizing a unified end-to-end architecture.
A dataset comprising 81,000 embedding GeoTIFFs derived from three foundation models, designed to benchmark pooling strategies for geospatial data while preserving class-discriminative signals.

Eval4Sim is an evaluation framework designed for persona simulations in large language models, providing a more grounded assessment of human conversational behavior compared to traditional LLM-as-a-judge approaches.

A strategy that integrates multimodal event knowledge to improve reasoning in Vision-Language Navigation tasks, addressing challenges related to coarse-grained instructions and long-horizon reasoning.
Event-Graph Neural Networks are hardware-aware neural architectures implemented on FPGA for efficient audio classification and keyword spotting, utilizing sparse event data from neuromorphic devices.
A diffusion-based framework for text-to-motion synthesis that defines events as semantically self-contained actions, enabling better alignment between text prompts and motion segments.

An adaptive communication control mechanism for distributed learning that allows nodes to autonomously decide when to exchange model information based on local model deviations, reducing communication overhead.
A design paradigm for real-time systems that focuses on responding to events while maintaining safety and timeliness, particularly in environments that may violate operational assumptions.
EventCast is a modular forecasting framework that integrates future event knowledge into demand forecasting for e-commerce, improving accuracy during high-impact periods.
A KV-cache management system that jointly optimizes eviction and compression decisions across all KV caches to minimize average generation latency in Large Language Model (LLM) inference systems.

EvoCorps is an evolutionary multi-agent framework designed to proactively depolarize online discourse by framing discourse governance as a dynamic social game, coordinating roles for monitoring and planning.

EoB is an automated benchmark designer for Black-Box Optimization that utilizes large language models to enhance diversity and objectivity in benchmark design, addressing biases inherent in human-crafted benchmarks.
The Evolution of Test Functions (EoTF) framework automatically generates diverse continuous optimization test functions to improve the evaluation of black-box optimization algorithms.
A framework that enhances language models' metacognitive abilities by aligning internal knowledge with explicit behaviors through a dual-prompt method.

ECS is an evolutionary method that optimizes context combinations for large language models, improving knowledge acquisition without the need for retraining by leveraging accuracy on a small development set.

E.term: Evolutionary eXploration of Augmenting Quantum Circuits (EXAQC)
E.definition: An evolutionary approach for the automated design and training of quantum circuits, addressing the challenges of scalability and adaptability in quantum computing.

E.term: Evolutionary Game Theory in Agent-Based Modeling
E.definition: A framework used to model the decision-making behavior of household agents during self-evacuation in disaster scenarios, focusing on competition for limited resources.

An automated framework that utilizes black-box optimization to map computational graphs to spatial accelerators, enhancing the deployment of inference workloads with low latency and energy consumption.
A framework that applies multiple evolution strategies for LLM-based Verilog generation and optimization, demonstrating superior functional correctness through Monte Carlo Tree Search.

A framework for generating high-quality benchmarks for hardware model checking using reinforcement learning, addressing the benchmark gap in existing suites and facilitating rigorous evaluation of verification techniques.

EvoSkill is a self-evolving framework designed for automated skill discovery in multi-agent systems, enabling agents to autonomously develop reusable workflows and domain-specific capabilities without manual intervention.
A meta-evolution framework that combines LLM-driven optimization with evolutionary search, allowing for the reuse of previously evaluated solutions to guide the discovery of new candidate solutions.
A theoretical result demonstrating the ability to control two-dimensional hydroelastic wave systems precisely over short time intervals, given specific initial and final conditions.
An algorithm for mixed integer programming that generates strong valid inequalities for the binary knapsack set, optimizing storage and computation through a dominance list structure.
A deep learning framework designed for pricing American options by solving the Black-Scholes-Merton equations, ensuring compliance with terminal conditions and handling non-smooth price behaviors.
A Gaussian process framework designed for causal inference in panel data, allowing for flexible nonlinear trends and ensuring exchangeability across treated and control units.
Execution flow, represented as I = dV/dt, is identified as a fundamental driver of market dynamics, calculated using a numerical framework based on the Radon-Nikodym derivative.
A Transformer-based architecture designed for forecasting daily exchange rate returns, featuring a multi-scale trend-aware self-attention mechanism to capture local and long-range dependencies.

A simulation framework designed to optimize exoskeleton controllers by combining neuromechanical simulation with human-aligned exploration, addressing the challenges of human adaptation to external forces.

The expectation-realisation gap quantifies the discrepancies between anticipated productivity gains from AI tools and actual outcomes, highlighting the calibration errors experienced by developers and organizations.
A metric that quantifies the expected improvement of a solution relative to the energy consumed, guiding the adaptive selection of operators in energy-aware metaheuristics.

A specialized guardrail model for large language models that ensures adherence to safety policies by moderating inputs and outputs in domain-specific contexts, particularly those rich in technical jargon.
A framework for evaluating explainability solutions across different domains, demonstrating its applicability through case studies in employee attrition prediction and urban resource allocation.
An empirical evaluation approach that assesses the reliability, computational feasibility, and interpretability of transfer learning models for IoT DDoS detection under resource constraints.
A methodology for analyzing malware through memory analysis that captures volatile system state to recover transient artifacts, enhancing interpretability of automated malware detection models.
Explanation-Guided Learning is a method that incorporates human-provided explanations into the training of deep neural networks to enhance interpretability and robustness against adversarial inputs.

A framework for evaluating large language models in finance that identifies and addresses specific biases such as look-ahead and survivorship bias, ensuring more reliable performance metrics and deployment claims.

E.term: Exploratory Landscape Features for Multi-Objective Optimization (MO-ELA)
E.definition: A meta-algorithmic approach that rigorously expands exploratory landscape features to enhance automated algorithm selection in continuous multi-objective optimization problems.
A mechanism for policy search in reinforcement learning that incorporates user-specified Gaussian perturbations to baseline controls, facilitating exploration in risk-sensitive portfolio management.

E.term: Exponential Convergence in Stochastic Gradient Descent
E.definition: A phenomenon where stochastic gradient descent achieves faster convergence rates by leveraging instability near the edge of stability in optimization trajectories.
A class of string-to-string functions characterized by exponential growth, extending the concept of polyregular functions, and exhibiting unique closure properties.
A method for generating cinematic portrait videos that maintains subject identity and expressions using personalized representations and improved disentanglement capabilities.
A mathematical property used in the analysis of nonconvex-nonconcave stochastic minimax optimization problems, enabling convergence without strong concavity assumptions.
Research methodologies that involve testing XR technologies in real-world settings, highlighting safety risks and the need for standardized safety protocols.
A domain-specific language used for generating executable simulation scenarios from multimodal crash report data, enhancing the safety validation process for autonomous driving systems.
A novel approach to integrate logic programming into typed functional programming languages, maintaining static typing while leveraging polymorphism and higher-order functions.
A serverless computing model where data movement over the network is managed by the platform, allowing applications to specify data requirements at each computation stage.

A computational problem that involves determining the relationship between two Boolean functions with minimal oracle queries, showcasing a significant difference in query complexity between quantum and classical approaches.

E.term: Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) for UIT Detection
E.definition: A machine learning technique that employs XGBoost to detect and identify unlawful insider trading transactions by analyzing complex patterns in large-scale financial data.

A novel algorithm designed for classifying data into multiple labels simultaneously, particularly effective in analyzing job vacancies. It utilizes contrastive learning and LLM-driven data augmentation to enhance classification accuracy.
ELAS is a method that utilizes very large effective array apertures in a monostatic wideband system for integrated sensing and communication, enabling operation in both near and far-field regimes.
XL-RIS refers to a large-scale intelligent surface that enhances communication and sensing capabilities in near-field systems, enabling efficient data transmission and target detection.
A phenomenon in cryptocurrency markets where extreme sentiment (fear or greed) predicts higher spreads, indicating excess uncertainty beyond realized volatility.
A novel method for discretizing tangent vector fields on triangle meshes, enabling the definition of continuous tangent vector fields and covariant derivatives for advanced mesh processing.

A lightweight attention-augmentation module that integrates human eye-gaze patterns into LLM-based code summarization, enhancing the model's ability to generate natural language descriptions of source code by modeling human attention during code reading.

H.definition: The Hardware-Aware Stochastic Simulated Annealing (HA-SSA) algorithm optimizes memory usage for FPGA implementations of simulated annealing, maintaining computational speed while reducing memory overhead.

H.term: HADUA (Hierarchical Attention and Dynamic Uniform Alignment)
H.definition: An adaptive learning framework for robust cross-subject emotion recognition that models intra-modal temporal dynamics and inter-modal semantic interactions.

A modification of the 1 Euro Filter designed for real-time animation blending, which includes algorithms for automatic data-driven tuning and filter triggering based on motion derivative boundary checks.

A fine-grained resource sharing quantum operating system that addresses the challenges of multiplexing quantum processors among multiple users, aiming to enhance resource utilization and reduce job wait times.

An innovative architecture for large language models that utilizes rational arithmetic to improve numerical stability and inference accuracy, moving away from traditional fuzzy floating-point methods.
A novel architecture that represents relations like part-whole intrinsically within its components, utilizing a graph-Hamiltonian operator to calculate energies among encoded data.

A framework that utilizes multimodal large language models for intention-driven 3D affordance grounding, aggregating interaction intentions into contact-aware embeddings for improved object interaction understanding.

A metric introduced to quantify the proximity of learners' actions to valid grammatical constructions during interactive gameplay, providing insights into cognitive strategies in language acquisition.

A one-player game involving colored blocks and flowers, where the objective is to make blocks contact with flowers of the corresponding color. The solvability of its levels is PSPACE-complete under certain restrictions.
Innovative use of weight and pressure as haptic feedback mechanisms in virtual reality user interfaces, enhancing user experience by conveying information through tactile sensations.

A technique for generating uniformly random polynomials in Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) schemes, aimed at reducing communication overhead by deriving random values from a small seed on the client side.

Harmonia is an algorithm-hardware co-design framework that enables the use of block floating point (BFP) activations across all layers of large language models, enhancing memory and compute efficiency.

A deep learning architecture designed to compute the Harmonic Beltrami Signature from binary-like images, providing a shape representation invariant to transformations such as translation and rotation.
A systematic approach for synthesizing topology reconfiguration schedules in chip-to-chip silicon photonics to minimize collective completion time during communication.

HateMirage is a novel dataset designed to decode faux hate and subtle online abuse, focusing on the nuanced ways misinformation can incite or normalize hate, thus advancing reasoning and explainability in online safety research.
A high-bandwidth flash-augmented vector engine aimed at accelerating large-scale Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search (ANNS) for retrieval-augmented generation tasks.
A storage management system that integrates confidential computing principles with disaggregated storage to enhance performance and security in modern data centers.

A method for generating high compatibility virtual reality environments that enhance redirected walking techniques, addressing challenges posed by geometric divergence between physical and virtual spaces.
HDINO is an efficient open-vocabulary object detector that employs a two-stage training strategy to reduce reliance on curated datasets and enhance detection capabilities using noisy samples.

A user-centric health data management system that empowers individuals to control, upload, query, and share their health data across various modalities, addressing fragmentation in healthcare ecosystems.
A statistical finding that correlates high temperatures with an increase in school shootings, suggesting a link between heat exposure and aggressive behavior.
A heatmap-guided decoding framework for large-scale Travelling Salesman Problems that treats heatmaps as soft edge priors, enabling probabilistic tour construction under feasibility constraints.
A simulation framework designed to analyze the impact of urban materials on the Urban Heat Island effect, providing high-resolution city-scale simulations to study temperature variations.
A classification framework that utilizes Heaviside loss and low-rank constraints to robustly handle matrix-structured observations, improving noise sensitivity compared to traditional methods.

H.term: Height Profile of Analog Error-Correcting Codes
H.definition: A concept that models the reliability of analog vector-matrix multipliers as a coding problem, determining the trade-off between error tolerance and the number of detectable outlying errors based on the height profile of the code.

This model incorporates vertical variations in signal behavior for spectrum sharing in aerial communications, validated through extensive measurements across various environments using a helikite-mounted software-defined radio.
Architected soft materials designed for soft continuum robots, providing tunable stiffness and strength while enabling the embedding of sensors for enhanced control and sensing capabilities.
A framework that harmonizes early and late fusion techniques along with large language model reasoning for improved multi-granular table-text retrieval in open-domain question answering.

An end-to-end audio language model designed for realistic social interactions, leveraging a massive dataset of real-life conversations and a modality-interleaved training strategy to enhance emotional resonance in speech.
A 72-parameter analytical color space designed for UI systems that maps CIE XYZ to a perceptually-organized Lab representation, enhancing color distance metrics for design applications.
A foundational logic for labelled transition systems that provides a framework for reasoning about system behaviors and equivalences in formal verification.

A formalization of Hennessy-Milner Logic for labelled transition systems, providing a syntax, satisfaction relation, and metatheory that emphasizes generality and reusability within the Lean Computer Science Library.
A Meta-RL approach that leverages task symmetries to enhance generalization by transforming states and actions through actions of a Lie group, enabling richer task space exploration.
A unified high-performance architecture for Number Theoretic Transform (NTT) that supports Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption (HHE) by exploiting both temporal and spatial parallelism.

HeRo is a heterogeneous-aware framework for adaptive orchestration of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) on mobile System-on-Chips (SoCs), optimizing low-latency execution across diverse hardware accelerators.
A hyperthread-level scheduling technique that addresses performance interference in microservices by modeling SMT contention dynamics for optimal resource allocation.
An AI/ML-based beam selection algorithm designed to function effectively across heterogeneous antenna hardware configurations, minimizing beam training overhead while maintaining performance.

A methodology for selecting clients in federated learning that accounts for statistical heterogeneity across clients, aiming to improve the accuracy of the global machine learning model without compromising local data privacy.
A learning paradigm that facilitates collaborative optimization among heterogeneous agents by sharing verified rollouts during training, allowing independent execution during inference.

A retraining-free framework designed for Sparse Mixture-of-Experts models that utilizes analog in-memory computing to improve memory and energy efficiency during inference without requiring noise-aware retraining.

A model for recovering hyperedges from projected graphs, focusing on the reconstruction of original hyperedges based on observed projections. The approach utilizes maximal cliques to achieve recovery under specific conditions.
A deep learning-based antenna coding optimization algorithm that trains multiple neural networks on various coding schemes and selects the optimal one for enhanced wireless communication performance.
An algorithmic strategy that routes simpler queries to lower-cost models and complex queries to higher-cost models, enhancing the cost-efficiency of large language model deployments.
This model describes a random hypergraph structure where hyperedges can vary in degree, facilitating the recovery of hyperedges from observed projections of the graph.
A framework developed under the Hexa-X-II project aimed at addressing security, privacy, and resilience in the sixth generation (6G) mobile networks, focusing on end-to-end system controls.
An open-source compilation stack targeting Qualcomm's Neural Processing Units (NPUs), built on the MLIR framework, which optimizes AI workloads through structured compilation passes.
A dataset comprising subject-rated 1080p videos encoded at various bitrates to study the perceptual effects of frame rate and compression strength in bitrate-constrained live streaming.

A modular, reconfigurable neuromorphic computing platform designed for executing large spiking neural networks with high efficiency, utilizing hierarchical address-event routing for optimized spike processing.
A simultaneous speech translation model that eliminates the need for word-level alignments, simplifying the training process and enabling scalability across diverse languages.
A hierarchical collaboration framework for multi-agent systems that optimizes intra-node LLM mixtures and inter-node communication topology to enhance agent performance.

An approach to classification that leverages hierarchical structures in data, particularly for tasks like vehicle make and model classification, allowing models to exploit semantic relationships through multi-task learning.
A decoding framework that assesses EEG signals to capture object representations across hierarchical abstraction levels, addressing challenges in recognizing fine-grained representations in EEG data.
A method for urban geospatial modification that utilizes agentic systems to iteratively adjust existing urban plans based on dependency awareness, facilitating efficient urban renewal.

H.term: Hierarchical Diffusion Models for Micro-Video Recommendation
H.definition: A modeling approach that utilizes hierarchical diffusion techniques to capture user preferences in micro-video recommendation systems, addressing challenges posed by noise and implicit feedback.
A framework for characterizing defect sensitivity in Binary Search ADCs, particularly in thin-film technologies, to improve fault tolerance in flexible electronics.
A methodological approach that models decision-making in repeated public goods games as discrete switches, addressing the volatility in contributions through behavioral trajectory clustering.

A novel architecture for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning that employs a sequential training scheme to learn from multiple perspectives across different hierarchy levels, integrating high-level objectives with low-level execution.

A multi-agent framework that utilizes large language models to decompose natural-language instructions into executable actions for heterogeneous robot teams, featuring prompt optimization for improved task planning.

HRA is a multimodal universal attack framework for vision-language models that optimizes the perturbation learning process by leveraging a temporal hierarchy of gradients, reducing computational overhead in adversarial attacks.

A differentiable architecture that selects spectral coefficients from a Boolean Fourier basis and composes them using Sinkhorn-constrained routing, aimed at improving the learning of precise Boolean logic.
This approach utilizes multiple pre-trained visual encoders to decode visual representations from brain signals, enhancing the understanding of human visual processing.
An evolutionary search approach that utilizes biological knowledge to optimize whole-brain models, improving predictive power and reducing overfitting in biophysical brain modeling.
A hierarchical layer attention framework for audio deepfake detection that utilizes margin-based contrastive learning to model dependencies across temporal frames and layers.

A hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for soccer robot control that integrates dual-frequency policies for long-term tactical planning and short-term motion execution, utilizing real-time vision detection.
A reference-based inpainting method aimed at generating high-fidelity human-product images, addressing challenges in detail preservation and training data diversity.
A method for generating high-fidelity 3D meshes using compact autoregressive dependencies, significantly improving the maximum generatable sequence length for detailed structural representation.
A block floating-point data format designed for deep learning that packs 64 4-bit elements with shared scaling metadata, enhancing representational efficiency and reducing hardware requirements.
Non-terrestrial networks that provide broad coverage and serve as a vital link between satellite systems and terrestrial networks, with applications in sensing, computing, and communication.
A methodological approach that uncovers complex networks of influence among entities directly from data, facilitating the analysis of spillover effects in corporate emissions.

H.term: High-Order Markov Chain Task for Transformers
H.definition: A task designed to study how transformers learn to integrate information from multiple past positions, revealing dynamics of competitive and cooperative attention patterns.

A set of bounds for universal prediction of stochastic sequences that guarantees vanishing regret with high probability, enhancing the understanding of prediction performance over finite time horizons.

This framework extends the bialgebraic abstract GSOS approach to higher-order languages, preserving compositionality of bisimilarity and offering a structured method for modeling operational and denotational semantics.

Higher-Order Circuit Theories define laws for strict higher-order circuit diagrams, incorporating concepts such as nesting, temporal and spatial composition, and equivalence between lower-order bipartite processes and higher-order bipartite states.
HOMs refer to the underlying dynamical rules that govern collective behaviors in complex systems characterized by higher-order interactions, identified through signatures of higher-order behaviors.

A weighted graph representation of the Hilbert space of a Heisenberg antiferromagnet, where vertices correspond to spin configurations and edges represent off-diagonal spin-flip terms of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian, weighted by wavefunction amplitudes.
An approach that integrates Hilbert space-filling priors into decentralized multi-robot learning, enhancing exploration efficiency and coverage in sparse-reward environments.

The problem of determining the solvability of multivariate polynomial equations is shown to lie within the counting hierarchy, allowing for polynomial-time computation of the number of solutions with oracle access.
A method for efficient long-context inference of Large Language Models (LLMs) on edge devices, utilizing hierarchical key-value eviction strategies to manage memory constraints effectively.

HiLoRA is a hierarchical low-rank adaptation framework for personalized federated learning, which introduces adapters at multiple levels to enhance shared representation learning and improve adaptation to unseen clients.
A technique in the VeriFast verification tool that allows for pragmatic certification of verification results by mirroring user-annotated properties to enhance correctness assurance.
A conjecture that generalizes the original matrix case to tensors, proposing new insights into the complexity of matrix and tensor operations.

A CLEF evaluation lab focused on person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical texts, targeting the classification of relations such as 'at' and 'isAt' based on temporal and geographical cues.

Hippasus is an automatic feature augmentation technique that enhances machine learning tasks on relational data by discovering and integrating useful features from related tables through efficient join operations.

A high-performance solver for trajectory optimization that employs an interior-point method with an adaptive barrier update strategy, capable of handling both inequality and hard equality constraints efficiently.
A ray tracing framework that utilizes the hardware ray tracing capabilities of AMD GPUs to accelerate rendering computations, enhancing performance in graphics applications.
A framework for automated C-to-Rust migration that combines a build-aware skeleton with historical retrieval to address dependency issues and improve translation accuracy.

Hierarchical Sparse Activation Compression is a framework designed for ultra-long sequence modeling in recommender systems, optimizing user behavior summarization and reducing latency and memory usage.

A dynamic algorithm that supports insertions and deletions in a balls-and-bins model while maintaining a maximum load of m/n + O(1) with high probability, achieving efficient recourse independent of the history of operations.
A newly recorded dataset for cardiorespiratory sounds, utilized for separation, clustering, and anomaly detection in AI-driven signal processing.
Hierarchical Multi-Agent System for Payments, a framework that automates end-to-end payment workflows using large language models, addressing challenges in agentic payment tasks.

Holographic-Pattern Division Multiple Access (HDMA) is a technique for multi-user communication that utilizes holographic surfaces to manage beamforming and user access, optimizing data transmission in virtual reality applications.

HomeAdam is a variant of the Adam and AdamW optimizers that aims to improve generalization by leveraging algorithmic stability, addressing the limitations of traditional adaptive algorithms in deep learning.
An optimization algorithm that enhances the generalization of Adam and AdamW by incorporating algorithmic stability, aiming to improve convergence rates in deep learning.

HoMMI (Whole-Body Mobile Manipulation Interface) is a data collection and policy learning framework that captures human demonstrations using egocentric sensing, enabling scalable mobile manipulation learning while addressing the human-to-robot embodiment gap.
An algorithmic challenge that involves reconstructing a graph based on homomorphism counts from given graphs, with complexity varying based on the representation of counts.

The hooking period analysis framework focuses on evaluating the initial three seconds of video ads, leveraging a multimodal approach to understand the interplay of visual, auditory, and textual elements that capture viewer attention.

HookLens is an interactive visual analytics system that assists developers in understanding the dependencies and data flows defined by React Hooks, enhancing maintainability and reducing complexity in React applications.
A benchmark designed to evaluate agentic search systems by balancing quality and efficiency, focusing on real-world user queries with underspecified preferences.
A telemetry system that monitors memory access patterns at runtime to optimize data placement and migration in memory tiering solutions, enhancing performance without interfering with workloads.
High-order wavefront sensing and control algorithms designed for maintaining wavefront stability in astronomical observatories, addressing computational demands with advanced hardware solutions.

The Hierarchical Sequential Transduction Unit, a generative recommender architecture that captures the temporal hierarchy of user interactions, improving the modeling of user behavior in recommendation systems.

An open-source security scanner designed to detect hubness poisoning in Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems, which can exploit frequent retrieval items to introduce harmful content and degrade system performance.
The huff Python package provides a comprehensive workflow for market area analysis, including data import, interaction matrix construction, parameter estimation, and visualization of market dynamics.
A study assessing the reasoning capabilities of large language models on digital logic circuit problems, evaluating their effectiveness as educational tools for undergraduate students.

A probabilistic model that assigns a likelihood to each unit being human in a population of interacting humans and AI agents, allowing for the estimation of average human composition in subpopulations.
HBI refers to the interaction between humans and architectural spaces, enhanced by advancements in robotics and sensing technologies that allow for adaptive and responsive environments.
An approach to human-AI collaboration that balances AI complementarity and trust-building, aiming to enhance decision-making without eroding human trust.
An approach that integrates physiological data (like eye-tracking and EEG) with content analysis to enhance the detection of nuanced social phenomena, such as sexism in memes.

Human-Certified Module Repositories (HCMRs) are architectural models aimed at constructing trustworthy software by ensuring the reliability of AI-generated components through rigorous provenance and review processes.
A human-inspired framework that models phonetic perception through adaptive inference, achieving state-of-the-art results on phonetic benchmarks with minimal training data.

HVP-Net is a framework for video-text retrieval that improves matching accuracy by extracting and refining features from multiple intermediate layers of a vision encoder, rather than relying solely on final-layer features.

A trading strategy that integrates technical analysis, machine learning, and financial sentiment to adapt to market regimes, utilizing methods like trend-following, mean-reversion, and sentiment analysis.

H.term: Hybrid Approach for Data Breach Reporting
H.definition: A proposed methodology to streamline data breach notifications under GDPR by automating the translation of forensic artifacts into structured reports, reducing cognitive burden on analysts.

H.term: Hybrid Architecture for Options Wheel Strategy
H.definition: A model-first hybrid architecture that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with Bayesian Networks to enhance decision-making in financial trading, particularly for the options wheel strategy.
An advanced deep learning architecture designed for edge-preserving denoising in cone-beam computed tomography, effectively reducing noise while maintaining fine anatomical details.

H.term: Hybrid Consensus with Quantum Sybil Resistance
H.definition: This consensus protocol integrates classical and quantum techniques to achieve Sybil resistance by leveraging the uncloneability of quantum states as a scarce resource, enhancing the security of decentralized systems.
A learning-based approach for matching systems that adaptively combines immediate and delayed matching strategies to improve market efficiency and handle user departure dynamics.

This framework automates the extraction of personal names from broadcast news videos, leveraging a curated corpus of annotated frames to address variability in graphical layouts and typographic conventions.
An approach for optimizing the delivery of panoramic scenes in immersive applications by utilizing two types of feedback signals to enhance user experience while adhering to bandwidth constraints.
An innovative rendering method that combines volumetric path tracing with Gaussian splatting to enhance interactive and immersive anatomical visualizations in medical imaging.
HyFi is a framework that combines decentralized finance (DeFi) with institutional backing to enhance market stability, transparency, and governance in cryptocurrency markets.

H.term: Hybrid Genetic Algorithm with Graph Neural Networks
H.definition: An optimization approach that combines a multi-modal Genetic Algorithm with a Graph Neural Network to enhance timetabling solutions by integrating domain knowledge into the search process.
A two-step procedure that combines the HAR (Heterogeneous Autoregressive) model with ElasticNet regularization to analyze volatility spillovers across multiple financial markets.

A framework that integrates insights from fine-tuned time-series large language models (TSLMs) into general reasoning large language models (GRLMs) to enhance diagnostic reasoning for time-series data.
A theoretical framework that characterizes the expressive power of various attention mechanisms, including linear and hybrid attention, in relation to standard full attention in transformers.

H.term: Hybrid LLM and Higher-Order Quantum Approximate Optimization (HO-QAOA)
H.definition: A hybrid pipeline for collateral optimization under ISDA Credit Support Annexes, integrating a language model for term extraction and a quantum-inspired optimization method for complex search spaces.
A dialogue system that combines the responsiveness of large language models with a theory-aligned, rule-based framework to support learner reflections in educational contexts.
A systematic trading framework that combines neural networks with tree-based voting models to forecast market risk and generate alpha, utilizing a diverse feature set across multiple asset classes.

H.term: Hybrid Model for Misophonia Trigger Sound Detection
H.definition: A sound event detection model that combines a frozen pre-trained CNN with a time-series module to identify trigger sounds in synthetic soundscapes, aimed at assistive technologies for misophonia.
An automatic glossing pipeline that combines neural sequence labeling with large language model post-correction, aimed at improving interlinear glossed text creation for low-resource languages.
